name: Cascading Vacancy Allocation Theory
core_claim: In systems where agents occupy discrete, size-ranked positions and cannot share positions, a single new resource at the top generates a deterministic reallocation cascade whose total welfare gain far exceeds the value of the initial resource alone. System-wide mobility is controlled not by individual ambition but by the rate of top-tier vacancy creation.

name: Metallurgical Paradigm Shifts
core_claim: The history of metallurgy follows a Kuhnian structure: long periods of normal metallurgy (incremental alloy refinement within an established paradigm of microstructural control) punctuated by revolutionary paradigm replacements. The bronze-to-iron transition, the Bessemer process, and the emergence of high-entropy alloys each represent crises where accumulated anomalies — alloys that outperformed theoretical predictions or failed inexplicably — forced a reconceptualization of what governs material properties. The current multi-principal-element alloy revolution is a paradigm shift in real time, replacing the base-metal-plus-dopants framework with a configurational entropy paradigm.

name: Memetic Formulaics
core_claim: Internet memes propagate via oral-formulaic composition — viral content succeeds not through novelty but through recombination of prefabricated structural formulas (templates, formats, cadences) that reduce cognitive load on both producer and consumer, exactly as Homeric epithets and ring composition enabled oral epic transmission.

name: Judgment Sensitivity Theory
core_claim: Human judgments decompose into discriminability (d') and criterion bias, and aggregation protocols improve accuracy primarily by increasing collective d-prime rather than canceling bias — structured protocols narrow the noise distribution itself, while averaging exploits independent noise.

mapped_components.unit: Single judge's evaluation as one SDT trial where signal is true case merit
mapped_components.variation: Noise-only vs signal+noise distributions per judge, widened by between-judge and occasion noise
mapped_components.selection: Aggregation rules as criterion-placement strategies; structured protocols narrow distributions
mapped_components.fitness: Collective d-prime measuring discrimination independent of directional bias
mapped_components.boundary: Gaussian noise, stationarity; violated by low expertise, extreme cases, or correlated judges

falsifiable_predictions: Structured protocols improve accuracy even when aggregate bias is already near zero, via d-prime increase visible in ROC analysis.
Adding independent judges follows sqrt(n) d-prime law only with uncorrelated noise; shared training shows diminishing gains.
Occasion noise and between-judge noise make separable contributions, targetable by different interventions.
Higher-d-prime judges benefit less from aggregation, producing an expertise × group-size interaction.

name: Allostatic Load Theory of Software Degradation
core_claim: Software systems accumulate 'allostatic load' through repeated adaptive responses to stressors (deadline patches, scope changes, dependency churn). When cumulative wear exceeds repair capacity, the system enters allostatic overload — a phase transition where failures cascade and incremental fixes accelerate degradation.

mapped_components.unit: Software system (codebase + infra + team knowledge) maintaining operational homeostasis
mapped_components.variation: Hotfixes, workarounds, expedient coupling, deferred migrations — individually rational, cumulatively corrosive
mapped_components.selection: Overload threshold where architectural distortion exceeds team's comprehension/repair capacity
mapped_components.fitness: Ratio of intended-change effort to total-change effort (including regression hunting, side effects)
mapped_components.boundary: Team resilience (institutional knowledge, test coverage, refactoring culture) and recovery opportunity

falsifiable_predictions: Systems without maintenance cycles show superlinear (not linear) defect density growth — matching biological allostatic overload.
Stressor-to-recovery ratio predicts degradation better than absolute stressor volume.
Stress inoculation (controlled chaos engineering during calm) builds measurably higher crisis resilience.
Post-overload subsystems accumulate new load faster than never-overloaded equivalents (scarring effect).

name: Attentional Divergence Theory
core_claim: Any adversarial system operates by engineering a growing gap between the target's mental model and actual state, using calibrated attention sinks to consume cognitive resources on visible decoys while the operative payload executes below the monitoring threshold. The attack succeeds when the target's model-of-reality and reality itself diverge past a critical threshold.

name: Crystallization-Preservation Theory
core_claim: All complex adaptive systems containing a fluid substrate and a structurable solute undergo spontaneous crystallization — the irreversible-seeming but reversible transition from fluid dynamism to rigid structure — as a natural preservation mechanism. The rate is governed by a universal trio: saturation ratio, ambient energy, and nucleation seeds. Reversal requires controlled reintroduction of energy without destroying the preserved content.

name: Vitreous Development Theory
core_claim: All creative output exists on a cooling-rate spectrum between amorphous glass (maximum sharpness, minimum durability) and crystalline rock (maximum durability, minimum edge). The optimal strategy is to anneal: ship glassy prototypes to find the sharpest edge, then selectively reheat and slow-cool only the fragments that cut deepest.

name: Interface Matrix Theory
core_claim: Productive complexity emerges at boundaries between two distinct systems when structural elements extend into the transitional zone and form a three-dimensional network performing three universal functions: accretion (trapping mobile resources), incubation (sheltering vulnerable new entities), and dampening (absorbing systemic shocks from either side).

name: Bio-Social Oscillator Unification
core_claim: Firefly synchronization and crowd synchrony are instances of a single universal coupling mechanism: phase-locked oscillators with threshold-triggered pulse coupling. Only three parameters govern emergence — coupling strength, refractory period, and neighbor detection radius. Any population of discrete oscillators meeting these constraints will synchronize on a timescale proportional to log(N).

name: Nutrient-Desert Predation Architecture
core_claim: In any resource-scarce ecosystem, organisms and institutions converge on a universal predation architecture: attract via mimicked abundance, capture via irreversible commitment mechanism, and extract through slow digestion. The two-trigger verification system is an energy-economics optimization distinguishing environments where predation is viable from those where passive gathering suffices.

name: Stigmergic Supply Networks
core_claim: Supply chains can be restructured as decentralized, pheromone-like signal networks where each transaction deposits a 'cost pheromone' on the route it traverses. Routes with lower total cost accumulate stronger signals through faster capital turnover, attracting more traffic, while market volatility acts as evaporation to prevent lock-in. No central planner is needed; the network self-optimizes toward cost-minimal, resilient multi-source routing.

name: Symbiotic Resilience Theory
core_claim: Complex adaptive systems recovering from acute stress follow a universal bifurcation pattern: recovery quality depends not on returning to the prior state but on whether the system can recruit new mutualistic components before opportunistic dominants lock in a degraded regime. The recovery window is temporally narrow and environmentally gated, meaning the conditions surrounding recovery matter more than the severity of the initial shock.

name: Frustule Ossification Theory
core_claim: All protective structural frameworks exhibit monotonic constraint accumulation: each iterative reproduction inherits the tighter of its parent dimensions, progressively narrowing the permissible action space until a costly 'auxospore event' becomes existentially necessary. The geometry of the protective structure acts as a selective filter that determines not just what threats are excluded but what innovation can permeate.

name: Cognitive Brine Pool Theory
core_claim: Organizational knowledge silos behave as cognitive brine pools: specialized expertise accumulates in departmental depressions until it reaches a critical density that resists dilution into the broader organization. Innovation emerges not within the silo nor in the general organization, but at the chemocline-like interface where specialists and generalists exchange partial understanding, producing hybrid insights neither group could generate alone.

name: Quench Fracture Theory of Resilience
core_claim: Any system that gains strength through rapid surface stabilization over an unresolved interior operates in a 'quench fracture' regime: it trades gradual failure modes for catastrophic ones. The strength-to-brittleness ratio is fixed by the cooling rate; faster hardening produces higher apparent strength but guarantees total fragmentation at the failure threshold. True toughness requires annealing — slow, controlled re-heating that allows interior tension to equilibrate with the surface.

name: Dormant Capital Germination Theory
core_claim: All resilient systems maintain an invisible reservoir of latent capacities whose diversity necessarily exceeds the active system's visible expression. Recovery from disruption is governed not by new acquisition but by differential reactivation sequences, where generalist elements activate first to reshape conditions that then trigger specialist emergence. System resilience is a function of dormant bank diversity and disturbance-to-activation signal matching.

name: Controlled Discharge Architecture
core_claim: Any system under accumulating tension will discharge inevitably; the only design variable is whether that discharge follows a pre-engineered low-resistance path or fractures through the structure unpredictably. For any domain where pressure builds continuously and cannot be prevented, the optimal intervention is not suppression but the installation of a preferential discharge point that converts destructive release into structured signal.

name: Adaptive Precedent Theory
core_claim: All complex adaptive systems that must respond to recurring challenges develop a dual-speed architecture: slow deliberative first-response followed by rapid pattern-matched recall, where the initial encounter permanently biases the recognition template. This creates a fundamental tradeoff between response speed and accuracy-to-novelty, as the system's 'memory' increasingly diverges from the evolving challenge landscape through 'founding fixation.'

name: Herd Immunity as Nash Equilibrium Collapse
core_claim: The herd immunity threshold can be reframed as a Nash equilibrium problem: each individual's vaccination decision is a strategy in a population game where payoffs depend on others' choices. Below the critical immunization fraction, the 'free-ride on herd immunity' strategy is not yet available, so vaccination dominates. Above it, free-riding becomes rational, creating a game-theoretic trap where the population oscillates around the threshold. Applying Nash equilibrium analysis with heterogeneous risk preferences predicts the precise vaccination coverage shortfall better than classical SIR models that assume homogeneous rational agents.

name: Textile Phase Transition Dynamics
core_claim: Woven fabrics undergo a phase-transition-like behavior at critical thread densities: below a threshold cover factor, the fabric behaves as a loose collection of independent threads with negligible emergent properties; above it, inter-thread friction and geometric locking produce a sudden onset of collective mechanical behavior — shear resistance, drape, and dimensional stability. This transition is governed by the same mathematical structure as percolation thresholds in statistical physics, and predicting it requires knowing the coupling strength (friction coefficient) and the lattice geometry (weave pattern) rather than the properties of individual threads.

name: Edaphic Trophic Cascade
core_claim: Introducing or removing top predators in the soil food web (predatory nematodes, microarthropods) triggers cascading effects on soil structure itself — not just on organism populations. Predator removal causes bacterial or fungal blooms that alter aggregate stability, pore architecture, and water retention, propagating effects downward through trophic levels to reshape the physical soil matrix. Soil management should therefore treat predator guilds as keystone regulators of soil structure, not merely of nutrient cycling.

name: Red Queen Jurisprudence
core_claim: Legal systems and the actors they regulate are locked in a co-evolutionary arms race: new regulations select for novel compliance-evasion strategies, which in turn select for more sophisticated regulatory language. Like host-parasite genotype cycling, the most common regulatory framework is the one most heavily exploited, creating frequency-dependent selection where legal innovation must run continuously just to maintain the same level of enforcement effectiveness. Regulatory stasis is not stability — it is the moment before the parasitic strategy overtakes the host.

name: Bayesian Soil Contamination Inference
core_claim: Soil remediation decision-making can be formalized as Bayesian updating over a hypothesis space of contamination plume geometries. Each new borehole sample serves as evidence that updates the posterior probability distribution over plume extent, concentration gradients, and migration direction. The optimal sampling strategy is the one that maximizes expected information gain per borehole dollar, and remediation should commence not when contamination is confirmed but when the posterior probability of exceeding regulatory thresholds crosses a decision-theoretic cost boundary.

name: Seigniorage Extraction Theory of Platform Economics
core_claim: Digital platforms extract seigniorage from their user-generated content currencies: just as sovereigns profit from the difference between a coin's face value and its metal content, platforms profit from the difference between the social value users attribute to their content (likes, shares, posts) and the marginal cost of hosting it. Platform enshittification follows the same debasement spiral as historical coinage — the platform progressively reduces the 'metal content' (organic reach, chronological feeds) while maintaining the 'face value' (user engagement metrics), until users invoke Gresham's Law and migrate to less-debased platforms.

name: Benford's Law Theory of Ecological Population Fraud Detection
core_claim: Fabricated ecological survey data can be detected using the same Benford's Law digit-frequency analysis applied in forensic accounting. Genuine population counts, biomass measurements, and species abundances follow the expected logarithmic first-digit distribution because they emerge from multiplicative natural processes. Fabricated or rounded data deviates systematically, with over-representation of round numbers and cognitive-bias-driven digit preferences, providing a statistical fingerprint of data fraud in ecological research.

name: Mycorrhizal Network Theory of Intercropping Communication
core_claim: Intercropping systems achieve resource complementarity not merely through niche partitioning but through common mycorrhizal network signaling that functions as an underground agricultural internet: stressed plants broadcast chemical distress signals through hyphal connections, triggering defensive compound production in neighboring crop species before pest arrival. The network's effectiveness depends on mycorrhizal host compatibility between crop species — polycultures with shared mycorrhizal partners outperform those without, regardless of above-ground complementarity.

name: Mycelial Routing Theory of Urban Transit
core_claim: Optimal public transit networks can be derived by applying mycelial resource-routing principles: routes that carry high passenger throughput are reinforced with greater frequency and capacity (analogous to nutrient-rich hyphal paths thickening), while underperforming routes are pruned. The resulting network topology converges on solutions that outperform centrally planned grids, as demonstrated by Physarum slime-mold experiments with Tokyo rail maps.

name: Costly Signal Gating Theory
core_claim: Systems facing high false-positive costs evolve multi-threshold activation gates where each successive trigger narrows the probability space, and the energy cost of the response mechanism itself enforces the requirement for redundant confirmation. The gate count converges on the minimum number where false-positive cost times false-positive rate falls below the per-gate maintenance cost.

name: Gresham's Law Theory of Attention Economy
core_claim: In information ecosystems, bad content drives out good through Gresham's Law dynamics: when low-effort content (debased currency) and high-effort content (sound currency) circulate at the same nominal value (one scroll-unit of attention), producers rationally hoard their best work (save the good coins) and flood the market with cheap content. The result is a spiral of content debasement that can only be arrested by establishing separate exchange rates — paywalls, curation, prestige signals — that re-differentiate the face value of content quality.

name: Navier-Stokes Theory of Crowd Dynamics
core_claim: Dense crowd movement in public spaces is governed by equations structurally identical to Navier-Stokes: each person acts as a fluid parcel with velocity and pressure fields, viscosity emerges from social friction (politeness norms, personal space), and boundary conditions are set by walls, barriers, and exits. Crowd crushes occur when the Reynolds number of crowd flow exceeds a critical threshold, transitioning from laminar (orderly) to turbulent (chaotic) flow in which individual agency is overwhelmed by collective momentum.

name: Ant Colony Pheromone Theory of Urban Pedestrian Infrastructure
core_claim: Optimal pedestrian path networks in cities should be designed through ant colony optimization: rather than imposing top-down path layouts, cities should observe emergent desire lines (pheromone trails) — the paths pedestrians actually take through parks, campuses, and neighborhoods — and pave the trails that accumulate the strongest 'pheromone signal' (foot traffic). The pheromone evaporation rate determines path persistence: paths in high-traffic areas maintain strong signals indefinitely, while paths in seasonal-use areas (beaches, festival grounds) experience seasonal evaporation and should be designed as temporary or adaptive infrastructure. The ant colony algorithm naturally produces near-optimal Steiner trees connecting multiple destinations with minimum total path length.
source_a: Ant Colony Pheromone Trail Optimization (Swarm Intelligence)

name: Superspreader Topology for Architectural Ventilation Design
core_claim: Epidemiological superspreader topology theory shows that heavy-tailed contact distributions produce outsized transmission from a few high-contact nodes. Applying this to building ventilation: airflow networks have their own 'superspreader' zones -- spaces where recirculated air from many zones converges before redistribution. These HVAC superspreader nodes amplify contaminant transmission disproportionately relative to their volume. Designing ventilation networks with superspreader topology analysis -- identifying and filtering at high-centrality air-mixing nodes rather than uniformly across all zones -- achieves equivalent pathogen reduction at 40-60% lower energy cost compared to uniform filtration strategies.

name: Morphogen Gradient Dyeing Theory
core_claim: Gradient dyeing techniques in textile engineering can be formalized using the morphogen gradient framework from developmental biology. The dye source acts as a morphogen-producing organizer, the fabric as the tissue field, and dye concentration thresholds determine color-zone boundaries with the same mathematics as Wolpert's positional information model. Predicting and controlling gradient dye patterns therefore reduces to managing source concentration, diffusion coefficients in the fiber matrix, and threshold sensitivities of different dye-fiber binding reactions — a framework that unifies resist-dyeing, dip-dyeing, and injection printing under one quantitative model.

name: Alloy Satisficing
core_claim: Metallurgical alloy design under real industrial constraints operates not by optimization but by satisficing: engineers search the composition-processing space and accept the first alloy meeting a threshold bundle of properties (yield strength, corrosion resistance, weldability, cost). The search order — starting from known alloy families and perturbing — determines which local satisfactory solution is found, producing path-dependent alloy traditions that vary by industrial culture. This explains why different national metallurgical traditions converge on different standard alloys for equivalent applications rather than discovering a single optimum.

name: Impedance-Matched Pedagogy
core_claim: Acoustic impedance matching — where energy transmission across a boundary is maximized when the impedances of the two media match — provides a quantitative framework for instructional design. The teacher and student each have a 'cognitive impedance' determined by their knowledge density, processing speed, and representational format. Maximum knowledge transfer occurs when these impedances are matched. Scaffolding functions as an impedance transformer (like the bones of the middle ear), converting high-impedance expert knowledge into a form that matches the student's lower impedance. The Zone of Proximal Development is precisely the impedance-matching bandwidth — the range of frequencies (concept complexities) that can be transmitted with acceptable loss.

name: Reaction-Diffusion Patterning of Urban Segregation
core_claim: Residential segregation patterns arise from a Turing-type instability in the coupled reaction-diffusion of two social groups. When the 'activation' rate of same-group clustering exceeds the 'inhibition' provided by cross-group economic interdependence, and when social diffusion rates differ between groups, stable spatial patterns of segregation spontaneously emerge from initially well-mixed conditions. The characteristic wavelength of neighborhood size is set by the ratio of social mobility rates between groups — predicting that unequal mobility produces finer-grained patchwork.

name: Quorum Sensing Theory of Classroom Engagement Thresholds
core_claim: Student engagement in classrooms activates through quorum sensing: individual students emit low-level 'autoinducer' signals (questions, visible note-taking, laughter) that accumulate in the social environment. Below a critical concentration threshold, each student remains in a low-expression state. Once the quorum is reached, a synchronized gene-expression-like switch activates collective engagement — participation becomes self-reinforcing. The threshold concentration explains why identical lectures produce radically different engagement in classes of similar composition, and why a few early participators can catalyze or inhibit the entire group.

name: Third-Body Layer Theory of Cultural Translation Zones
core_claim: At the interface between two cultures in sustained contact, a third-body layer forms spontaneously — a hybrid cultural material (pidgins, syncretic religions, fusion cuisines, border identities) that is neither culture A nor culture B but an emergent debris layer generated by their mutual friction. This third-body layer is self-organizing, adapts its composition to minimize destructive wear on both parent cultures, and can become load-bearing — the primary medium through which all intercultural exchange occurs — even as both parent cultures claim no affinity with it.

name: Fitts's Law Theory of Permaculture Zone Optimization
core_claim: Permaculture zone placement follows Fitts's Law of motor targeting: the frequency of human interaction with a garden element must be inversely proportional to its distance from the dwelling, weighted by the precision of care required. High-frequency, high-precision elements (herbs, salad greens) must occupy the near-field zone where the 'index of difficulty' is lowest, while low-frequency, low-precision elements (timber, forage) can tolerate the far-field. Violating this information-theoretic constraint on human motor capacity causes systematic neglect of misplaced elements.

name: Stigmergic Theory of Codebase Architecture
core_claim: Software architecture emerges through stigmergy rather than top-down design: each developer deposits 'traces' (abstractions, naming conventions, patterns) in the codebase that guide subsequent developers' actions. Well-designed code functions as a strong pheromone trail that channels future contributions along coherent architectural lines, while poorly structured code produces weak or contradictory traces that lead to architectural drift. Code review is the evaporation mechanism that degrades low-quality traces before they can mislead. The implication is that architecture documents are less important than the legibility of the traces already deposited in the code itself.

name: Jacquard Binary Theory of Gene Regulation
core_claim: Gene regulatory networks operate as biological Jacquard looms: each transcription factor acts as a binary hook (up/down) controlling whether a gene is expressed. The combinatorial pattern of thousands of simultaneous binary decisions across the genome weaves the 'fabric' of cell identity. Just as Jacquard punch cards encode complex patterns through simple binary lift decisions, epigenetic marks encode cell-type-specific expression programs through binary methylation/acetylation states.

name: Tobler's Law Theory of Musical Key Relations
core_claim: Perceived harmonic relatedness between musical keys follows Tobler's first law of geography: near keys (on the circle of fifths) are more related than distant keys, and this relationship governs not just harmonic tension but listener memory and expectation. Modulations to geographically 'nearby' keys feel natural because they preserve more shared pitch content, while modulations to distant keys produce the musical equivalent of teleportation disorientation. The circle of fifths is literally a geographic map of tonal space.

name: Wafer Lock Tolerance Theory of Software Vulnerability
core_claim: Software vulnerabilities are wafer lock tolerance exploits: security mechanisms with loose tolerances (permissive input validation, broad type coercion, generous timeout windows) can be bypassed by 'jiggling and raking' — fuzzing, parameter manipulation, and timing attacks that exploit the gap between the intended key and the range of inputs the lock actually accepts. Tight-tolerance software (strict typing, narrow validation, precise timing checks) resists these attacks but is harder to use (like a tight lock requiring a precisely cut key). The security-usability trade-off is fundamentally a tolerance specification problem.

name: Bypass Attack Theory of Regulatory Arbitrage
core_claim: Financial regulatory arbitrage is a bypass attack: rather than picking the lock (complying with regulations through the intended mechanism), sophisticated actors find attack surfaces that circumvent the key-pin interface entirely — offshore structures, synthetic instruments, and novel legal entities that route around the regulatory mechanism without technically violating it. The lock-picker's taxonomy of bypass attacks (shimming, comb picks, bumping) maps to regulatory bypass categories (structural arbitrage, jurisdictional arbitrage, temporal arbitrage). The defense, as in physical security, is to assume that every lock can be bypassed and design the security system with depth — multiple independent mechanisms that cannot all be bypassed by the same technique.

name: Cryptographic Trophic Cascade
core_claim: In layered cryptographic ecosystems, the compromise of a top-level primitive (e.g., a widely-used hash function) propagates downward through the dependency stack in a manner structurally identical to a trophic cascade. Removing the apex predator — the hardness assumption that keeps attackers in check — releases suppressed attack vectors at every lower level, causing protocol-level 'population explosions' of vulnerabilities. The magnitude of the cascade is governed by the topology of the dependency web: monoculture cryptographic stacks experience devastating cascades, while diverse stacks exhibit damped, localized effects.

name: Soil as Autopoietic System
core_claim: Mature soil is an autopoietic system: its biological and mineral components continuously regenerate the organizational structure that maintains the soil body's identity. Root exudates feed microbes that produce binding agents that stabilize aggregates that create pore space that sustains root growth. When this self-producing loop is disrupted (tillage, sterilization), the soil loses organizational closure and degrades toward an inert mineral substrate. This reframes soil health not as a checklist of chemical parameters but as the integrity of a self-maintaining boundary between organized pedosphere and disordered parent material.

name: Taphonomic Bias Theory of Historical Memory
core_claim: Collective memory of historical events is governed by preservation biases identical in structure to fossil taphonomy: only events embedded in favorable 'sedimentary' conditions (institutional recording, material culture, emotional salience) survive into the historical record, and the processes of decay, compression, and mineralization systematically distort what persists. The historical record is not a sample of the past but a taphonomic residue shaped by the chemistry of cultural preservation. Historiographic correction requires identifying the specific 'diagenetic' processes — political rewriting, archival neglect, medium decay — that have sculpted what appears to be evidence.

name: Stimulated Emission Rhetoric
core_claim: Laser physics' stimulated emission — where a photon passing an excited atom triggers release of an identical, phase-coherent photon, producing exponential amplification of a single mode — models how rhetorical cascade events work. In a population with high 'rhetorical inversion' (many people holding strong but unexpressed opinions), a single well-crafted public statement (seed photon) can trigger coherent, synchronized expression of identical sentiments. The 'optical cavity' is the media ecosystem that reflects and re-amplifies the message. The result is not merely viral spread but phase-coherent amplification: everyone saying the same thing at the same time with the same emotional valence, producing a rhetorical laser beam of extraordinary persuasive intensity.

name: Liminality as Psychosocial Phase Transition
core_claim: Rites of passage operate as controlled phase transitions in social identity: the liminal phase is the critical point at which the old identity structure loses long-range order before the new one crystallizes. Turner's communitas is the analogue of critical fluctuations — temporary equality and mutual recognition that exists only at the transition temperature. Societies that eliminate structured liminal experiences force individuals through uncontrolled phase transitions, producing the social equivalent of amorphous glass rather than well-ordered crystal: functional but brittle identities.

name: Preferential Attachment Corrosion Theory
core_claim: Pitting corrosion on metal surfaces follows preferential attachment dynamics: existing pits that are already large attract disproportionately more corrosive ions due to their deeper electrochemical gradients, growing faster than smaller pits in a rich-get-richer feedback loop. The resulting pit size distribution follows a power law, and the largest pit at any time can be predicted from the total pit population and the attachment exponent. Protective coating strategies should therefore focus on preventing the first few pits from reaching critical depth rather than uniformly reducing corrosion rate, because once preferential attachment takes hold, the distribution becomes dominated by extremes.

name: Taphonomic Selection Theory of Historical Memory
core_claim: Collective memory is not shaped primarily by the importance of events but by taphonomic selection — the differential preservation of evidence based on material and institutional 'sediment chemistry.' Events that occurred in contexts with robust recording media, institutional archivists, and stable political strata fossilize into the historical record; equally significant events in volatile or orally-mediated contexts decompose without trace. The historical canon is therefore a biased fossil assemblage, not a representative sample, and its apparent patterns reflect preservation filters as much as actual event distributions.

name: Mycorrhizal Network Theory of Institutional Knowledge Transfer
core_claim: Large organizations transfer tacit knowledge through common mycorrhizal network dynamics: experienced employees (mature trees) subsidize newcomers (seedlings) through underground fungal-like channels of mentorship and informal guidance, with the 'fungi' being mid-level managers and social connectors who extract a carbon tax (credit, influence) for facilitating the transfer. Organizations that eliminate these connector roles in restructuring suffer the equivalent of mycorrhizal network disruption — knowledge transfer collapses even when the source expertise remains intact.

name: Agroforestry Microclimate Theory of Urban Neighborhood Resilience
core_claim: Resilient urban neighborhoods function as agroforestry systems: the 'canopy layer' (major institutions, anchor businesses) buffers the microclimate for understory enterprises and residents, reducing temperature extremes (economic volatility), increasing humidity retention (social capital), and breaking wind (external shocks). When the canopy is removed (hospital closure, factory departure), the understory experiences the full force of macro-economic weather and often dies back. Effective urban planning should design for canopy-understory complementarity rather than monoculture development.

name: Inflammation Resolution Theory of Post-Conflict Reconciliation
core_claim: Post-conflict reconciliation follows the inflammation resolution cascade: an initial pro-inflammatory phase (truth commissions, trials, public reckoning) must actively switch to a pro-resolution phase (amnesty mechanisms, shared institutions) through the production of specialized mediators (restorative justice practitioners, cultural bridge-builders). Failure to switch — chronic inflammation — produces perpetual grievance cycles. The switch is not passive healing but requires active biosynthesis of resolution signals, explaining why reconciliation demands sustained institutional investment.

name: Hardy-Cross Theory of Permaculture Water Cascade Design
core_claim: Keyline water harvesting systems should be designed using Hardy-Cross iterative balancing: the network of swales, dams, and channels forms a closed hydraulic loop where water must be balanced across all paths simultaneously rather than optimized path-by-path. Each iteration corrects flow imbalances by redistributing water between swale segments according to their head-loss characteristics, converging on a design where every part of the landscape receives proportional hydration without any single swale overtopping or running dry.

name: Drake Equation Theory of Innovation Pipeline Forecasting
core_claim: The expected number of commercially viable innovations emerging from an R&D portfolio can be estimated by a Drake-like product of conditional probabilities: the rate of idea generation, the fraction that survive initial screening, the probability of technical feasibility, the fraction that find product-market fit, and the expected commercial lifespan. Like the Drake Equation, the value of this framework lies not in its output but in forcing explicit estimation of each factor, revealing which stages of the pipeline are genuine bottlenecks versus which are merely assumed to be. Most organizations dramatically overestimate the idea generation term and underestimate the product-market fit fraction.

name: Enzyme Kinetics Theory of Bureaucratic Processing
core_claim: Government bureaucracies process citizen requests following Michaelis-Menten saturation kinetics: processing rate increases linearly with request volume at low loads, but saturates at Vmax when all bureaucratic 'active sites' (caseworkers, review committees) are occupied. The Km of a bureaucracy (the request volume at half-max processing rate) reveals its institutional efficiency. Reforms that add active sites shift Vmax; reforms that reduce procedural complexity lower Km. Most reform efforts fail because they target the wrong parameter.

name: Key Bitting Combinatorial Theory of Password Security
core_claim: Password policies can be optimized using key bitting combinatorial analysis: just as MACS (Maximum Adjacent Cut Specification) eliminates physically impossible keys from the theoretical keyspace, password composition rules should eliminate cognitively impossible passwords — combinations that no human would actually choose or remember. The effective password space is not 95^n but the much smaller subset satisfying human cognitive MACS constraints. Security improves not by expanding the theoretical keyspace (longer passwords) but by ensuring the effective keyspace is explored uniformly — the locksmith's lesson that a million possible keys provide no security if every lock ships with the same three bittings.

name: Brood Cycle Thermoregulation Theory of Data Center Management
core_claim: Optimal data center thermal management follows honeybee brood cycle thermoregulation: worker bees maintain the brood nest at exactly 35 degrees C through a distributed feedback system where individual bees fan, cluster, or evaporate water based on local temperature sensing, without any central thermostat. Data centers that replace centralized HVAC control with distributed, server-level thermal agents — each adjusting its own fan speed, workload acceptance, and heat dissipation based on local conditions — can achieve the same remarkable thermal precision (plus or minus 0.5 degrees C) at dramatically lower energy cost, because the control granularity matches the heat generation granularity.

name: Synaptic Niche Construction
core_claim: Active synapses modify their local biochemical microenvironment (the 'synaptic niche'), which reshapes selection pressures on future plasticity. Learning is co-evolution between neural activity and the micro-environment it constructs.

name: Decision Tempo Nudging
core_claim: Choice architecture can be optimized by matching nudge timing to the OODA decision cycle — interventions placed at the Orient phase (when mental models update) are disproportionately effective compared to those at Decide or Act, because they reshape the framing before options crystallize.

name: Preferential Pattern Accretion
core_claim: Design patterns accumulate connections proportional to existing connectivity, causing a few hub patterns to dominate built environments while most remain peripheral — producing scale-free topology rather than Alexander's egalitarian web.

mapped_components.unit: Design pattern — a named recurring problem-solution pair
mapped_components.variation: New patterns emerge and attach to existing ones; each references a subset of established patterns
mapped_components.selection: Attachment probability proportional to existing degree — rich-get-richer dynamics
mapped_components.fitness: Aliveness of resulting space correlates with centrality of instantiated pattern subgraph
mapped_components.boundary: Cultural context, building technology, and regulation set growth rate and initial conditions

falsifiable_predictions: Alexander's 253 patterns show power-law degree distribution with <10% accounting for >50% of cross-references.
Buildings instantiating higher mean-degree pattern subgraphs rate as more 'alive' in post-occupancy evaluation.
Novel patterns attached to hub patterns diffuse faster regardless of intrinsic functional merit.

name: Nested Loop Habit Architecture
core_claim: Sustainable behavior change requires engineering three nested habit loops — micro (seconds), meso (session), macro (identity) — where each loop's reward serves as the cue for the next iteration. Keystone habits succeed when they function as the core loop of a personal behavioral game.

name: Titled Memory Governance
core_claim: Rust's ownership model maps to property law: every memory value has exactly one owner (title holder), borrowing follows lease semantics, and lifetimes are easement durations. This reframes memory safety as a legal governance problem with enforceable property rights.

name: Pellicle Theory of Organizational Culture
core_claim: Team culture forms like a kombucha SCOBY: a symbiotic colony of practices and values that creates a protective pellicle at the interface between the team and its environment. The pellicle is both product and producer of the culture beneath it.

name: Derelict Systems Salvage Theory
core_claim: Abandoned complex systems follow identical legal and operational dynamics governing claim, custody, and value preservation. The tension between law of finds (full ownership) and law of salvage (compensated stewardship) maps onto the rewrite-vs-refactor decision, where institutional memory constitutes archaeological value.

name: Single-Stroke Intervention Theory
core_claim: Optimal intervention in any complex adaptive system requires a single, precisely calibrated action along the system's natural grain rather than repeated or broad-spectrum forcing. The interventionist must read the substrate to identify the correct angle, force, and timing that preserves systemic integrity.

name: Necrophoretic Organizational Theory
core_claim: Organizations operate a continuous death-signal detection system where units must actively suppress markers of failure through performative vitality signaling. Units that fail to mask decline markers trigger automated removal processes regardless of actual productivity, creating systematic vulnerability to signal-based misclassification.

name: Macrophagic Persistence Theory
core_claim: Institutional memory persists not because organizations actively maintain it but because certain knowledge is continuously consumed and re-expressed by institutional macrophages — individuals and processes that ingest departing knowledge and re-present it to new members, creating the illusion of permanence through constant turnover.

name: Complication Load Theory
core_claim: Complexity cost per module grows super-linearly because each new module must negotiate constraint surfaces with all existing modules, not just the base platform. A system's maximum viable complication count is determined by the ratio of base-architecture tolerance to inter-module coupling density, and exceeding this threshold produces catastrophic rather than graceful degradation.

name: Checkpoint Hijack Theory of Institutional Immunity
core_claim: Complex adaptive systems maintain integrity through inhibitory checkpoints that distinguish self from threat. Parasitic agents survive by co-opting checkpoint signaling to mask exploitation as legitimate function. Restoring defense requires checkpoint disruption, which carries an inherent tradeoff: the same removal of brakes that enables attack on parasites also risks autoimmune destruction of healthy components.

name: Interstitial Assembly Theory
core_claim: The capability of any composite system is governed more by the quality of its negative space — the interfaces, gaps, and relational channels between units — than by the quality of the units themselves. Optimizing unit-to-unit fit requires irreversible reshaping, and emergent function is only legible at a scale removed from individual components.

name: Civilizational Subduction Theory
core_claim: Cultural collisions operate through a subduction mechanic: the less institutionally dense culture is forced beneath the dominant one, where its practices are heated under social pressure until they partially melt and re-emerge as hybrid expressions — syncretic religions, creole languages, fusion cuisines. The collision zone generates social friction, new institutional formations, and deep identity crises whose intensity correlates with the density differential.

name: Cryptobiotic Organizational Theory
core_claim: Organizations facing existential stress should execute a deliberate, reversible metabolic shutdown — replacing liquid operational capacity with stabilized minimal structures. Survival duration scales not with reserves burned during active resistance, but with the fidelity of the preservation state and the speed of rehydration protocols pre-encoded before shutdown.

name: Symbiotic Entity Theory
core_claim: High-performing entities in hostile environments are obligate symbioses between a structural partner (resource acquisition, protection) and a generative partner (core value creation). Resilience is proportional to the tightness of metabolic coupling, and failure mode is almost always decoupling rather than external destruction.

name: Institutional Petrification Theory
core_claim: Institutions undergo petrification: a molecule-by-molecule replacement of original purpose, personnel, and animating culture with bureaucratic mineral — procedural compliance, credentialism, self-perpetuation — while preserving the exact external structure of the founding organization. The result is an entity that exhibits perfect morphological fidelity to its origin while being composed of entirely different operational substance.

name: Latent Succession Infrastructure Theory
core_claim: Complex systems that persist across leadership transitions maintain a dormant developmental pipeline — organisms or candidates kept in an undifferentiated state, activatable by environmental signals. The cost of maintaining idle succession capacity is justified because the alternative produces colony or organizational collapse at a rate that exceeds the maintenance cost over evolutionary or market timescales.

name: Pod Strategy Lock-In Theory
core_claim: Coordinated teams develop a single dominant strategy through cultural transmission and reinforcement, creating deep competence but structural rigidity. Switching strategies carries prohibitive retraining costs because the coordination patterns, role specializations, and tacit knowledge are co-adapted to one approach. Optimal performance requires accepting strategic monomorphism rather than pursuing versatility.

name: Caldera Governance Theory
core_claim: Organizational crises that fully exhaust a central authority's resources trigger an inward structural collapse analogous to caldera formation, where the resulting vacuum is not mere absence but a topologically distinct basin that preferentially accumulates novel talent, projects, and culture impossible under the prior regime. The depth and completeness of collapse determines ecosystem richness, suggesting organizations should resist premature re-centralization.

name: Attention Allocation Games in Neurolinguistic Processing
core_claim: Game theory's attention allocation framework -- where players distribute finite cognitive attention across simultaneous strategic interactions -- directly models how the brain allocates processing resources across concurrent linguistic tasks (reading while listening, simultaneous translation, monitoring multiple conversations). The model predicts that linguistic tasks with higher 'payoff volatility' (ambiguous syntax, unfamiliar accents) command disproportionate attention, creating neglect-induced errors in concurrent lower-volatility tasks. This explains why simultaneous interpreters make more lexical errors during syntactically complex passages: attention is reallocated to the higher-variance syntactic game.

name: Marine Biofilm Stigmergy
core_claim: Sessile marine organisms colonizing hard substrates (coral larvae, barnacles, tube worms) practice a form of stigmergy: early colonizers deposit biochemical traces that alter the substrate's chemical and textural properties, indirectly guiding the settlement decisions of later arrivals. The resulting community structure is not centrally coordinated but emerges from layered environmental modification, producing predictable succession sequences. Reef restoration could be dramatically improved by engineering artificial substrates that replicate the stigmergic trace signatures of mature reef biofilms rather than the structural geometry alone.

name: Rhetorical Impedance Matching
core_claim: Persuasive communication fails at audience boundaries for the same reason acoustic energy reflects at medium interfaces: impedance mismatch. A speaker's rhetorical register (vocabulary density, emotional amplitude, epistemic assumptions) functions as communicative impedance. When it closely matches the audience's cognitive-cultural impedance, argumentative energy transmits efficiently. Mismatched impedance causes reflection — the audience hears but does not absorb. Skilled rhetoricians intuitively perform impedance matching by modulating register, just as horn loudspeakers use flared geometry to match air impedance.

name: Covenantal Versioning
core_claim: Covenant theology's mechanism — where successive covenants in divine history supersede prior ones while fulfilling their essential promises — maps precisely onto software API versioning strategy. Each API version constitutes a 'covenant' between provider and consumer. A new version must fulfill the core commitments of the prior covenant (backward-compatible data contracts) while superseding its operational terms (deprecated endpoints, new authentication). The 'redemptive completeness' metric becomes API coverage completeness: a version history is well-designed when each successor makes the prior covenant unnecessary while honoring every promise it made.

name: Crystal Nucleation Theory of Social Movements
core_claim: Social movements form through a nucleation mechanism: diffuse grievances exist in a supersaturated solution of discontent, but collective action only crystallizes when a critical nucleus of committed actors exceeds a threshold size. Below this critical radius, movements dissolve back into atomized complaint. Above it, crystallization propagates rapidly through the population. Impurities — pre-existing organizational structures, charismatic leaders — lower the nucleation barrier, explaining why movements often catalyze around existing institutions rather than forming de novo.

name: Quantum Circuit Compilation of Fermentation Pathways
core_claim: Metabolic pathway optimization in industrial fermentation operates under the same pressure as quantum circuit compilation: the goal is to minimize the total number of enzymatic steps (gates) and the longest sequential dependency chain (circuit depth) to reach a target metabolite, subject to the 'native gate set' of available enzymes and the 'hardware connectivity' of metabolic network topology. Synthetic biology pathway engineering is literally compiling a desired chemical transformation into the minimum-depth enzyme circuit executable on the host organism's metabolic hardware, and the same depth-vs-fidelity tradeoffs apply.

name: PageRank Ventilation Theory
core_claim: Indoor air quality in complex buildings can be modeled by applying PageRank to the room-connectivity graph where directed edges represent airflow paths weighted by volumetric flow rate. Rooms with high PageRank scores accumulate pollutants because they are downstream recipients of air from many upstream zones, analogous to web pages receiving many inbound links. The damping factor maps to air exchange rate with the exterior. HVAC optimization should target not the most polluted rooms but the highest-PageRank rooms, because interventions there propagate benefits through the network's stationary distribution.

name: Autocatalytic Set Theory of Startup Ecosystems
core_claim: Startup ecosystems achieve self-sustaining growth only when they form an autocatalytic set: each type of entity (founders, investors, universities, acquirers, talent pools, service providers) catalyzes the production of at least one other type, and the set is collectively self-sustaining under catalytic closure. Silicon Valley is a mature autocatalytic set where every species catalyzes at least one other. Failed ecosystem-building efforts (government tech parks, artificial innovation districts) fail because they inject individual molecular species without achieving catalytic closure — they provide funding and lab space but lack the acquirer-to-serial-founder recycling catalyst or the university-to-spinoff reaction. The minimum viable ecosystem can be predicted by finding the smallest autocatalytically closed subset of the full reaction network.

name: Locard Exchange Theory of Pedagogy
core_claim: Every pedagogical contact leaves a bidirectional trace: the teacher deposits conceptual fragments in the student and simultaneously absorbs the student's naive models, misconceptions, and fresh perspectives. Teaching therefore contaminates the expert's knowledge structure with the novice's framing over time, explaining both why veteran teachers develop superior intuition for student confusion and why they progressively lose access to their own original learning trajectory. The forensic implication: the quality of any educational encounter can be assessed by examining the traces left in both directions, not just the student's test scores.

name: Ant Colony Optimization Theory of Wound Vascularization
core_claim: Angiogenic sprouting during wound healing follows an ant colony optimization algorithm: endothelial tip cells lay down VEGF-reinforced 'pheromone trails' as they extend toward hypoxic tissue, subsequent sprouts preferentially follow higher-concentration paths, and evaporation (VEGF degradation) prevents lock-in to suboptimal routes. The resulting vascular network converges on a near-optimal solution to the oxygen delivery problem without central coordination, and pathological healing (keloids, chronic wounds) corresponds to pheromone saturation or evaporation failure.

name: Honeybee Waggle Dance Theory of Scientific Citation
core_claim: Scientific citations function as waggle dances: each citation communicates the direction (intellectual lineage) and distance (methodological proximity) of a productive resource site (prior finding) to other foragers (researchers). High-impact papers recruit more dancers (citers), and the dance's precision degrades with distance — citations to methodologically remote work are interpreted less accurately by the audience. Citation networks thus constitute a decentralized recruitment system where the colony (discipline) allocates foraging effort through the accumulated choreography of individual dancers, not through central planning.

name: Pest Dilution Theory of Misinformation Resistance
core_claim: Informationally diverse media ecosystems resist misinformation through the pest dilution effect: when an audience consumes information from many different sources (high crop diversity), any single misinformation vector (pest species) encounters non-host material more often than host material, reducing its effective transmission rate. Monoculture information diets (consuming only one news source or platform) create the conditions for epidemic-level misinformation damage, just as agricultural monocultures enable pest outbreaks. The prescription is not better pesticide (fact-checking) but greater crop diversity (source plurality).

name: Ant Colony Pheromone Theory of Archival Discovery
core_claim: Researchers navigating archival collections follow pheromone-trail dynamics: each successful discovery leaves traces — citations, finding-aid annotations, catalog notes — that evaporate over time but temporarily strengthen paths for subsequent researchers. Well-trodden collections develop reinforced trails that attract disproportionate attention, while equally rich but un-pheromoned collections remain undiscovered. The decay rate of scholarly attention determines whether the archive self-organizes toward optimal coverage or locks into suboptimal canonical paths.

name: Acid Paper Degradation Theory of Institutional Memory Loss
core_claim: Organizations lose institutional memory following the same kinetics as acid paper degradation: the acidic byproducts of normal organizational metabolism (turnover, restructuring, system migrations) catalyze the hydrolysis of knowledge-bearing substrates (documentation, tacit expertise, relationship networks). The degradation follows a sigmoid curve — negligible for decades, then rapidly catastrophic — and is accelerated by heat (organizational stress) and humidity (information overload). Deacidification requires active, sustained intervention (knowledge management programs) that most organizations begin too late.

name: Tephra Dispersal Theory of Misinformation Spread
core_claim: Misinformation spreads through media ecosystems like tephra from a volcanic eruption: the largest fragments (outright fabrications, easily debunked) settle close to the source and are quickly identified. Medium-sized particles (misleading framings, selective statistics) travel further and accumulate in a broader ring. The finest ash (subtle bias, shifted Overton windows, normalized assumptions) reaches global distribution, persists in the atmosphere for years, and is nearly impossible to clean up. The size-sorting is aerodynamic: larger lies have higher terminal velocity in the epistemic medium and fall out of circulation faster.

name: Galvanic Corrosion Theory of Bimetallic Currency Instability
core_claim: Bimetallic monetary systems fail through a mechanism isomorphic to galvanic corrosion: when two metals of different 'economic nobility' (intrinsic value relative to face value) are coupled in a common monetary electrolyte (market exchange), the less noble metal is preferentially dissolved from circulation as it is hoarded, melted, or exported. The rate of monetary corrosion depends on the potential difference between the metals' market and face values, exactly as galvanic corrosion rate depends on the electrode potential difference.

name: Reynolds Flocking Theory of Re-epithelialization
core_claim: Collective cell migration during wound re-epithelialization follows Reynolds flocking rules: each keratinocyte at the wound edge applies three local rules — separation (avoid overcrowding neighboring cells), alignment (match migration direction with neighbors), and cohesion (move toward the local center of mass of the advancing front). These three simple rules, without any global coordinator, produce the coherent sheet migration observed in re-epithelialization, and the failure modes of wound closure (stalling, fingering, incomplete coverage) correspond to specific parameter regimes in the flocking model.

name: Immune Clonal Selection Theory of Startup Ecosystems
core_claim: Startup ecosystems function as adaptive immune systems for economies: venture-funded companies are lymphocyte clones, each encoding a different 'antigen receptor' (business hypothesis). When a market opportunity (antigen) appears, the startup with the closest product-market fit undergoes clonal expansion (scaling), while its competitors undergo apoptosis (failure). Somatic hypermutation corresponds to the rapid pivoting of early-stage startups, and the memory B-cell analog is the serial entrepreneur who retains pattern recognition from previous market encounters. The ecosystem's diversity is its immune repertoire, and monoculture venture strategies produce the equivalent of immunodeficiency.

name: Acoustic Impedance Matching Theory of Psychotherapy
core_claim: Therapeutic alliance functions as acoustic impedance matching: the therapist must match the 'impedance' of the client's emotional and cognitive style to maximize the transmission of therapeutic insight across the boundary between them. Impedance mismatch -- a highly analytical therapist with a highly emotional client, or vice versa -- causes most of the therapeutic 'signal' to be reflected back rather than transmitted, regardless of the signal's content quality. The middle ear bones of therapy are the therapeutic techniques (reframing, validation, Socratic questioning) that transform the impedance of the intervention to match the client's receptive characteristics. This explains why therapeutic modality matters less than therapeutic alliance: all modalities work when impedance is matched.

name: Crystal Nucleation Theory of Startup Formation
core_claim: Startup formation follows crystal nucleation kinetics: entrepreneurial 'embryonic nuclei' (small teams with an idea) form constantly through thermal fluctuations in the talent market, but only those exceeding a critical radius (minimum viable team size, capital threshold, market timing) survive to grow. Below the critical nucleus size, surface-energy costs (coordination overhead, burn rate) exceed volume-energy gains (revenue potential), and the startup dissolves back into the talent pool.

name: Hormesis Theory of Creative Practice
core_claim: Creative output follows a hormetic dose-response to constraint: zero constraint produces unfocused, low-quality work; moderate constraint (deadlines, formal requirements, material limitations) triggers adaptive creative responses that produce the strongest work; excessive constraint crushes creative capacity entirely. The optimal 'dose window' of constraint is narrow and individual-specific, explaining why the same workshop exercise liberates one artist and paralyzes another.

name: Perfume Pyramid Theory of Narrative Structure
core_claim: Effective narratives follow the temporal architecture of a perfume pyramid: top notes (the opening hook) are volatile and attention-grabbing but dissipate within minutes of engagement; heart notes (character development, thematic complexity) emerge as the top notes fade and constitute the bulk of the reader's experience; base notes (the residual meaning, the haunting final image) are heavy molecules that persist long after the book is closed. Stories that front-load base notes feel ponderous; those with only top notes feel disposable. The fixative is genre convention, which retards the evaporation of familiar elements.

name: Swarm Trigger Threshold Theory of Bank Runs
core_claim: Bank runs initiate through a mechanism identical to honeybee swarm trigger dynamics: individual depositors accumulate private signals of institutional distress (scout bees assessing candidate sites), and withdrawal begins only when the density of alarmed depositors exceeds a quorum threshold — not when any single signal crosses a critical value. The quorum mechanism explains why banks can survive severe individual shocks but fail to apparently minor ones that happen to synchronize across the depositor population. Deposit insurance works by raising the quorum threshold, not by eliminating individual alarm signals.

name: Olfactory Fatigue Theory of News Desensitization
core_claim: News desensitization follows olfactory receptor adaptation kinetics: sustained exposure to a particular class of news stimulus (violence, political scandal, pandemic updates) causes the corresponding cognitive receptors to downregulate, raising the detection threshold until only increasingly extreme stimuli register. The adaptation is stimulus-specific — desensitization to political news does not reduce sensitivity to celebrity gossip, just as adaptation to one odorant leaves other receptors intact. Media organizations compensate by escalating stimulus intensity (sensationalism) or introducing novel odorants (new crisis categories), producing an attention economy locked in an arms race against its own audience's adaptive biology.

name: Gear Train Theory of Supply Chain Amplification
core_claim: The bullwhip effect in supply chains is a gear train backlash problem: each tier of the supply chain is a gear that amplifies torque (order size) while reducing speed (responsiveness). The cumulative backlash — dead zones between gear teeth where motion is transmitted with delay and distortion — produces the characteristic oscillatory amplification of demand signals as they propagate upstream. Just as horological gear trains minimize backlash through tight tolerances and anti-backlash spring gears, supply chains reduce the bullwhip effect through information sharing (eliminating dead zones) and vendor-managed inventory (anti-backlash mechanisms that maintain constant tooth contact).

name: Harmonic Preferential Attachment
core_claim: In the evolution of musical styles, certain chord progressions and melodic motifs acquire disproportionate prevalence not because they are objectively superior but because they benefit from preferential attachment: the more frequently a progression appears in the canon, the more likely composers and listeners are to reference and reinforce it. This produces a power-law distribution of harmonic vocabulary usage, with a small number of 'hub' progressions (I-IV-V-I, ii-V-I) dominating, while the vast majority of possible progressions remain unexplored. Genre revolutions occur when a critical mass of musicians deliberately route new edges to low-degree nodes.

name: Argumentative Nucleation
core_claim: Rhetorical movements crystallize through a nucleation process: scattered dissenting claims circulate as subcritical clusters in public discourse, unable to sustain themselves. When a cluster exceeds a critical mass — enough mutually reinforcing arguments with sufficient internal coherence — it overcomes the activation energy barrier of established consensus and rapidly grows into a crystalline ideological formation. Failed movements are subcritical nuclei that dissolved back into noise. This explains why identical ideas succeed at some historical moments and fail at others: the supersaturation of public frustration determines whether the nucleus can grow.

name: Lotka-Volterra Dynamics of Attention and Content
core_claim: Online content ecosystems exhibit predator-prey oscillations where content creators (prey) and audience attention (predator) are locked in coupled dynamics. When creators are abundant and attention scarce, only the fittest content survives; the resulting high-quality pool attracts attention growth, which then enables lower-quality content to survive, diluting the pool until attention crashes. Platform health is determined by the oscillation amplitude: shallow cycles produce stable ecosystems while deep cycles produce boom-bust virality. Algorithmic recommendation acts as an environmental parameter that tunes the coupling strength between the two populations.

name: Extinction Learning Markets
core_claim: Exposure therapy's mechanism — where repeated presentation of a feared stimulus without aversive outcome gradually overwrites the conditioned fear response through inhibitory learning — explains how financial markets recover from crash-induced risk aversion. Post-crash, market participants exhibit conditioned fear responses to stimuli associated with the crash (rising leverage ratios, yield curve inversions). Recovery occurs through 'market exposure therapy': repeated encounters with these stimuli without accompanying losses gradually build inhibitory associations. The context-specificity of extinction learning explains why recovery is fragile and fear can return when market conditions shift to resemble the original crash context.

name: Trophic Cascade Theory of Market Regulation
core_claim: Financial regulators function as apex predators in market ecosystems: their presence suppresses predatory intermediaries (mesopredators), which in turn allows small investors (primary producers) to flourish. Regulatory removal triggers a mesopredator release — proliferation of extractive financial actors that devastate retail participation. The effect propagates across at least three trophic levels, meaning that the full impact of deregulation is not visible at the level where the regulator operated but one or two levels below.

name: Graph Coloring Crop Rotation Theory
core_claim: Optimal crop rotation on a spatially heterogeneous farm can be solved as a graph coloring problem where each field-season pair is a vertex, adjacency encodes spatial proximity and temporal succession, and colors represent crop families. The chromatic number of this graph gives the minimum number of crop families needed to satisfy all pest-break and nutrient-cycling constraints simultaneously. Fields sharing irrigation or drainage edges impose additional coloring constraints that standard rotation calendars miss, and the four-color theorem guarantees that planar field arrangements always admit solutions with at most four crop families.

name: Morphogen Gradient Theory of Urban Gentrification
core_claim: Gentrification propagates through urban space as a morphogen gradient: a source neighborhood (the artsy district, the tech campus) emits a diffusing signal of rising property values and cultural capital, and surrounding neighborhoods interpret threshold concentrations as fate instructions — below one threshold they remain unchanged, above another they fully differentiate into luxury residential, and in the intermediate zone they adopt a mixed transitional phenotype. Displacement of existing populations is the developmental apoptosis of this urban morphogenesis.

name: Acid Paper Degradation Theory of Software Technical Debt
core_claim: Legacy codebases degrade through a mechanism identical to acid paper degradation: the acid catalyst (poor architectural decisions, deprecated dependencies) is embedded at creation time and causes slow hydrolytic chain scission of the code's logical polymers. The degradation rate follows first-order kinetics — each breaking change accelerates subsequent breakage. Deacidification (refactoring) can halt but not reverse the damage, and the decision between preservation (maintaining legacy) and migration (reformatting to new media) follows the same cost curves that archivists use for paper collections.

name: Digital Format Obsolescence Theory of Language Death
core_claim: Minority languages die through a process identical to digital format obsolescence: the language (format) remains internally coherent but the ecosystem of readers (speakers, institutions, media) migrates to dominant formats, rendering the minority language unreadable not because of any intrinsic deficiency but because the rendering infrastructure (schools, government services, economic incentives) no longer supports it. Preservation requires the archival science strategy of format migration — continuously translating the language's cultural content into whatever format the current ecosystem supports, while maintaining the original encoding for future recovery.

name: Mnemonic Scaffolding Theory of Software API Design
core_claim: Well-designed APIs employ the same mnemonic scaffolding strategies as oral epic traditions: formulaic expressions (consistent naming conventions), thematic clustering (related endpoints grouped by resource), ring composition (request-response symmetry), and metrical constraints (consistent parameter ordering) all serve to reduce the cognitive load of memorizing the interface. APIs that violate these oral-mnemonic principles require constant documentation reference — the developer equivalent of a bard who cannot perform without reading from a scroll — while APIs that embody them become 'recitable' from memory after moderate exposure.

name: Dam Overtopping Theory of Emotional Burnout Cascades
core_claim: Professional burnout follows dam overtopping cascade dynamics: emotional stressors accumulate as reservoir inflow behind the dam of coping mechanisms. Overtopping begins not as catastrophic failure but as erosive sheet flow over the crest, which progressively deepens the breach channel through positive feedback — each increment of emotional overflow erodes more coping capacity, accelerating further overflow. The cascade is irreversible once the breach reaches a critical depth, explaining why burnout transitions from manageable stress to total collapse are rapid and difficult to reverse.

name: Formulaic Speech Preservation Theory of Software API Stability
core_claim: Software APIs persist not because they are well-designed but because they are formulaic: heavily-used API patterns become metrically fixed (exact parameter orders, naming conventions, return types) and resist modification through the same selection pressures that preserve oral formulaic expressions. Developers transmit API usage patterns through code copying (oral reproduction), and any mutation to a high-frequency formula is immediately selected against by build failures across thousands of downstream dependents. The most stable APIs are the most formulaic, not the most elegant.

name: Isostatic Rebound Theory of Post-Colonial Cultural Recovery
core_claim: Cultures recovering from colonial suppression undergo isostatic rebound: the removal of the colonial 'ice sheet' (political domination, cultural suppression, economic extraction) allows the depressed cultural lithosphere to slowly rise back toward equilibrium. The rebound is not immediate but lags by decades to centuries, as the cultural mantle (intergenerational memory, language revitalization, institutional rebuilding) flows viscously back into the depression. The rebound overshoots equilibrium, producing periods of cultural hypercorrection (aggressive purism, exclusionary nativism) before settling into a new isostatic balance.

name: Dam Overtopping Theory of Alarm Fatigue Collapse
core_claim: Alarm fatigue in ergonomic systems cascades through a dam overtopping failure mechanism: each ignored alarm erodes the 'dam' of operator vigilance by a small increment, while the reservoir of unattended alerts rises. Once overtopping begins — when the operator's cognitive threshold is breached — erosion becomes self-reinforcing as the breach widens, ignored alarm volume increases, and the operator's entire monitoring capacity fails catastrophically. The critical variable is the freeboard (spare cognitive capacity) maintained above the normal alarm water level.

name: Jacquard Binary Patterning Theory of Genetic Regulation
core_claim: Gene regulatory networks operate as biological Jacquard looms: each transcription factor binding event is a binary hook-lift decision (bound/unbound), and the combinatorial pattern of thousands of such decisions across the genome produces the complex 'fabric' of cell-type-specific gene expression. The developmental program is the punch card, and cell differentiation is the sequential reading of cards that produce increasingly specific tissue patterns. Epigenetic modifications function as the card storage mechanism, preserving the pattern instructions across cell divisions. Cancer represents a corrupted card deck producing chaotic, non-repeating patterns.

name: Herd Immunity Theory of Misinformation Resistance
core_claim: Populations develop resistance to misinformation through a herd immunity mechanism: when a sufficient fraction of the population has been 'immunized' against a specific false claim -- through prebunking, media literacy, or prior exposure to debunked versions -- the claim cannot sustain transmission even among the remaining susceptible individuals. The critical immunization threshold depends on the misinformation's basic reproduction number (virality), and falls between 60-90% for most viral false claims. Partial immunization campaigns that fail to reach threshold produce the worst outcome: a population confident enough to stop fact-checking but not immune enough to resist novel variants of the claim.

name: Hamilton's Rule Theory of Open Source Contribution
core_claim: Open source contribution follows Hamilton's rule of kin selection: developers contribute code (altruistic cost c) when the relatedness r (shared technological stack, shared employer, shared user community) multiplied by the benefit b (value to the project) exceeds the cost. This explains why contributions cluster within 'kinship groups' defined by shared technology identity (Rust developers contributing to Rust ecosystem, regardless of project), why corporate sponsorship works (it increases r by making contributors organizational kin), and why projects that fail to establish strong community identity struggle to attract contributions -- they have low r across all potential contributors.

name: Lanchester's Law Theory of Market Share Warfare
core_claim: Competition between firms in saturated markets follows Lanchester's square law: a firm's attrition advantage over competitors scales with the square of its resource concentration (sales force, advertising spend, distribution points) in a given market segment. This explains why market leaders with 2x the resources achieve 4x the competitive impact, and why niche strategies (concentrating force in narrow segments) can defeat larger opponents who spread resources evenly.

name: Eusociality Theory of Open-Source Software
core_claim: Sustained contributions to open-source projects follow Hamilton's rule for eusociality: developers contribute unpaid labor when rb > c, where r is 'relatedness' (shared technical identity, community membership, reputational linkage), b is the benefit to the project community, and c is the contributor's opportunity cost. Projects that maximize perceived r (through strong community identity, shared tooling, and contributor recognition) can sustain altruistic contribution at levels that projects relying solely on individual benefit cannot.

name: Psychoacoustic Masking Theory of Competitive Strategy
core_claim: In saturated markets, competing products mask each other following psychoacoustic principles: a dominant product (loud signal) renders competitors within its 'critical band' (feature similarity window) imperceptible to consumers, regardless of their individual quality. Startups succeed not by being louder but by occupying unmasked frequency bands — feature combinations or value propositions that fall outside the masking threshold of incumbents. The temporal masking effect explains why launching immediately before or after a major competitor's release produces forward and backward masking of consumer attention.

name: Queen Pheromone Theory of Startup Culture
core_claim: Startup culture is maintained by a founder pheromone analogous to the queen mandibular pheromone: the founder's physical presence, communication style, and behavioral patterns emit chemical-like social signals that suppress 'reproductive' behavior in employees (starting their own ventures, pursuing independent agendas) and maintain colony cohesion.
