When the founder departs or the company exceeds the pheromone's effective radius (~150 employees, matching Dunbar's number), the suppressive signal attenuates, workers begin 'laying eggs' (side projects, political factions), and the colony either produces a new queen (cultural successor) or fragments into swarms (spin-offs).

name: Thematic Map Classification Theory of Psychiatric Diagnosis
core_claim: Psychiatric diagnostic categories are thematic map classification schemes applied to the continuous surface of human psychological variation. The choice of classification method — natural breaks (cluster-based nosology), equal interval (symptom count thresholds), or quantile (prevalence-normed) — determines which variations are visible and which are obscured. DSM categories use natural breaks that maximize within-diagnosis homogeneity but create artificial boundaries where the underlying surface is smooth. Dimensional approaches are the continuous-tone maps that eliminate class boundaries but sacrifice the diagnostic legibility that clinicians require for treatment decisions.

name: Stribeck Curve Theory of Organizational Communication
core_claim: Organizational communication friction follows a Stribeck curve: at very low communication velocity (rare meetings, minimal email), friction is high and static (boundary lubrication — people interact through formal, rigid protocols). As communication frequency increases, friction drops dramatically as a hydrodynamic film of familiarity and shared context develops (full-film lubrication). But at excessive communication velocity (constant Slack messages, daily standups, meeting culture), friction rises again due to turbulent losses — information overload, context-switching costs, and the paradox of overcommunication where more messages produce less understanding. The Stribeck optimum is the communication frequency that minimizes total organizational friction.

name: Perfume Pyramid Theory of Software Architecture
core_claim: Robust software architectures follow the temporal layering of a perfume pyramid. The top notes (UI, API surface) are volatile and frequently changed — they provide the first impression and must be attention-grabbing but are expected to evolve rapidly. The heart notes (business logic, domain models) are the substantive middle layer that defines the application's character and persists across UI redesigns. The base notes (data models, infrastructure, core algorithms) are heavy molecules that persist for years and anchor everything above them. Architectures that conflate layers — putting base-note logic in the UI or top-note volatility in the data model — produce the software equivalent of a perfume that smells like pure fixative on first spray.

name: Erratic Displacement Theory of Diasporic Identity
core_claim: Diasporic communities are cultural erratics: geological foreigners deposited by historical glaciation events into landscapes where they are compositionally alien. Like glacial erratics, diasporic cultures develop a dual foreignness, becoming too transformed for their origin and too distinct for their location, making them permanent epistemic anomalies that reveal the movement of historical forces otherwise invisible.

name: Prosodic Boundary Encoding in Architectural Wayfinding
core_claim: Speakers encode syntactic structure through prosodic boundaries -- pitch resets, pauses, and syllable lengthening that chunk continuous speech into parseable units. Buildings can encode circulation hierarchy through analogous 'architectural prosody': ceiling height changes, material transitions, and spatial compressions at corridor junctions serve as boundary markers that chunk continuous movement paths into cognitively parseable segments. Buildings designed with explicit prosodic boundary markers at syntactic joints (where circulation hierarchy changes) show measurably better wayfinding performance than buildings that rely solely on signage, because users parse the spatial structure using the same boundary-detection mechanisms they use for language.

name: Network Hormesis
core_claim: Small, controlled disruptions to network topology — random node removals, temporary link failures — can paradoxically strengthen a network's long-term robustness by triggering adaptive rewiring that eliminates single points of failure. Below a critical disruption dose, the network reorganizes into a more resilient configuration with greater path redundancy; above it, the network fragments. This is hormesis applied to network architecture: moderate stress improves fitness, excessive stress destroys it. The framework predicts an optimal disruption schedule for infrastructure and organizational networks.

name: Acoustic Impedance Theory of Cross-Cultural Communication
core_claim: Information transfer between cultural communities fails not primarily because of content differences but because of impedance mismatch — the structural properties of how each culture packages and processes information (speed, density, emotional loading, implicit vs explicit coding) differ enough to cause most communicative energy to reflect rather than transmit at the interface. Effective cross-cultural mediators function as impedance-matching transformers: they do not translate content but restructure the wave characteristics of the message to match the receiving medium. Maximum information transfer occurs when the mediator's impedance is the geometric mean of the two cultures' impedances.

name: Liturgical Load Paths
core_claim: Structural load path analysis — where loads follow the stiffest continuous path to ground — explains how doctrinal authority propagates through religious institutional hierarchies. Theological claims (loads) are transmitted through the 'stiffest' available path: the most institutionally rigid and credentialed chain of authorities. When a link in this chain fails (a bishop defects, a seminary closes), the load redistributes to adjacent paths, sometimes overloading them. Schisms occur when no continuous load path to the foundational authority (ground) remains, and the structure must find a new foundation or collapse. Reform movements function as structural retrofits that create redundant load paths.

name: Tobler's Law of Emotional Proximity
core_claim: Tobler's First Law of Geography — 'everything is related to everything else, but near things are more related than distant things' — when transplanted from spatial attributes to psychological space, produces a quantitative theory of emotional contagion and social influence decay. Emotional states exhibit spatial autocorrelation in 'psychosocial space' (a metric space defined by interaction frequency, relational intimacy, and identity overlap). Mood, motivation, and belief intensity follow distance-decay functions in this space, with Moran's I measuring the clustering coefficient of emotional states within social networks. This predicts that interventions targeting emotionally central individuals (low average psychosocial distance to others) will produce maximum population-level mood shift — a principle for designing mental health interventions at community scale.

name: Autocatalytic Set Theory of Legal Precedent
core_claim: Stable legal doctrines function as autocatalytic sets: each ruling catalyzes the viability of other rulings in the network, and the set is collectively self-sustaining even though no single precedent can justify itself in isolation. Doctrinal collapse occurs not when one precedent is overturned but when enough are removed to break catalytic closure — the remaining rulings can no longer mutually sustain each other. This explains why overturning a seemingly minor case can trigger doctrinal cascade failure.

name: Gene Regulatory Network Model of Belief System Dynamics
core_claim: An individual's belief system is a regulatory network where beliefs activate and suppress each other like genes in a regulatory circuit. Stable worldviews correspond to network attractors — self-reinforcing patterns of belief co-expression. Conversion experiences are transitions between attractor basins, requiring perturbation strong enough to push the system past a separatrix. The number of stable worldviews available to a culture is determined by network topology, not by the number of individual beliefs, just as the number of cell types is determined by the gene regulatory network, not the gene count.

name: Galvanic Corrosion Theory of Ocean Acidification Triage
core_claim: In multi-species calcifying communities under ocean acidification, the most vulnerable species is sacrificially dissolved to buffer local carbonate chemistry, protecting adjacent species with higher dissolution resistance -- exactly as the anodic metal in a galvanic couple corrodes preferentially to protect the cathodic partner. The 'electrolyte' is the shared seawater; the 'potential difference' is the differential between species' aragonite saturation thresholds. Community-level calcification persistence depends not on individual species tolerance but on the galvanic hierarchy of dissolution potentials, and losing the sacrificial species removes protection for the next-most-vulnerable, triggering a corrosion cascade.

name: Shannon Entropy Weathering Index
core_claim: The degree of rock weathering at a geological surface can be quantified as the Shannon entropy of its mineral grain size distribution. Fresh unweathered rock has low entropy (dominated by few crystal size classes), while deeply weathered rock approaches maximum entropy as diverse chemical and mechanical processes fragment grains into a uniform distribution across size classes. This entropy measure provides a single scalar index that integrates all weathering processes without requiring identification of individual mechanisms, and its rate of increase with depth follows channel-capacity-like bounds set by local climate and lithology.

name: Bullwhip Effect in Liturgical Renewal
core_claim: Liturgical reform in hierarchical religious institutions amplifies variance at each echelon of implementation, identical to the bullwhip effect in supply chains. A modest doctrinal signal from the central authority (e.g., Vatican II's 'noble simplicity') is interpreted with increasing variance by each downstream node — national bishops' conferences, diocesan liturgical offices, parish priests, volunteer music directors — until the on-the-ground implementation bears little resemblance to the original signal. Each echelon adds its own forecast error (what do they really mean?) and safety stock (better go further than intended to show compliance). The result is liturgical inventory oscillation: overcorrection followed by backlash, with period and amplitude increasing at greater distance from the source signal.

name: Magma Chamber Theory of Revolutionary Movements
core_claim: Social revolutions follow magma chamber dynamics: grievances accumulate as slow recharge into a crustal reservoir of collective discontent, crystallization of moderate reforms reduces the volatile fraction, and eruption occurs only when the internal pressure of unaddressed demands exceeds the lithostatic threshold set by state repressive capacity. The eruption style — effusive reform vs. explosive revolution — is determined by the volatile content (ideological radicalism) and the viscosity (social cohesion) of the accumulated discontent, not by any single triggering event.

name: Creep Rupture Theory of Organizational Burnout
core_claim: Organizational burnout follows Larson-Miller creep rupture dynamics: sustained moderate stress (workload below yield strength) causes progressive deformation over time through dislocation climb and grain boundary sliding at the individual level. The Larson-Miller parameter — combining stress duration and intensity into a single predictive variable — explains why short bursts of extreme work and long periods of moderate overwork produce equivalent failure. Organizations can predict collective burnout thresholds by plotting their stress-temperature-time trajectories against known rupture curves.

name: Sediment Transport Threshold Theory of Social Movement Activation
core_claim: Social movements activate when the applied shear stress of collective grievance exceeds the Shields parameter threshold for the 'sediment' of potential participants. Larger, heavier 'grains' (risk-averse individuals with more to lose) require higher shear stress to entrain, while fine particles (precarious individuals) mobilize first at lower thresholds. The movement's composition at any given stress level is therefore predictable from the grain-size distribution of the population, and selective transport explains why early-stage movements are demographically unrepresentative of the broader grievance pool.

name: Permaculture Zone Gradient Theory of Cognitive Resource Allocation
core_claim: Human cognitive resource allocation follows permaculture zone design: Zone 0 (self-awareness, inner monologue) receives the most frequent attention visits; Zone 1 (immediate tasks, close relationships) receives daily intensive management; Zone 2 (professional skills, community) receives regular but less frequent tending; and Zones 3-5 (distant interests, abstract knowledge, the unconscious) receive progressively less active intervention. Burnout occurs when Zone 3-5 demands are misclassified as Zone 1 activities, collapsing the gradient and requiring unsustainable attention-energy expenditure.

name: Lava Flow Channelization Theory of Bureaucratic Process Formation
core_claim: Bureaucratic processes self-organize through lava flow channelization: initially, administrative activity spreads as a broad sheet flow across the organizational landscape. As the flow cools (routinizes), levees form at the margins — hardened procedural boundaries that constrain future flow into established channels. Over time, the process becomes a lava tube — a rigid, self-insulating conduit that efficiently transports decisions from input to output but is completely resistant to lateral movement. Breakouts (workarounds) occur only when internal pressure exceeds levee strength, and they immediately begin forming their own channels.

name: Quorum Sensing Theory of Numismatic Debasement Crises
core_claim: Coin users tolerate debasement individually until a quorum threshold is reached: once the density of people who have personally detected debased coins exceeds a critical fraction, a collective behavioral switch activates — hoarding, metal testing, premium demands — that transforms gradual debasement into an acute monetary crisis. The crisis is not triggered by the degree of debasement but by the social density of awareness, exactly as bacterial virulence factors activate only above a quorum-sensing autoinducer threshold.

name: Tannin Polymerization Theory of Legal Precedent Hardening
core_claim: Legal precedents undergo tannin-like polymerization over time: individual rulings (monomers) cross-link through citation and interpretation into progressively larger doctrinal polymers that become more rigid, less soluble in new factual contexts, and increasingly resistant to judicial modification. Early in the polymerization, precedents are astringent but flexible; late-stage doctrines are smooth but brittle. The process is irreversible under normal conditions — only the extreme heat of legislative override or constitutional amendment can depolymerize a fully hardened doctrine.

name: Bathtub Curve Theory of Romantic Relationship Failure
core_claim: Romantic relationships fail at rates that follow the bathtub curve: high early failure rates (infant mortality — incompatibility discovered in the first months) decline to a low plateau of random failures (the stable middle years), then rise again as wear-out failures accumulate (accumulated resentments, divergent life trajectories, empty-nest decoherence). Interventions targeting different life-phases require different strategies — screening for infant mortality, resilience-building for random failures, and preventive maintenance for wear-out — and applying the wrong strategy to the wrong phase is counter-productive.

name: Pheromone-Trail Theory of Archival Discoverability
core_claim: Finding aids in archival systems gain discoverability through a mechanism analogous to ant colony pheromone trails: each researcher access event deposits a 'citation pheromone' that evaporates over time, but frequently accessed collections accumulate reinforced trails that guide future researchers toward high-value materials while neglected collections fade into obscurity. The decay rate of these access signals determines whether archival discovery converges on optimal paths or locks into suboptimal popularity cascades.

name: Attention Mechanism Theory of Forensic Pattern Detection
core_claim: Forensic accounting anomaly detection should be restructured as a transformer-style attention mechanism: each financial transaction generates query, key, and value vectors encoding its temporal, relational, and magnitude properties. The softmax attention score between any two transactions quantifies their 'suspicious similarity,' and transactions that attend strongly to each other despite being in different accounts, time periods, or entities form the high-attention clusters that correspond to fraud rings, round-tripping schemes, or duplicate payment networks.

name: Enological Terroir Theory of Game-Theoretic Equilibria
core_claim: Nash equilibria in repeated games are not universal constants but terroir-dependent: the same game structure produces different stable equilibria depending on the 'microbiome' of cultural norms, institutional history, and player experience that constitute the game's social terroir. Just as identical grape varieties produce radically different wines across regions due to site-specific microbial communities, identical payoff matrices produce different behavioral equilibria across populations. Attempts to transplant institutional designs across cultures fail for the same reason monoculture vineyards lose complexity -- they strip the terroir that selected for the local equilibrium.

name: Bullwhip Effect Theory of Emotional Contagion in Organizations
core_claim: Emotional signals amplify through organizational hierarchies exactly as demand signals amplify through supply chains: a mild expression of concern by a CEO is interpreted as serious worry by VPs, as alarm by directors, and as crisis by front-line managers. Each echelon adds its own forecast error (projection of intent onto ambiguous signals) and safety stock (defensive overreaction), producing emotional bullwhip oscillations where the front line experiences wild swings between panic and complacency while the C-suite perceives only gentle modulation. Information delay (slow communication channels) and batch processing (weekly all-hands meetings) worsen the amplification.

name: ADME Pharmacokinetics Theory of Information Lifecycle in Organizations
core_claim: Information in organizations follows ADME pharmacokinetics: Absorption (how information enters through sensing mechanisms), Distribution (how it spreads across departments and hierarchies via communication channels), Metabolism (how it is transformed into actionable knowledge through meetings, analysis, and synthesis), and Excretion (how outdated information is purged from institutional memory). The 'bioavailability' of a piece of information -- the fraction that actually reaches decision-makers in usable form -- is typically below 20%, and the 'therapeutic window' is narrow: too little information produces uninformed decisions while too much produces analysis paralysis. First-pass metabolism by middle management is the primary site of information loss.

name: Minsky Financial Instability Theory of Ecosystem Resilience
core_claim: Ecosystems undergo Minsky cycles: stable environmental conditions breed ecological risk-taking as species evolve increasingly specialized, interconnected, and efficiency-optimized strategies (the ecological equivalent of moving from hedge to speculative to Ponzi finance). Each generation of stability reduces redundancy and increases dependence on continued stability. When perturbation finally arrives, the ecosystem's fragility is inversely proportional to the duration of the preceding stability. This framework explains why ecosystems with regular moderate disturbance (fire-adapted forests, flood plains) are more resilient than those in prolonged stable environments (deep ocean communities, old-growth in stable climates) -- the former never reach the Ponzi phase.

name: Locard Exchange Theory of Software Debugging
core_claim: Every interaction between a developer and a codebase leaves a bidirectional trace: the developer changes the code (intended), but the code also changes the developer's mental model (often unnoticed). Bugs persist when the developer's mental-model traces diverge from the code's actual state traces. Effective debugging requires forensic reconstruction of both trace directions, not just code inspection.

name: Red Queen Theory of Fashion Cycles
core_claim: Fashion trends follow Red Queen co-evolutionary dynamics: mainstream consumers (hosts) constantly adopt new styles to signal status, while mass-market producers (parasites) track and copy the most common styles. Rare, avant-garde styles enjoy temporary immunity from copying, driving fashion innovators to continuously generate novelty not for aesthetic reasons but to escape the parasite's frequency-dependent tracking. The rate of fashion cycling is set by the copying industry's latency, not by creative inspiration.

name: Map Projection Distortion Theory of Legal Translation
core_claim: Translating legal concepts between jurisdictions is a cartographic projection problem: the curved surface of one legal system cannot be flattened onto another without introducing systematic distortion. A translation can preserve local 'angles' (doctrinal relationships — conformal projection) or 'areas' (scope of rights — equal-area projection) but never both simultaneously. The Tissot indicatrix of legal translation reveals that private law concepts project with moderate distortion while constitutional principles suffer extreme stretching at the periphery, precisely where they matter most for human rights.

name: Pyrotechnic Color Chemistry Theory of Brand Identity
core_claim: Brand identity operates through the same spectral purity constraints as pyrotechnic color chemistry: brands that emit a pure, narrow-band signal (clear positioning, consistent messaging) are perceived as vivid and memorable, while brands attempting to emit across the full spectrum (everything to everyone) produce muddy, desaturated impressions. Contaminant ions (off-brand partnerships, inconsistent campaigns) broaden the emission spectrum and degrade color purity. The sodium-yellow problem — sodium's dominant emission line overwhelms all other colors — explains why price competition (the sodium of branding) drowns out subtler value propositions unless physically separated from the formulation.

name: Acoustic Impedance Matching Theory of Technology Adoption
core_claim: Technology adoption fails at impedance mismatches: when the 'impedance' of a new technology (complexity, cost, cognitive load) differs sharply from the impedance of the adopting environment (existing skills, infrastructure, expectations), most of the innovation's energy is reflected back rather than transmitted into practice. The characteristic impedance of an adopter group is the product of its information density (cultural 'bulk modulus') and its rate of change tolerance (social 'density'). Quarter-wave transformers — intermediary technologies, training programs, or simplified interfaces that gradually step between impedances — maximize transmission of innovation energy across the boundary.

name: Nectar Flow Foraging Theory of Venture Capital Allocation
core_claim: Venture capital allocation follows honeybee nectar flow foraging optimization: scout investors identify promising sectors (nectar sources) and recruit fellow investors through waggle-dance-equivalent signals (pitch meetings, market reports, conference presentations). The colony allocates foragers (capital) proportional to the reported quality of each source, creating a distributed optimization system that tracks shifting opportunities without central planning. Herding behavior in VC is the pathological case where all scouts report the same source (AI, crypto, cleantech in sequence), producing over-recruitment to a single flower patch that depletes before the colony can redirect.

name: Fixative-Mediated Evaporation Theory of Cultural Memory
core_claim: Cultural memories persist differentially based on their 'volatility' and the strength of available 'fixatives.' Light, volatile memories (fashions, slang, pop culture moments) evaporate within a generation unless fixed by institutional preservatives (museums, curricula, media archives). Heavy base-note memories (founding myths, religious narratives, ancestral trauma) persist without fixation due to their inherent molecular weight (emotional and identity significance). The perfumer's fixative mechanism — heavy molecules that physically retard the evaporation of lighter ones — maps to the way canonical works anchor ephemeral references: Shakespeare fixes Elizabethan slang; the Bible fixes ancient Near Eastern customs that would otherwise have evaporated millennia ago.

name: Fretting Fatigue Theory of Long-Term Relationships
core_claim: Long-term relationship deterioration follows fretting fatigue mechanics: small-amplitude oscillatory friction (daily minor irritations, repeated micro-conflicts) at the contact surface between two intimately coupled individuals nucleates fatigue cracks that propagate far more destructively than equivalent stress applied in a single large event. The fretting fatigue explanation resolves the paradox of relationships that survive major crises (high-cycle fatigue with rest periods) but fail under the cumulative weight of trivial daily friction (fretting with no relief). The oxide debris generated by fretting (accumulated resentment) acts as an abrasive third body that accelerates crack growth, explaining why unaddressed minor grievances become self-amplifying.

name: Stigmergic Network Convergence Theory
core_claim: Optimal transport networks can emerge without centralized planning through a reinforce-or-decay feedback loop operating on flow volume, and such networks converge toward mathematically similar topologies regardless of whether the substrate is biological or infrastructural. The key requirement is that the medium itself carries the signal — making the network its own optimization algorithm.

name: Quorum Pricing
core_claim: Markets exhibit quorum-sensing-like behavior: individual firms do not adjust prices based solely on their own cost signals but instead respond to the aggregate density of competitor price changes in their local market. Below a threshold density of price-movers, firms hold steady (analogous to bacterial quiescence); above it, a coordinated repricing cascade sweeps the sector. This reframes price stickiness not as irrational friction but as a rational wait-for-quorum strategy that conserves adjustment costs until collective action is likely.

name: Dislocation Theory of Institutional Reform
core_claim: Institutions deform under political stress not through uniform yielding but through the propagation of discrete reform dislocations — minimal structural rearrangements that move through the institutional lattice one bond at a time. Like crystal dislocations, each reform step requires far less energy than would be needed to shear the entire institution simultaneously, explaining how massive institutional changes accumulate from individually minor procedural adjustments. Reform hardening occurs as dislocations pile up at institutional grain boundaries (departmental borders, jurisdictional lines), and sustained reform requires periodic 'annealing' — deliberate structural relaxation that clears dislocation tangles.

name: Quasispecies Jurisprudence
core_claim: Eigen's quasispecies theory — where selection in high-mutation-rate systems acts on a cloud of mutant variants rather than individual sequences — reframes how legal doctrines evolve under common law. A legal doctrine is not a single rule but a quasispecies: a cloud of varying judicial interpretations centered on a consensus reading. Selection pressure (appeals court rulings, legislative response) acts on the distribution of interpretations, not on any single holding. The 'error threshold' concept explains doctrinal collapse: when lower courts vary interpretations beyond a critical rate, the doctrine loses coherent identity and must be replaced by statute or landmark ruling — a legal analogue to the error catastrophe.

name: HPA Axis Model of Economic Stress Response
core_claim: National economies respond to shocks through a three-tier negative-feedback loop structurally isomorphic to the HPA hormonal axis. The initial alarm (market panic) triggers a hypothalamic signal (central bank communication), which activates a pituitary relay (interbank lending rate adjustment), which drives a cortisol-like effector (broad monetary policy). Negative feedback — economic stabilization suppressing further intervention — normally restores baseline. Chronic economic stress, however, downregulates receptors (market participants become desensitized to policy signals), producing a persistent elevated-intervention state analogous to adrenal fatigue.

name: Zone Refining of Rhythmic Entrainment
core_claim: Musical ensemble rehearsal achieves timing purity through a process analogous to zone refining in metallurgy. A narrow 'molten zone' of focused attention sweeps through a piece passage by passage, and timing impurities (micro-timing deviations) with segregation coefficients less than one are swept toward the boundaries of rehearsed sections, concentrating at transitions. Multiple rehearsal passes progressively purify the interior timing while impurities accumulate at section boundaries, which is why transitions between sections remain the last timing problems solved and require disproportionate rehearsal investment.

name: Structural Holes in Soil Pore Networks
core_claim: Soil fertility hotspots correspond to structural holes in the soil pore network: locations where otherwise disconnected pore clusters are bridged by a single macropore or root channel. These broker pores control the flow of water, dissolved nutrients, and microbial colonizers between isolated microsites, analogous to Burt's network brokers gaining information advantages by bridging disconnected social clusters. Soil organisms that colonize structural-hole pores gain disproportionate access to diverse nutrient streams and become keystone species for local biogeochemical cycling. Tillage practices that destroy macropore bridges eliminate these structural holes and homogenize the pore network, paradoxically reducing nutrient cycling efficiency despite increasing total porosity.

name: Stratigraphic Superposition of Software Architecture
core_claim: Legacy software systems accumulate architectural layers through a process identical to geological deposition: each era's dominant paradigm (structured programming, object-orientation, microservices, serverless) deposits a stratum of code, and the principle of superposition holds — deeper layers are older unless disturbed by refactoring events analogous to tectonic intrusions. The archaeological method of Harris matrix analysis can be applied to codebases: by mapping cutting relations (where newer code interrupts older layers), interfacial units (API boundaries), and depositional contexts (git blame epochs), engineers can reconstruct a site chronology of the system that reveals its true load-bearing strata versus superficial recent deposits, guiding modernization efforts toward the actual foundation layers.

name: Demographic Transition Model of Language Death
core_claim: Endangered languages die through a demographic transition: in Stage 1 (pre-contact), high rates of both language innovation (neologism, dialectal drift) and language loss (forgetting of archaic forms) maintain a stable linguistic population. In Stage 2 (early contact with a dominant language), loss of archaic forms accelerates while innovation continues, producing a net vocabulary expansion through borrowing. In Stage 3 (advanced shift), innovation collapses as young speakers switch to the dominant language, but loss accelerates — the language population crashes. In Stage 4 (post-shift), a tiny stable remnant of ceremonial or heritage speakers persists at low equilibrium. Intervention is most effective at the Stage 2-3 boundary, analogous to family planning programs being most effective during the demographic transition's inflection point. Once Stage 3 is well underway, revitalization requires the linguistic equivalent of pro-natalist policy: artificial incentive structures that reverse the natural adoption of the dominant language.

name: Hormetic Theory of Democratic Stress
core_claim: Democracies exhibit a hormetic dose-response to political crisis: low-dose stressors (contested elections, moderate protest, judicial challenges) trigger adaptive institutional responses that strengthen democratic resilience, but high-dose stressors (coups, civil war, hyperinflation) overwhelm adaptive capacity and cause democratic collapse. The narrow dose window explains the paradox that democracies which have never been tested are more fragile than those that have survived moderate crises, and why deliberately shielding democracies from all stress produces institutional atrophy indistinguishable from the damage caused by excessive stress.

name: Intercropping Complementarity Theory of Research Team Composition
core_claim: Research team productivity follows intercropping resource complementarity: combining disciplines with different 'root architectures' (methodological depths) and 'canopy structures' (theoretical frameworks) allows the team to exploit more of the available intellectual resource space than any monoculture. Overyielding occurs when complementarity (different resource niches) exceeds interference (communication costs), and the Land Equivalent Ratio analog predicts that a well-designed interdisciplinary team of N members can outperform N/LER disciplinary specialists working independently.

name: Paris Law Fatigue Theory of Democratic Norm Erosion
core_claim: Democratic norms erode through Paris Law fatigue crack growth: each violation of a norm (election interference, procedural override, executive overreach) extends the crack by an increment proportional to the stress intensity raised to a power m. Below a threshold stress intensity, cracks do not grow (minor norm violations heal). Above it, each cycle extends the crack predictably, and the crack growth rate accelerates as the remaining ligament of institutional integrity shrinks. The critical crack length — beyond which fracture is sudden and catastrophic — can be estimated from the material toughness of the constitutional order.

name: Alarm Fatigue Theory of Prophetic Tradition Decay
core_claim: Prophetic traditions lose authority through alarm fatigue habituation: the initial prophetic message functions as a critical alarm — urgent, attention-capturing, demanding immediate response. As the tradition institutionalizes, the alarm repeats with increasing frequency (weekly sermons, daily prayers, constant moral exhortation) without corresponding novel threats, producing habituation. The community's response threshold rises until genuinely critical prophetic warnings are indistinguishable from background liturgical noise. Prophetic renewal movements succeed precisely by introducing novel alarm modalities that bypass the habituated channels.

name: Dam Overtopping Cascade Theory of Emotional Breakdown
core_claim: Emotional breakdowns propagate as dam overtopping cascades: psychological compartmentalization creates a series of emotional reservoirs in sequence, each held back by a dam of coping mechanisms. When upstream stress exceeds the first dam's capacity, the overflow does not simply spill — it adds its full volume to the next reservoir, which was already near capacity, triggering a cascade of sequential failures. The catastrophic nature of breakdowns (disproportionate to the triggering event) is explained by the multiplicative amplification through the reservoir chain, not by the magnitude of the final stressor.

name: Common Mycorrhizal Network Theory of Academic Citation
core_claim: Academic citation networks function as common mycorrhizal networks: established researchers (large trees) provide carbon (prestige) to the network in exchange for phosphorus (novel ideas from junior researchers), while the fungal network (journals, conferences, reviewers) extracts a carbon tax for facilitating the exchange. The network preferentially routes resources toward kin-group (same-lab, same-school) connections and can be parasitized by mycoheterotrophic agents — researchers who extract citations without contributing original work.

name: Shields Entrainment Theory of Social Mobility Thresholds
core_claim: Social mobility requires exceeding a Shields-type entrainment threshold: individuals are 'entrained' upward only when the fluid shear stress of economic opportunity exceeds the gravitational and frictional forces of their social sediment — class inertia, network poverty, credential barriers. Larger particles (individuals with more social mass — dependents, debts, community ties) require proportionally greater shear stress to mobilize. The resulting sorting produces graded social strata analogous to sediment sorting in hydraulic channels, with the finest (most mobile) particles transported farthest from their origin.

name: Water Hammer Theory of Market Flash Crashes
core_claim: Financial flash crashes are water hammer events: a sudden valve closure (trading halt, large order cancellation, liquidity withdrawal) sends a pressure transient through the pipeline of interconnected order books at a speed determined by the elasticity of the market medium (depth of limit orders). The pressure wave reflects at pipe junctions (exchange boundaries, dark pool interfaces) and can constructively interfere to produce pressure spikes far exceeding the initial disturbance. The Joukowsky equation analog predicts crash magnitude from the product of flow velocity (trading volume) and the speed of sound in the medium (information propagation speed).

name: Orographic Precipitation Theory of Agroforestry Microclimate
core_claim: Tree rows in agroforestry systems function as miniature mountain ranges that induce orographic effects on field-scale airflow: wind carrying moisture and heat is forced upward over the canopy barrier, cooling adiabatically and depositing dew on the leeward side, while creating a rain shadow of reduced evaporative stress downwind. The height and permeability of the tree row determine whether the 'precipitation' effect (moisture deposition, temperature buffering) or the 'rain shadow' effect (wind acceleration, drying) dominates at each distance from the row.

name: Stress Corrosion Cracking Theory of Digital Format Degradation
core_claim: Digital format obsolescence proceeds not through simple aging but through stress corrosion cracking: the 'stress' of increasing software incompatibility acts synergistically with the 'corrosive environment' of institutional knowledge loss about the format's internal structure. Neither factor alone would cause failure — files remain bitwise intact and specifications remain theoretically available — but their combination produces crack nucleation at the format's most complex features (macros, embedded objects, custom metadata), which propagate until the file becomes functionally unreadable despite being physically preserved.

name: PID Control Theory of Emotional Regulation
core_claim: Mature emotional regulation operates as a PID controller: the proportional term responds to the current magnitude of emotional disturbance, the integral term accumulates unresolved emotional residue over time (preventing chronic low-grade distress from being ignored), and the derivative term anticipates emotional trajectory (enabling preemptive coping). Psychopathology corresponds to mistuned gains: anxiety disorders have excessive derivative gain (overreacting to predicted threats), depression has excessive integral gain (accumulated unresolved distress dominates), and borderline personality features reflect excessive proportional gain with insufficient integral and derivative damping.

name: Comparative Advantage Theory of Neural Specialization
core_claim: Cortical regions specialize according to Ricardian comparative advantage rather than absolute advantage: a region does not need to be the best at processing a particular input type -- it only needs to have the lowest opportunity cost. This explains why brain areas that seem 'suboptimal' for their assigned function (e.g., the fusiform face area processing faces despite not having the highest spatial resolution) persist: they specialize because their opportunity cost of face processing is lower than that of regions with higher absolute capability but greater demand for their processing in other domains. Lesion-induced reorganization follows the same logic, with surviving regions re-specializing based on updated comparative advantages.

name: Red Queen Theory of Pedagogical Method Evolution
core_claim: Teaching methods and student resistance strategies co-evolve in a Red Queen arms race: effective pedagogies (active learning, flipped classrooms) initially produce learning gains because students have not yet evolved countermeasures. But students rapidly develop 'resistance genotypes' -- strategic surface compliance, grade-optimized shortcuts, and social loafing techniques -- that neutralize each pedagogical innovation. The most effective long-term teaching strategy is not any single method but the maintenance of methodological diversity, which prevents any student resistance strategy from becoming dominant. Static pedagogy is the equivalent of a clonal host population -- maximally vulnerable to adapted parasitic strategies.

name: Stimulated Emission Theory of Charismatic Leadership
core_claim: Charismatic leadership operates through stimulated emission: a leader in a 'population-inverted' organization (one where the majority of members are in an excited, frustrated, or aspirational state above their ground state) can trigger coherent, phase-locked collective action by emitting a signal at the resonant frequency of the group's latent energy. The resulting 'laser' of coordinated movement is qualitatively different from the spontaneous emission of uncoordinated individual actions. The optical cavity corresponds to the organizational boundary that reflects the leader's signal back through the population, amplifying it on each pass. Without population inversion (genuine collective frustration or aspiration), the same leadership signal produces only incoherent fluorescence -- diffuse enthusiasm that dissipates without directionality.

name: HPA Axis Theory of Organizational Burnout Cycles
core_claim: Organizations under sustained competitive stress undergo a hormonal feedback loop analogous to the HPA axis: initial stress triggers a cortisol-like 'urgency response' (overtime mandates, process shortcuts, hiring freezes) that temporarily boosts performance. Chronic stress causes receptor downregulation (employee desensitization to urgency signals), eliminating the negative feedback loop and producing a pathological state of permanent crisis mode. The resulting organizational allostatic load manifests as turnover, quality collapse, and institutional exhaustion.

name: Waggle Dance Theory of Scientific Citation
core_claim: Scientific citations function as waggle dances: the citing paper encodes the direction (intellectual vector) and distance (relevance) to a productive resource (the cited paper) for other forager-researchers. High-impact papers are rich nectar sources that elicit vigorous dances (extensive citation with methodological detail), while perfunctory citations are weak dances that recruit few followers. Citation cartels are the equivalent of bees dancing for depleted sources — they misdirect collective foraging effort. The round dance / waggle dance threshold maps to the near-field / far-field distinction in interdisciplinary vs. intradisciplinary citation.

name: Cartographic Generalization Theory of Memory Consolidation
core_claim: Sleep-dependent memory consolidation is cartographic generalization: the hippocampus produces a detailed 'large-scale map' of daily experience that is progressively generalized during slow-wave sleep into the 'small-scale maps' stored in neocortex. Generalization preserves spatial relationships (causal and temporal structure) and legibility (retrievability) at the target scale (long-term utility) while eliminating features below the resolution threshold (irrelevant perceptual detail). The Douglas-Peucker algorithm of memory determines which experiential vertices to retain: points of maximum deviation from the smooth narrative arc survive; intermediate points are discarded.

name: Rootstock-Scion Theory of Institutional Mergers
core_claim: Corporate mergers succeed or fail based on rootstock-scion compatibility: the acquiring company (rootstock) provides the resource-extraction infrastructure (capital, distribution, regulatory relationships) while the acquired company (scion) provides the productive canopy (products, talent, innovation). Vascular continuity — the alignment of communication channels, data systems, and cultural norms across the graft union — determines whether the combined organism thrives or suffers graft incompatibility (immune rejection of the acquired culture). The most successful mergers use interstock (integration teams) to bridge incompatible rootstock-scion combinations.

name: Isochronism Theory of Therapeutic Dosing Intervals
core_claim: The ideal drug dosing interval exhibits isochronism: the therapeutic effect should be independent of the amplitude (dose variation) over a range of physiological conditions, just as a well-designed pendulum's period is independent of its swing amplitude. Drugs with high isochronism (wide therapeutic windows, first-order elimination kinetics) maintain consistent effect duration regardless of moderate dose variations due to absorption differences, body weight, or metabolic rate. Drug delivery systems — extended-release formulations, transdermal patches — are pharmaceutical escapements designed to impose isochronism on inherently non-isochronous drug kinetics.

name: Marionette String Coupling Theory of Central Banking
core_claim: Central bank policy operates through marionette string coupling: each policy instrument (interest rate, reserve requirements, quantitative easing) is a string attached to a control bar, and the real economy is the puppet. The coupling matrix — the relationship between bar movements (policy changes) and puppet limb responses (economic variables) — is non-diagonal: pulling one string moves multiple limbs, and moving one limb cleanly requires coordinated pulls on multiple strings. The fundamental puppetry challenge applies: the number of independently controllable economic variables cannot exceed the number of independent policy strings, and tangled strings (interacting policy channels) reduce effective control dimensionality below the nominal count.

name: Signature Imposition Theory of Curriculum Design
core_claim: Curriculum design is a bookbinding imposition problem: individual learning modules (pages) must be arranged into signatures (course units) that, when folded (delivered sequentially), produce the correct page order (knowledge progression) with minimal waste (redundant content) and even bulk (balanced workload). Misimposition — placing a topic in the wrong signature — means students encounter concepts before their prerequisites or after their relevance, producing a 'misbound' education that is locally comprehensible but globally incoherent. The optimal curriculum minimizes the number of signatures (course boundaries) while keeping each signature thin enough to fold cleanly (cognitively manageable unit size).

name: Intertidal Zonation Theory of Social Media Platform Stacking
core_claim: Social media platforms occupy distinct zones along a gradient of content exposure intensity, analogous to intertidal zonation. The splash zone (anonymous forums, 4chan) hosts extremophile content creators adapted to maximal turbulence and minimal moderation. The high intertidal (Twitter/X, Reddit) supports organisms tolerant of periodic waves of viral attention and desiccating controversy. The mid intertidal (Instagram, TikTok) is the zone of maximum diversity, where the richest community of content types coexist. The low intertidal (LinkedIn, professional Slack) is submerged in the calm of professional norms. Each platform's content moderation policy acts as the tide level, and creators that attempt to live outside their adapted zone suffer the ecological equivalent of thermal stress and predation.

name: Hostile Medium Preservation Paradox
core_claim: Cultural archives function as institutional peat bogs: they preserve cultural artifacts by creating conditions hostile to the living culture that produced them — decontextualizing, dehydrating practice into artifact, and suspending organic cultural processes in institutional anoxia. The paradox is structural: the very conditions that enable preservation are antagonistic to the living use, mutation, and communal ownership that defined the culture being preserved.

name: Syntactic Prediction Gradient in Epidemiological Forecasting
core_claim: The neurolinguistic mechanism by which the brain maintains competing syntactic parse trees and prunes them via surprisal-driven reanalysis maps onto how epidemiological models should handle competing transmission hypotheses during an emerging outbreak. Just as a garden-path sentence forces costly reanalysis when initial syntactic predictions fail, an epidemic surveillance system that commits too early to one transmission model (airborne vs. fomite vs. vector) incurs high 'reanalysis cost' when anomalous data forces model revision. Optimal outbreak response maintains multiple competing parse trees of transmission dynamics, pruning via Bayesian surprisal from incoming case data.

name: Warp Tension Equilibrium Jurisprudence
core_claim: Legal doctrines within a jurisdiction behave like warp yarns under tension on a loom: each doctrine is held taut by constitutional anchors at both ends, and new case law (the weft) interlaces through them, redistributing stress across the entire fabric. Doctrinal coherence emerges not from any single thread's strength but from the collective crimp geometry — the way each doctrine bends around its neighbors. When a court overrules a precedent, it is severing a warp thread, and the resulting tension redistribution can propagate structural distortion far from the original cut, explaining why narrow rulings sometimes cascade into wholesale doctrinal realignment.

name: Choreographic Stigmergy
core_claim: Improvisational dance ensembles coordinate without verbal communication through stigmergic traces left in shared space-time: a dancer's movement through a stage region leaves a spatial-kinesthetic residue (wake turbulence, visual afterimage, rhythmic pattern) that subsequent dancers read and respond to. The stage floor becomes a medium of indirect communication analogous to pheromone trails. This framework explains how contact improvisation groups achieve coherent emergent structures without choreographic authority and predicts that ensemble coherence scales with the persistence time of these movement traces.

name: Quasispecies Theory of Startup Ecosystems
core_claim: Startup ecosystems function as quasispecies: the unit of competitive selection is not the individual company but the mutant cloud of related ventures exploring adjacent business models simultaneously. An ecosystem's fitness is determined by the mean viability of this swarm, not by any single firm's performance. There exists an 'error threshold' in startup pivoting: when the rate of business model mutation exceeds the inverse of the ecosystem's complexity, the swarm loses coherence and dissolves into random noise. Successful venture capital implicitly manages mutation rate to stay just below this threshold, maximizing exploration without crossing into incoherence.

name: Magma Chamber Theory of Revolutionary Pressure
core_claim: Social revolutions are governed by pressure-vessel dynamics identical to volcanic eruptions: grievances accumulate as recharge flux into a crustal reservoir of discontent, while institutional safety valves (free press, elections, protests) act as volatile exsolution vents. Revolution triggers when accumulated social pressure exceeds the lithostatic threshold of institutional containment — and the eruption style (reformist effusion vs violent explosion) is determined by the viscosity of social structure, which is itself a function of inequality crystallization. The most dangerous configuration is a society with high recharge rate, sealed vents, and high structural viscosity: it guarantees explosive rather than effusive discharge.

name: Choreographic Multiplier Effect
core_claim: The Keynesian multiplier — where a single injection of spending propagates through an economy via marginal propensity to consume — maps onto choreographic ensemble dynamics. A single dancer's movement phrase, when injected into an ensemble operating with high 'propensity to respond' (trained mimetic sensitivity), propagates and amplifies through successive dancer-to-dancer transmissions, producing a total kinetic output that exceeds the initial movement by a factor determined by the ensemble's responsiveness coefficient. Choreographers can tune this multiplier by adjusting rehearsal regimes that govern how readily each dancer absorbs and retransmits movement impulses.

name: Auxin Gradient Economics
core_claim: Plant hormonal signaling via auxin — where concentration gradients across tissues direct growth through polar transport and threshold-dependent cell responses — provides a model for how investment capital shapes regional economic development. Capital, like auxin, flows directionally through institutional channels (banks, venture funds, government programs) and accumulates in specific zones. Above certain concentration thresholds, capital triggers 'economic cell elongation' (business formation, infrastructure growth). The 'gravitropic response' equivalent is how capital flows toward existing economic mass, while policy interventions function as exogenous auxin application — redirecting growth toward underserved regions by artificially establishing concentration gradients.

name: Thermohaline Stratification of Social Trust
core_claim: Social trust in large institutions forms density-driven layers analogous to ocean thermohaline circulation. Surface trust (public opinion) is volatile and responsive to media temperature, while deep trust (institutional legitimacy) is cold, saline, and slow-moving. Trust crises occur when surface disruptions penetrate deep enough to destabilize the density gradient, triggering a turnover event where previously buried distrust rapidly surfaces. Restratification after such events takes decades because deep trust, like deep water, forms only under sustained conditions of low turbulence.

name: Topological Error Correction Choreography
core_claim: Ensemble choreography can be made robust to individual dancer errors by encoding movement phrases across redundant dancer subgroups, analogous to how topological quantum error correction distributes a logical qubit across many physical qubits. When a dancer deviates, the surrounding ensemble's spatial and temporal relationships act as syndrome measurements that allow the audience to perceive the intended pattern despite local faults. The choreographic 'logical phrase' survives as long as the fraction of erring dancers stays below a fault-tolerance threshold determined by ensemble density and spatial redundancy.

name: Nash Equilibrium Soil Horizon Theory
core_claim: Soil horizons stabilize at depths where no single weathering process (chemical dissolution, frost heaving, root penetration, bioturbation) can unilaterally shift the horizon boundary without triggering compensating responses from the others. Each horizon represents a Nash equilibrium of competing pedogenic agents: clay illuviation, organic matter accumulation, and mineral weathering reach mutual best-response configurations under the local climate regime. Disturbing one process (e.g., deforestation removing root penetration) destabilizes the equilibrium and causes horizon boundaries to migrate until a new Nash state is reached, often on decadal timescales.

name: Acoustic Impedance Theory of Pedagogical Transfer
core_claim: Learning transfer between domains fails or succeeds based on impedance matching: each knowledge domain has a characteristic impedance determined by its abstraction density (analogous to medium density) and its procedural fluency demands (analogous to wave velocity). When a learner moves from one domain to another, the fraction of understanding that transmits across the boundary is governed by the impedance mismatch between the two domains. Maximum transfer occurs when impedances are matched — explaining why mathematics transfers well to physics (similar impedance) but poorly to literary criticism (massive mismatch). Effective pedagogy inserts impedance-matching layers (bridging analogies, worked examples in the target domain's idiom) that function like acoustic coupling gels, maximizing the transmitted energy fraction of prior knowledge.

name: Quasispecies Theory of Legal Doctrine
core_claim: Legal doctrines persist not as single canonical interpretations but as quasispecies clouds — distributions of slightly variant readings maintained by the high mutation rate of case-by-case application. Selection acts on the cloud's mean fitness, not on any individual interpretation, explaining why doctrines survive despite internally contradictory precedents. An error threshold exists: when the rate of interpretive mutation (driven by judicial creativity, novel fact patterns, or ideological diversity on courts) exceeds the inverse of the doctrine's logical complexity, the doctrine undergoes an error catastrophe and fragments into incoherent sub-doctrines.

name: Tephra Dispersal Theory of Information Virality
core_claim: Viral content disperses through social media following tephra dispersal sorting: the 'eruption column' (initial post) lofts content fragments to varying altitudes (platform amplification levels), and prevailing 'winds' (algorithmic currents, trending topics) carry fragments of different sizes to different distances. Coarse fragments (long-form analysis) deposit proximally near the source community; fine ash (memes, screenshots, quote-tweets) travels farthest but arrives as decontextualized particles. The isopach map of content deposition predicts which communities receive which grain sizes of the original message.

name: Malolactic Fermentation Theory of Grief Processing
core_claim: Grief processing undergoes a secondary fermentation structurally identical to malolactic conversion in winemaking: the sharp, acidic primary grief (malic acid) is gradually converted by psychological 'bacteria' (social support, meaning-making, ritual) into a softer, more complex sadness (lactic acid). The conversion is not a reduction in emotional content but a biochemical transformation that makes the emotion more bearable and integrated. Grief that fails to undergo this secondary fermentation remains perpetually sharp and unstable, prone to refermentation under stress.

name: Mycorrhizal Succession Filter Theory of Startup Ecosystems
core_claim: Startup ecosystems develop through mycorrhizal succession: early-stage ecosystems are dominated by arbuscular mycorrhizal analogs (angel investors, incubators) that form simple, non-specific connections and support pioneer species (first-time founders). As the ecosystem matures, ectomycorrhizal analogs (specialized VCs, corporate venture arms) develop complex mantles around specific sectors, forming exclusive networks that filter which startups can access resources. Late-succession ecosystems risk mycorrhizal lock-in, where the fungal network's preferences override the host community's actual fitness landscape.

name: Firn Densification Theory of Institutional Memory Consolidation
core_claim: Institutional memory consolidates through firn densification: fresh experiences (snow) are initially porous, loosely packed, and easily rearranged. Through sustained organizational pressure (repeated reference, training programs, cultural reinforcement), the memory compresses through successive stages — firn (documented procedures), then glacial ice (deep institutional culture). The densification rate depends on overburden pressure (organizational emphasis) and temperature (rate of personnel turnover). At the critical close-off depth, air bubbles (alternative interpretations) are permanently sealed, and the memory becomes a rigid, non-renegotiable organizational assumption.

name: Hydraulic Jump Theory of Paradigm Shifts
core_claim: Scientific paradigms flow in a supercritical regime — fast, shallow, and unable to transmit upstream disturbances (anomalies cannot propagate back to revise core assumptions). A paradigm shift is a hydraulic jump: the supercritical flow abruptly transitions to a subcritical regime that is deeper and slower, dissipating enormous intellectual energy as turbulence. The Froude number analog — the ratio of theoretical velocity to the speed of critical reflection — predicts when the jump becomes inevitable.

name: Myofibroblast Tension Theory of Organizational Restructuring Scars
core_claim: Post-restructuring organizations develop scar tissue through myofibroblast-like dynamics: the surviving employees at the wound margin differentiate into hyper-contractile units that pull the organizational edges together with excessive force, producing a rigid scar (over-centralized processes, defensive CYA documentation, trust-deficit protocols) that is mechanically strong but functionally inferior to the original tissue. The scar contracts over time, distorting the surrounding organizational architecture. Without active remodeling (deliberate culture rebuilding), the scar becomes permanent and constrains all future organizational movement.

name: Boid Flocking Theory of Permaculture Guild Assembly
core_claim: Permaculture plant guilds self-assemble through three local rules analogous to Reynolds' boid flocking: separation (allelopathic spacing that prevents root competition), alignment (phenological synchronization so that nitrogen fixers, dynamic accumulators, and ground covers operate in temporal concert), and cohesion (mutual attraction through shared mycorrhizal networks and complementary pest-deterrence signals). No central planner is needed; the guild emerges from local rule-following, and the characteristic guild radius is set by the balance between separation and cohesion forces.

name: Seigniorage Extraction Theory of Platform Data Monetization
core_claim: Digital platforms extract seigniorage from user data exactly as medieval mints extracted seigniorage from coin: they accept raw material (user behavioral data) at face value, stamp it with their proprietary analytics (minting), and return a debased version (ad-supported services) while retaining the difference. The seigniorage margin is invisible to users, who perceive the minted coin (the service) as having face value equal to the raw material surrendered. Platform monopolies mirror monetary sovereignty — the power to define the exchange rate between data and services is the power to extract unlimited seigniorage.

name: Bathtub Curve Theory of Coin Trust Degradation
core_claim: Trust in a monetary token follows a bathtub-shaped failure curve: new currencies experience high 'infant mortality' as users test and reject unfamiliar denominations, followed by a stable plateau of routine acceptance, and finally a wear-out phase where debasement, counterfeiting accumulation, or political instability triggers accelerating trust decay. The duration of the plateau is determined by the seigniorage extraction rate — if it exceeds a threshold, the wear-out phase arrives catastrophically early.

name: Moraine Formation Theory of Paleoclimate Proxy Validation
core_claim: Ice core isotope records should be validated against moraine depositional records using the same signal-processing logic that moraines themselves encode: each moraine is a 'snapshot' of glacial extent at a moment of equilibrium, and the spacing between moraines records the frequency of climate oscillations. By treating moraines as an independent low-pass filter on climate signals and comparing their frequency content with ice core data, paleoclimatologists can identify which oscillations in the isotope record are genuine climate signals and which are artifacts of ice-flow distortion or post-depositional diffusion.

name: Acid Paper Degradation Theory of Volatile Acidity Drift in Wine
core_claim: Volatile acidity accumulation in stored wine follows acid paper degradation kinetics: the autocatalytic production of acetic acid mirrors the acid-catalyzed hydrolysis of cellulose, where the reaction products (acid) accelerate the reaction itself. Below a critical acidity threshold, the degradation is imperceptibly slow; above it, the wine enters a self-accelerating decline where acetic acid production feeds back into further bacterial activity. The 'half-life' of a wine's drinkability can be predicted from its initial VA level and storage temperature using the same Arrhenius-modified degradation curves used for paper preservation planning.

name: Glacial Erratic Theory of Sunk Cost Persistence
core_claim: Sunk cost fallacy persists in organizations through a mechanism analogous to glacial erratic transport: large commitments (projects, strategies, hires) are picked up during periods of institutional momentum and deposited far from their origin context when the momentum recedes. Like boulders stranded on alien lithology, these commitments become landmarks around which subsequent decisions orient, not because they belong in the current landscape but because their sheer mass makes removal costlier than accommodation. The 'till' of accumulated justifications buries the original decision rationale, making the commitment appear native to its current context.

name: Felting Entanglement Theory of Social Movement Consolidation
core_claim: Social movements consolidate through a felting mechanism: initially loose, randomly oriented individual grievances are subjected to the agitation of protest events, the heat of media attention, and the moisture of shared emotional experience. These conditions cause the 'cuticle scales' of individual identity -- directional commitments that normally prevent bonding -- to interlock irreversibly. Once felted, the movement cannot be decomposed back into individual threads without destroying the fabric entirely. This explains why movements that never achieve sufficient agitation remain loose collections of complaints, and why successful movements resist internal disaggregation even when their original goals are met.

name: Lanchester Square Law Theory of Academic Citation Competition
core_claim: Citation competition between research groups follows Lanchester's square law: the citation advantage of a larger research group scales with the square of its publication output, not linearly. This is because each paper in a larger group can cite every other paper in the group (cross-citation), creating a network effect that smaller groups cannot match with quality alone. The implication is that fragmented research communities with many small groups will always lose citation dominance to consolidated groups, even if the aggregate output and quality are identical. This provides a purely structural explanation for the Matthew effect in science independent of actual research quality.

name: Crystal Nucleation Theory of Religious Conversion
core_claim: Religious conversion follows crystal nucleation dynamics: a 'supersaturated' individual -- one carrying more existential uncertainty than their current worldview can dissolve -- experiences random fluctuations of meaning-making that occasionally form embryonic belief clusters. Most such clusters are below the critical radius and dissolve back into doubt. But once a belief cluster exceeds the critical size (a threshold number of interlocking convictions, social connections, and experiential confirmations), it spontaneously grows into a complete faith commitment. The free energy barrier explains why conversion is typically sudden rather than gradual, and why 'seeding' by a charismatic figure or crisis event dramatically lowers the activation energy.

name: Musashi Timing Theory of Market Entry
core_claim: Optimal market entry follows Musashi's theory of timing rather than conventional first-mover advantage logic: success depends not on being first but on seizing the interval the incumbent cannot adapt to. The 'rhythm' of incumbent innovation cycles creates predictable gaps -- moments between product releases, during leadership transitions, or while attention is focused on a different competitive front -- that correspond to Musashi's 'void in the opponent's rhythm.' The entrant who strikes during this void gains initiative not through speed alone but through temporal precision. Fast followers succeed not despite being second but because they attack during the incumbent's recovery phase between rhythm beats.

name: Tobler's First Law Theory of Moral Intuition
core_claim: Moral intuitions follow Tobler's First Law: moral concern for entities 'near' the self (in social, genetic, temporal, and spatial distance) is always stronger than concern for distant entities, and this gradient is not a bias to be corrected but a fundamental structure of moral cognition analogous to spatial autocorrelation. Utilitarian ethics attempts to flatten this gradient into uniform concern, which is as physically implausible as a universe without spatial autocorrelation. The practical implication is that effective moral frameworks must work with the gradient rather than against it, designing institutional structures that telescope local moral concern outward through chains of proximate relationships rather than demanding direct long-range moral connection.
source_b: Trolley Problem / Doctrine of Double Effect

name: Stigmergic Theory of Archival Arrangement
core_claim: Archival collections self-organize through stigmergic traces: each archivist's processing decision (folder labeling, finding aid annotation, cross-reference note) deposits an environmental signal that guides subsequent archivists' decisions, producing emergent organizational coherence without centralized planning. The resulting arrangement reflects not the original creator's logic but the accumulated trace-history of every handler, much as termite mounds reflect pheromone trails rather than any single insect's blueprint.
source_b: Ranganathan's Five Laws of Library Science

name: Immune Clonal Selection Theory of Artistic Style
core_claim: An artist's mature style develops through a process analogous to clonal selection in the immune system: early career experimentation produces diverse stylistic 'lymphocytes,' each representing a different aesthetic approach. When a particular style achieves strong 'antigen binding' (audience resonance, critical recognition, market success), it is clonally expanded through repetition and refinement, while non-resonant styles are eliminated. Somatic hypermutation (deliberate experimentation within the successful style) fine-tunes the artist's signature approach.

name: Island Biogeography Theory of Programming Language Ecosystems
core_claim: Programming language library ecosystems follow island biogeography: language 'islands' closer to the 'mainland' of established languages (sharing syntax, paradigms, or FFI interoperability) have higher immigration rates of libraries and developers, while smaller, more isolated languages reach equilibrium at lower ecosystem richness. The species-area relationship predicts that doubling a language's user base increases library count sublinearly, and that niche languages must compensate for isolation through unique features that attract 'endemic species' (libraries with no equivalent elsewhere).

name: Terroir Expression Theory of Neural Individuation
core_claim: Individual cognitive style emerges through a mechanism analogous to viticultural terroir: the same genetic 'grape variety' (neural architecture) planted in different developmental 'soils' (early sensory environments, attachment patterns, nutritional gradients) produces brains with distinctive and repeatable 'flavor profiles' — not because the environments teach specific content, but because they modulate the micro-chemical gradients during critical periods of synaptic pruning. Cognitive terroir is why identical twins raised apart share temperament but diverge in aesthetic preference, humor, and creative style.

name: Scent Molecule Competition Theory of Attention Markets
core_claim: Digital content competes for human attention through the same receptor-binding kinetics as scent molecules: higher-affinity content (emotionally triggering, personally relevant) masks lower-affinity content at shared cognitive receptors, regardless of the masked content's informational value. Olfactory fatigue has a direct attention analog — prolonged exposure to any content type causes receptor adaptation, explaining the relentless acceleration of novelty cycles. The attention economy is a perfumer's organ: content creators must compose accords from multiple binding modalities (visual, emotional, intellectual) because single-note content fatigues fastest.

name: Phenological Tracking Theory of Product Lifecycle Management
core_claim: Product launches succeed when timed to phenological indicators of market readiness, not calendar dates. Just as vintners track sugar-acid balance across growth stages to determine optimal harvest, product managers must track the ratio of market demand signals (sugar) to competitive saturation (acid) across the product development cycle. Harvesting too early (launching into an immature market) produces thin, acidic products that fail to convert; harvesting too late produces flabby, over-ripe products that lack differentiation. The phenological stages — budbreak (concept validation), flowering (beta), veraison (product-market fit), harvest (launch) — each have measurable indicators that are more reliable than fixed timelines.

name: Scale-Dependent Feature Theory of Historical Periodization
core_claim: Historical periodization is a scale-dependent feature representation problem: events and trends that are salient at one temporal zoom level (decades) disappear or transform at another (centuries, millennia). The 'cartographic rules' of historiography — which events to represent at each scale — are rarely made explicit, producing the illusion that history has natural joints. At high zoom, history is a continuous flow of contingent events; at low zoom, it congeals into 'periods' whose boundaries are artifacts of the generalization algorithm, not properties of the past itself. Historiographical debates about periodization (When did modernity begin?) are arguments about zoom level disguised as arguments about chronology.

name: Spine Flexibility Theory of API Design
core_claim: Well-designed APIs exhibit the spine flexibility of fine bookbinding: they allow 'flat opening' at any page (function call) without cracking (breaking changes) or excessive resistance (boilerplate). APIs with rigid spines (tightly coupled interfaces) resist opening and eventually crack under the stress of evolving requirements. APIs with no spine at all (unstructured data passing) flop open everywhere but cannot hold their shape. The bookbinder's ideal — a spine that is rigid enough to hold its shape on the shelf but flexible enough to lie flat when opened — is the API designer's ideal of convention over configuration: structural when idle, compliant when used.

name: Nectar Foraging Theory of Academic Research Funding
core_claim: Academic research funding ecosystems optimize through nectar foraging dynamics: funding agencies (hive) deploy scouts (program officers) to evaluate research directions (flower patches), and successful scouts recruit foragers (grants) proportional to patch quality (scientific promise). The system self-corrects as depleted patches (exhausted research veins) naturally reduce recruitment through declining publication rates (reduced waggle dance intensity). However, the colony's response to sudden nectar flow collapse (paradigm shift, negative replication results) is dangerously slow — foragers continue visiting depleted patches for cycles after the nectar is gone, wasting colony resources on post-peak fields.

name: Waggle Dance Theory of Startup Hiring
core_claim: Startup hiring networks operate through waggle dance dynamics: current employees who have found the company to be a 'rich nectar source' (rewarding workplace) perform vigorous recruitment dances (referrals, LinkedIn posts, conference talks) that encode direction (company mission, cultural values) and distance (compensation, work-life balance) to potential candidates. The dance's persuasive power is proportional to the dancer's own foraging success (personal satisfaction, equity upside). Employee referral programs formalize the waggle dance, but their effectiveness depends on whether the dancing bees are genuinely reporting rich nectar or performing coerced dances for depleted patches — the biological system's honesty constraint is what most referral bonus schemes fail to replicate.

name: Commitment Erosion Dynamics in Fermentation Process Control
core_claim: In game theory, commitment devices erode as players test the rigidity of binding agreements over successive rounds. In industrial fermentation, process control setpoints (temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen) function as 'commitments' that the microbial community tests over successive generations. Rigid setpoints attract stable microbial cooperation but cannot adapt to drift in culture composition; flexible setpoints enable adaptation but invite opportunistic strain takeover. The commitment erosion model predicts an optimal setpoint adjustment frequency for each fermentation stage -- rigid during exponential growth (when cooperation is critical) and increasingly flexible during stationary phase (when adaptation matters more).

name: Zero-Knowledge Courtroom Protocol
core_claim: Certain legal procedures — particularly trade-secret litigation and classified-evidence hearings — already approximate zero-knowledge proof systems: one party must convince the court of a fact's truth without revealing the fact itself.
