Individual deposits are negligible, but over decades they form massive, immovable stalactites that obstruct the passage (organizational function) they were meant to protect. The stalactite model predicts that rule accumulation is fastest where the drip rate is highest (sectors with frequent incidents or political attention) and that stalactites grow from the ceiling down (top-level regulations producing derivative rules that extend downward through organizational layers). Breaking a stalactite (regulatory reform) is easy compared to the effort of dissolving it back into solution (cultural deregulation), because the mineral has recrystallized into a form more stable than the original dissolved state.

name: Biofouling Succession Theory of Software Dependency Accumulation
core_claim: Software dependency accumulation follows marine biofouling succession: a clean hull (new codebase) first attracts pioneer organisms (small utility libraries — lodash, moment.js) that create a biofilm (dependency layer) enabling secondary colonizers (frameworks that depend on the pioneers) and eventually climax communities (entire platform ecosystems — React + Redux + React Router + testing libraries). Each layer makes the next layer's attachment easier and the hull (codebase) slower, heavier, and harder to clean. The biofouling model predicts that dependency removal (hull cleaning) must be done regularly and completely — partial cleaning actually accelerates recolonization because the remaining biofilm provides better attachment surfaces than clean hull. 'Vendoring' dependencies is the software equivalent of antifouling paint: it doesn't prevent attachment but slows the succession cycle.

name: Phyllotaxis of Market Stall Placement
core_claim: Optimal vendor placement in circular or semi-circular market spaces follows phyllotactic packing: each new vendor should be placed at the position of least competitive inhibition from existing vendors, which converges on the golden angle (~137.5 degrees) of separation in the circular layout. This arrangement maximizes customer exposure per vendor while minimizing same-category clustering, just as leaf primordia maximize light capture. Markets that self-organize through first-come-first-served placement approximate this pattern because vendors intuitively avoid direct competitors, and the inhibitory field radius is set by customer walking tolerance. Deviations from the golden-angle pattern predict zones of vendor failure due to over-competition.

name: Fitts's Law Theory of Theological Argumentation
core_claim: The difficulty of theological arguments follows Fitts's Law: the 'movement time' required to make a persuasive theological claim increases logarithmically with the ratio of the claim's ambition (distance to target) to the precision of available scriptural or experiential evidence (target width). Broad existential claims ('God exists') are easy targets; narrow doctrinal claims ('the filioque is heretical') require exponentially more argumentative precision. Theological disputes persist precisely where the index of difficulty is highest — where claims are ambitious but evidentiary targets are small.

name: Triboelectric Charge Theory of Cultural Polarization
core_claim: Cultural polarization accumulates through triboelectric charge transfer: every contact between ideologically different groups (media exposure, political debates, mixed social spaces) transfers charge — each side becomes more extreme in opposite directions, with the magnitude depending on the groups' positions in the 'triboelectric series' of ideological reactivity. Groups far apart on the series (progressive academics and rural conservatives) generate the most charge per contact. Discharge occurs as sudden, destructive 'sparks' (riots, culture wars), and grounding strategies (bridging institutions, deliberative forums) prevent dangerous charge accumulation.

name: Dansgaard-Oeschger Theory of Fashion Trend Oscillation
core_claim: Fashion trends exhibit Dansgaard-Oeschger-style oscillations: rapid warming events (trend explosions that sweep the industry in a single season) are followed by gradual cooling (slow decline into ubiquity and then unfashionability). The oscillation is driven by a bipolar seesaw between high-fashion and mass-market adoption — when one warms, the other cools — with the abrupt transitions triggered by threshold crossings in cultural saturation, not by designer intent.

name: Stress Corrosion Cracking Theory of Bilingual Language Attrition
core_claim: First-language attrition in bilinguals follows stress corrosion cracking mechanics: the sustained tensile stress of dominant-language immersion, combined with the corrosive environment of reduced L1 input, produces intergranular cracking along the grain boundaries of the L1 system — the weakest morphosyntactic structures fail first, while phonological 'grains' with strong crystallographic orientation resist longest. Neither stress alone (immersion without L1 disuse) nor corrosion alone (L1 disuse without immersion pressure) would produce the characteristic failure pattern; only their synergy cracks the language.

name: Glacial Surge Theory of Malolactic Fermentation Onset
core_claim: Malolactic fermentation onset in wine follows glacial surge dynamics: lactic acid bacteria accumulate slowly at the base of the wine's microbial ecology, building up metabolic 'pressure' beneath a surface layer of chemical inhibition (SO2, low pH). When the inhibitory layer thins past a threshold — through SO2 binding, pH rise, or nutrient availability — the bacterial population surges forward in a rapid, self-reinforcing advance that converts malic acid to lactic acid across the entire volume before decelerating as substrate is exhausted, leaving behind a transformed sensory 'landscape.'

name: Stribeck Curve Theory of Archival Format Migration
core_claim: Digital format migration in archives follows Stribeck curve lubrication regimes: at low migration frequency (boundary regime), each format conversion involves high friction — data loss, metadata corruption, format-specific edge cases. At moderate frequency (mixed regime), institutional memory and tooling reduce losses unpredictably. At high frequency (hydrodynamic regime), continuous migration pipelines create a self-sustaining lubricant layer of format-awareness that minimizes conversion loss. Archives must identify their Stribeck minimum — the optimal migration frequency that minimizes total information loss — rather than defaulting to either extreme.

name: Creep Rupture Theory of Situation Awareness Degradation
core_claim: Situation awareness degradation under sustained cognitive load follows Larson-Miller creep rupture mechanics: the operator's cognitive 'material' deforms slowly under constant stress (workload), accumulating microscopic voids (attention gaps, missed cues) that are individually harmless but collectively reduce the load-bearing cross-section of awareness. The time to rupture (total loss of situation awareness) can be predicted by a Larson-Miller parameter combining stress intensity (task complexity) and temperature (arousal level), explaining why high-arousal operators fail faster under the same workload than low-arousal ones.

name: Mercerization Theory of Institutional Transformation
core_claim: Deep institutional reform follows mercerization dynamics: immersion in a strong reforming agent (crisis, regulatory mandate, new leadership) causes differential swelling of the institution's 'crystalline' structures -- some units convert from rigid hierarchical form (cellulose I) to a more flexible, higher-capacity configuration (cellulose II), gaining 'luster' (public trust) and 'dye affinity' (ability to absorb new mandates). But the transformation is irreversible and must be conducted under tension -- without maintained pressure toward the reform goals, the institution relaxes into a disordered amorphous state rather than the desired crystalline rearrangement. Partial mercerization produces the worst outcome: an institution that is neither traditionally rigid nor flexibly reformed.

name: Feedback Linearization Theory of Cross-Cultural Communication
core_claim: Effective cross-cultural communication requires feedback linearization: the nonlinear dynamics of cultural interpretation (where the same message produces wildly different responses depending on cultural context) must be canceled by an appropriate 'control law' that transforms the communication into a linear, predictable system. A skilled cross-cultural communicator implicitly estimates the nonlinear response function of their interlocutor's culture and applies the inverse transformation to their message, producing the intended linear effect. Communication failures occur when the estimated nonlinearity is incorrect, producing over- or under-compensation that amplifies rather than cancels the cultural distortion.

name: Miura-Ori Auxetic Theory of Economic Resilience
core_claim: Economies that exhibit negative Poisson's ratio behavior — expanding laterally when stretched longitudinally — are structurally resilient in the manner of Miura-ori folds. When stressed along one axis (trade shock, supply chain disruption), auxetic economies widen their engagement across adjacent sectors rather than contracting. This counter-intuitive response emerges from the folded geometry of inter-industry linkages: industries connected in a zigzag pattern of alternating dependencies, rather than aligned in parallel, produce collective expansion under tension. The fold pattern can be deliberately engineered through industrial policy.

name: Paper Grain Direction Theory of Team Composition
core_claim: Teams function optimally when members' cognitive 'grain direction' — the axis along which they think most flexibly — is aligned parallel to the project's spine (primary challenge axis). Cross-grain teams (members whose flexibility axis is perpendicular to the problem) produce work that cockles under stress: individuals resist bending in the required direction, creating internal tensions that warp the output. The bookbinder's rule — always fold with the grain — translates to the management principle that teams should be composed not for diversity of skill alone but for alignment of cognitive flexibility with the project's primary deformation axis.

name: Pin Tumbler Sequence Theory of Cryptographic Key Exchange
core_claim: Secure key exchange protocols can be understood through pin tumbler sequential setting: each step of the Diffie-Hellman or ECDH protocol 'sets' a mathematical pin to its shear line (contributes partial information to the shared secret) in a binding order determined by the protocol's algebraic structure. An eavesdropper who observes the partial settings faces the same challenge as a lockpicker without tactile feedback: they can see that pins are being set but cannot determine the binding order or the correct height of each pin. The security of the exchange rests on the computational analog of manufacturing tolerance — the discrete logarithm problem ensures that the 'tolerance' between correct and incorrect pin heights is computationally indistinguishable.

name: Coral Holobiont Theory of Open-Source Software
core_claim: Open-source software projects are holobionts: the core codebase is the coral skeleton, maintainers are the symbiotic algae providing energy (code review, direction), and the contributor community forms the associated microbiome. Project health depends on holobiont balance — bleaching events (maintainer burnout) expel the symbiotic algae, leaving a skeleton that still exists structurally but can no longer grow. Fork events are fragmentation analogous to coral breakage, where each fragment may or may not recruit new symbionts.

name: Steel Tempering Theory of Resilience Training
core_claim: Psychological resilience is produced by tempering, not by hardening alone: acute stress exposure (quenching) creates a hard but brittle martensite-like psychological state (hypervigilance, rigidity), which must be followed by controlled re-heating (therapeutic processing, gradual re-exposure) that transforms the brittle structure into tough, tempered martensite. PTSD results from quenching without tempering. The optimal tempering temperature (therapeutic intensity) and duration are specific to the individual's 'alloy composition' (genetic predisposition, prior experience), just as different steel grades require different tempering protocols.

name: Spine Flexibility Design Theory of Constitutional Amendments
core_claim: Constitutional design faces the same tradeoff as bookbinding spine flexibility: too rigid a spine (unamendable constitution) cracks under stress and pages (provisions) detach; too flexible a spine (easily amended constitution) allows pages to shift out of alignment, losing structural coherence. The optimal spine stiffness matches the expected usage pattern — constitutions for rapidly changing societies need flexible perfect-binding, while those for stable societies benefit from rigid case-binding. The U.S. Constitution's Article V amendment process is a tight case-binding that has begun to crack at the joints.

name: Sourdough Starter Succession Theory of Online Community Moderation
core_claim: Online communities undergo sourdough starter succession: early colonizers (founding members) establish a microbial culture (community norms) that selects for specific subsequent arrivals. A healthy community, like a stable sourdough, reaches a climax state where lactobacilli (norm-enforcing regulars) and wild yeast (creative contributors) maintain a symbiotic equilibrium. Platform migration events are analogous to 'refreshing' the starter — transferring a portion of the culture to new flour (platform), where it must re-establish dominance over environmental contaminants (trolls, spam) before stabilizing.

name: Puppet Articulation Hierarchy Theory of API Design
core_claim: Well-designed APIs follow puppet articulation hierarchy: the number of independently controllable joints (endpoints) must match the complexity of movements (operations) the puppet (system) needs to perform. Too few joints produces stiff, unconvincing movement (rigid, monolithic endpoints that force clients into awkward workarounds); too many joints produces an uncontrollable tangle of strings (over-granular microservices). The master puppeteer's insight is that joints should be hierarchically organized — the shoulder moves the whole arm, the elbow moves the forearm, the wrist moves the hand — creating a natural cascade of control granularity.

name: Sandcastle Height Scaling Theory of Organizational Growth Limits
core_claim: Organizational growth follows sandcastle height-scaling laws: maximum achievable height scales with the square root of the base footprint, not linearly. Doubling an organization's height (hierarchical depth, market reach) requires quadrupling its base (support infrastructure, operational capacity). Organizations that grow vertically without proportional base expansion produce the sandcastle equivalent of a tall, narrow tower — impressive but critically unstable, prone to catastrophic collapse from lateral forces (competitive pressure, regulatory change) that a wider-based structure would absorb. The scaling exponent is a measurable organizational vital sign.

name: Kintsugi Fragment Reassembly Theory of Post-Conflict Truth and Reconciliation
core_claim: Truth and reconciliation processes are kintsugi fragment reassembly: the shattered society cannot be invisibly restored to its pre-conflict state, but the fragments (communities, institutions, narratives) can be gathered, matched, and rejoined with visible, valued seams (shared acknowledgment of what was broken and how). The gold lacquer (restorative justice) is stronger than the original ceramic — the repaired society has structural properties the original lacked, including visible evidence of its own fragility. The reassembly process requires identifying which fragments belong to the same vessel (shared national identity) and which are fragments of a different object entirely (incompatible narratives that cannot be joined).

name: Perfume Pyramid Sequencing Theory of Architectural Experience Design
core_claim: Architectural experience should be designed as a perfume pyramid: top notes (the building's facade, entrance moment — immediate, volatile, attention-grabbing), heart notes (the main circulation spaces, spatial rhythm — the sustained middle experience that defines character), and base notes (the deep structural logic, material honesty, acoustic quality — the lingering impression that persists in memory after leaving). Most failed architecture over-invests in top notes (spectacular facades) while neglecting base notes (thermal comfort, acoustic intimacy), producing buildings that photograph well but evaporate from memory. The perfumer's discipline of balancing all three temporal layers is the missing design methodology.

name: Numismatic Gresham's Law Theory of Language Death
core_claim: Language death follows Gresham's Law of currency debasement: when two languages circulate in the same community, the 'bad money' (economically dominant language with lower cultural information content per utterance) drives out the 'good money' (minority language with higher cultural specificity per word). Speakers begin hoarding the minority language for intimate and ceremonial contexts (saving good money) while spending the dominant language in daily transactions, until the minority language's circulation drops below the threshold needed to sustain intergenerational transmission. Language revitalization programs are effectively re-monetization efforts — restoring a debased currency to circulation by creating contexts where it is the only legal tender.

name: Helmholtz Resonator Theory of Echo Chamber Formation
core_claim: Online echo chambers are Helmholtz resonators: a cavity (ideological community) with a narrow neck (selective information gateway) that resonates at a specific frequency (ideological wavelength) and amplifies signals at that frequency while filtering out all others. The resonator's key property — that its resonant frequency is determined by the ratio of neck area to cavity volume, not by the content of the signal — explains why echo chambers amplify specific ideas independently of their truth value. The Helmholtz prediction is precise: making the neck narrower (more selective gatekeeping) while expanding the cavity (growing the community) lowers the resonant frequency (makes the chamber responsive to longer-wavelength, more abstract ideological signals), explaining the radicalization trajectory from specific grievances to abstract conspiratorial worldviews.

name: Knit Stitch Topology Theory of Social Safety Net Design
core_claim: Social safety nets should be designed using knit stitch topology: knitted fabric's unique property — that each loop is held in place by its neighbors, creating a structure where any single thread failure is contained locally rather than propagating catastrophically (unlike woven fabric where a broken warp thread unravels an entire column) — maps onto the principle that safety net programs should be interlocking loops (programs that catch beneficiaries falling through other programs) rather than parallel threads (independent programs that each serve different populations without cross-referencing). A knitted safety net where unemployment insurance loops through housing assistance which loops through food assistance which loops back to job training creates a self-stabilizing fabric; a woven safety net where each program operates as an independent column creates single-point-of-failure vulnerability.

name: Scale-Dependent Justice Geometry
core_claim: Sentencing outcomes exhibit fractal self-similarity: the same patterns of disparity at the individual case level reproduce at courtroom, district, circuit, and national scales. Measurement granularity determines perceived fairness just as measurement scale determines coastline length.

name: Loom Networks: Interlace-Pattern Computation
core_claim: Neural network architectures can be reconceived as weave drafts where each layer is a shed, weights are thread tensions, and float/sink decisions are activation gates. Twill-like architectures produce robust generalization; satin-like produce smooth interpolation at the cost of adversarial fragility.

name: Cognitive Erosion Cycle Theory
core_claim: Memory systems evolve through geomorphic stages: youth (steep retrieval gradients), maturity (balanced consolidation/interference), old age (gist peneplain). Spaced repetition functions as tectonic rejuvenation re-elevating traces above the retrieval base level.

name: Volatility Dramaturgy
core_claim: Temporal experiences follow a volatility curve: high-volatility elements hook early but dissipate, medium-volatility carry core identity, low-volatility provide persistent residue determining memorability. Quality depends on coherent transitions between volatility tiers.

name: Quorum Governance Theory
core_claim: Democratic systems function as macro-scale quorum sensing networks where citizen expressions act as political autoinducers. Policy regimes remain dormant until signal density crosses activation thresholds, triggering institutional program execution.

name: Predictive Gauge Theory of Economic Forecasting
core_claim: Economic indicators function like knitting gauge swatches: small standardized samples that predict large-scale outcomes only when the practitioner accounts for their own systematic tension bias and environmental conditions.

name: Zonation Dynamics Theory of Social Ecology
core_claim: Classroom social dynamics follow tide pool zonation: students self-sort into zones based on stress tolerance, with splash-zone students (high engagement, high stress) showing different adaptive strategies than subtidal students (low engagement, protected).

name: Depositional Stratification Theory of Capital
core_claim: Wealth accumulates like stalactite formation: mineral-laden water (income) deposits calcium carbonate (savings) drop by drop, with growth rate determined by solution chemistry (financial literacy), drip rate (income frequency), and cave atmosphere (economic environment).

name: Capillary Capital Theory
core_claim: Organizational cohesion follows granular bonding physics: discrete units cohere through narrative surface tension between present state and projected potential. Cohesion strength is non-monotonic, peaking at optimal external validation, and collapses via liquefaction when binding medium exceeds load-bearing capacity.

name: Thermal Schedule Theory of Learning
core_claim: Neural network training is governed by irreversible phase transitions analogous to ceramic firing, where each transition must complete before the next begins or the model structurally fails. Optimal training requires a precise thermal schedule with controlled heating, sustained holds at critical temperatures, and managed cooling.

name: Thermophilic Integration Theory
core_claim: Transformative human experiences follow composting kinetics: raw psychic material must reach sufficient emotional activation energy to decompose into integrated wisdom. The ratio of affective intensity to cognitive structuring determines whether processing yields fertile integration or toxic residue.

name: Crease-Zone Duality Theory
core_claim: Urban zoning maps and origami crease patterns are isomorphic constraint-satisfaction systems where local fold/zone rules propagate to determine global realizability of 3D form from 2D encodings. A single local change can cascade through the constraint graph to either unlock or collapse the entire structure's feasibility.

name: Affective Convection Theory
core_claim: Interpersonal conflicts operate as thermodynamic convection cells where emotional energy cycles through updraft (escalation) and downdraft (withdrawal) phases. Chronic conflicts function as supercell arguments that sustain themselves when communicative wind shear separates provocation from response.

name: Capillary Cognition Theory
core_claim: Sustained attention operates as a combustion system where motivational interest acts as capillary force, drawing cognitive fuel upward through processing layers until it ignites into conscious experience. Too much fuel smothers the flame, too little starves it, and environmental turbulence disrupts the laminar flow.

name: Refractive Identity Theory
core_claim: Personality assessment works like a prism: a single beam of behavior, when passed through the assessment instrument, separates into component wavelengths (traits). Different instruments (prisms) produce different spectral decompositions of the same behavior, and no single decomposition captures the original beam.

name: Stigmergic Governance Theory
core_claim: Effective large-scale governance emerges not from central planning but from environmental traces left by prior decisions that guide subsequent actors. Democratic deliberation works best when it modifies the environment (laws, infrastructure, norms) rather than attempting to direct behavior.

name: Attention Bioluminescence Theory
core_claim: Viral content creation follows energy-constrained signaling logic: creators convert metabolic resources into attention-photons through catalytic reactions, and the functional category of light produced — confusion, attraction, signaling, or camouflage — determines optimal deployment strategy.

name: Cultural Song Drift Theory
core_claim: Brand identity evolves through the same cultural transmission dynamics as whale song: all members express the same identity which changes gradually, novel approaches sweep through industries in waves, and distinctiveness correlates with competitive success. Brand voice is hierarchical (words to campaigns) just as whale song is hierarchical (notes to songs).

name: Sociomagnetic Excitation Theory
core_claim: Societies possess a layered 'sociomagnetic field' of institutional structures that channels disruptive external stimuli along pre-existing network lines to specific social strata, where each stratum emits a characteristic behavioral 'color' determined not by the stimulus itself but by the socioeconomic position of the excited group. The protective function and the spectacular disruption are the same mechanism: institutions cannot shield without also channeling.

name: Laban Effort Theory of Cryptographic Key Ceremony
core_claim: The security of cryptographic key generation ceremonies depends on the movement quality of the participants, analyzable through Laban effort dimensions. Weight (decisiveness of keystrokes), space (attention to environmental threats — direct vigilance vs. flexible scanning), time (urgency of the ceremony under exposure risk), and flow (bound procedural compliance vs. free improvisation when anomalies arise) define an expressive space of ceremony styles. Ceremonies that score high on bound flow and direct space produce keys with the highest operational security, while those drifting toward free flow and flexible space introduce entropy in the wrong places — not in the key material but in procedural adherence. Training key ceremony officers should therefore include Laban movement analysis to diagnose and correct risky behavioral signatures before they manifest as side-channel vulnerabilities.

name: Stribeck Lubrication Theory of Diplomatic Relations
core_claim: International diplomacy operates across three regimes identical to the Stribeck curve: at low relative velocity (peacetime stasis), boundary lubrication dominates — direct state-to-state contact generates high friction and territorial disputes. As interaction speed increases (trade acceleration, cultural exchange), a mixed regime emerges where institutional lubricants (treaties, international law) partially separate the surfaces. At high velocity (deep economic interdependence), full hydrodynamic lubrication develops — the 'fluid film' of shared institutions completely separates sovereign surfaces, and friction drops to near zero. War occurs when the lubricant film breaks down and the system snaps back to boundary contact.

name: Volatile Acidity Oscillation Theory of Team Morale Dynamics
core_claim: Team morale oscillates through volatile acidity dynamics: acetic acid bacteria (cynics, complainers) convert the ethanol of team enthusiasm into vinegar of dissatisfaction in the presence of oxygen (external scrutiny, management pressure). The oscillation arises because high morale (high ethanol) invites exposure to oxygen (visibility, new demands), which feeds acetic bacteria, which reduces morale (converts ethanol to acid), which reduces oxygen exposure (team becomes invisible), allowing ethanol to rebuild. Maintaining morale requires either anaerobic conditions (shielding from external pressure) or sulfite addition (active suppression of cynicism vectors).

name: Glacial Surge Theory of Creative Block Resolution
core_claim: Creative blocks resolve through glacial surge dynamics: subglacial meltwater (unconscious processing) slowly accumulates beneath the frozen creative surface, reducing basal friction until the entire ice mass lurches forward in a surge of productivity. The quiescent phase is not absence of work but pressurization; the surge phase is not inspiration but mechanical release. The oscillation period depends on the ratio of accumulation rate (input/experience) to the drainage efficiency of incremental output.

name: Soil Microbiome Feedback Theory of Cryptocurrency Community Health
core_claim: Cryptocurrency communities develop plant-soil microbiome feedback loops: early community members (pioneer plants) condition the social substrate (norms, governance, culture) in ways that either facilitate or inhibit subsequent community growth. Positive feedback communities accumulate beneficial microbiota (constructive developers, honest validators) that enhance the token's health. Negative feedback communities accumulate pathogens (scammers, pump-and-dump operators) that make the soil toxic for legitimate participants. Once established, the feedback polarity is self-reinforcing and extremely difficult to reverse — explaining why early community culture is the strongest predictor of long-term token viability.

name: Hydraulic Jump Theory of Phonological Neutralization
core_claim: Phoneme neutralization in unstressed syllables occurs through a process structurally identical to a hydraulic jump: high-energy articulatory distinctions in stressed positions flow into low-energy unstressed environments, where a critical threshold is crossed and the laminar distinctions between phonemes collapse into a turbulent, undifferentiated vowel space. The energy dissipated at this jump predicts which contrasts neutralize first and how much articulatory information is irrecoverably lost.

name: Firn Densification Theory of Soil Horizon Development
core_claim: Soil horizon formation follows firn densification feedback dynamics: fresh organic inputs (analogous to snowfall) accumulate at the surface and undergo progressive compression, chemical transformation, and grain restructuring as they are buried by successive layers. The transition from O-horizon to A-horizon mirrors the firn-to-ice transition — a critical density threshold where interconnected pore spaces close off, fundamentally changing the material's hydrological and biological properties. The depth of this transition zone is governed by precipitation rate, temperature, and biological activity, just as firn densification depth depends on accumulation rate, temperature, and overburden pressure.

name: Wound Healing Theory of Cryptographic Key Rotation
core_claim: Cryptographic key rotation follows the phases of wound healing: the inflammatory phase (breach detection triggers immediate key revocation), the proliferative phase (new keys are generated and distributed across endpoints, forming a 'granulation tissue' of provisional trust), and the remodeling phase (redundant key material is pruned and access patterns mature into their final topology over weeks). Systems that skip the proliferative phase and jump directly to new keys suffer the cryptographic equivalent of wound dehiscence -- re-opening of the vulnerability because trust relationships were not properly re-established.

name: Mountain-Valley Assignment Theory of Moral Dilemma Resolution
core_claim: Moral dilemmas are crease assignment problems: each ethical consideration defines a fold line, and the dilemma is solvable (flat-foldable) only if each crease can be consistently assigned as mountain (principle upheld) or valley (principle compromised) without the resulting fold pattern self-intersecting (logical contradiction). Tragic dilemmas are crease patterns with no valid mountain-valley assignment — every possible assignment produces a self-intersection somewhere, meaning no resolution can avoid moral contradiction. The Maekawa theorem analog states that at every decision vertex, the number of principles upheld must differ from the number compromised by exactly two.

name: Dye-Mordant Theory of Memory Consolidation
core_claim: Memory consolidation requires a neurochemical mordant analogous to textile dyeing: the experience itself is the dye (transient electrochemical signal), but it will wash out unless fixed by a mordant process (sleep-dependent protein synthesis at the synapse). Different emotional valences act as different mordants — cortisol fixes traumatic memories with excess fastness (PTSD), while dopaminergic mordants fix reward-associated memories. Memories that fail consolidation are simply undyed synapses that return to baseline.

name: Benford's Law Theory of Ecological Population Monitoring
core_claim: Healthy ecosystems produce population counts that conform to Benford's Law across species: the leading digits of population sizes follow the expected logarithmic distribution because populations arise from multiplicative growth processes. Ecological disturbance — pollution, habitat fragmentation, invasive species — distorts the Benford distribution in predictable ways. Deviation from Benford's Law in wildlife census data can serve as an early-warning system for ecosystem degradation, just as it flags financial fraud.

name: Contact Improvisation Theory of Negotiation
core_claim: Successful negotiation follows the dynamics of contact improvisation dance: both parties must continuously share and receive weight (concessions) through a point of contact (the negotiating table), and the negotiation 'dance' proceeds only when both partners maintain the contact point while shifting weight. Stalemate occurs when one party refuses to receive weight (accept any concession as meaningful), and collapse occurs when both parties lean in simultaneously without a stable base — the negotiation equivalent of two dancers falling because neither maintained structural support.

name: Creep Rupture Theory of Institutional Corruption
core_claim: Institutional corruption follows creep rupture dynamics: under sustained low-level stress (lobbying pressure, revolving-door incentives), institutional integrity slowly deforms over time even at stress levels well below the yield point. The Larson-Miller parameter — combining temperature (political heat) and time (duration of pressure) — predicts when rupture will occur. Institutions that appear structurally sound can be deep into tertiary creep (accelerating deformation) with rupture imminent, explaining why corruption scandals seem to emerge suddenly from previously trusted institutions.

name: Deep-Sea Vent Chemosynthetic Theory of Underground Economies
core_claim: Informal and underground economies function as deep-sea hydrothermal vent ecosystems: cut off from the 'photosynthetic' energy of the formal economy (legal commerce, regulated banking), they sustain themselves through chemosynthesis — deriving economic energy from chemical gradients between the formal and informal sectors (price differentials, regulatory arbitrage, tax avoidance). Vent communities (black markets) cluster around the steepest gradients (most distortionary regulations), and their biodiversity (variety of goods and services) is highest where the chemical energy flux is greatest.

name: LSB Embedding Theory of Architectural Adaptive Reuse
core_claim: Adaptive reuse of buildings follows steganographic least-significant-bit embedding: successful reuse modifies only the 'least significant bits' of a structure (interior partitions, services, surface finishes) while preserving the 'most significant bits' (structural frame, facade rhythm, spatial proportions). Buildings with high embedding capacity — those whose original design contains redundant or flexible spatial information — tolerate more radical functional change without perceptible distortion, just as images with high-frequency noise tolerate more hidden data before artifacts appear.

name: Falconry Imprinting Theory of First-Language Phonological Acquisition
core_claim: First-language phonological acquisition follows falconry imprinting dynamics: there is a critical developmental window during which the infant 'imprints' on the phonological system of its linguistic environment, and this imprinting is nearly irreversible — just as an imprinted falcon bonds to a specific handler species, the infant's perceptual system permanently tunes to the phonemic contrasts of its first language. Late-acquired languages are always 'manned' (falconry term for taming a wild-caught bird) rather than imprinted — functional but never exhibiting the effortless bond of the imprinted system. The falconer's knowledge that imprinting timing is species-specific maps onto cross-linguistic variation in critical period boundaries.

name: Cave Ecosystem Zonation Theory of Corporate Innovation Pipeline
core_claim: Corporate innovation pipelines exhibit cave ecosystem zonation: the entrance zone (market-facing R&D) receives external light and nutrients (customer feedback, market signals) and supports diverse, fast-moving organisms (incremental innovations). The twilight zone (applied research) receives diminishing external signal and hosts specialized organisms adapted to low-light conditions (platform technologies). The dark zone (basic research) is completely isolated from external energy sources and supports only chemolithoautotrophs — innovations that feed on internal organizational chemistry (core competencies) rather than market signals. Most companies over-invest in entrance-zone innovation while starving the dark zone that produces truly novel capabilities.

name: Glass Gather Viscosity Theory of Early-Stage Venture Capital Timing
core_claim: Venture capital investment timing follows glass gather viscosity: the molten glass (startup) must be worked within a narrow viscosity window — too hot (too early) and it flows uncontrollably (the company has no shape, any direction is possible but none is stable); too cool (too late) and it resists shaping (the company's trajectory is set and investor influence is minimal). The optimal working temperature varies by glass composition (industry vertical, technology type), and the glassblower's critical skill is reading viscosity by color and movement, not by thermometer — just as the best VCs read market timing through qualitative signals rather than quantitative metrics.

name: Sourdough Fermentation Timing Theory of Creative Project Management
core_claim: Creative projects require sourdough fermentation timing: the dough (project) must be allowed to ferment (develop) until it reaches optimal gas production (idea generation) and flavor development (conceptual depth), then shaped and baked before over-fermentation produces an acidic, collapsed loaf (over-worked, stale creative output). The baker's 'poke test' (pressing the dough to assess fermentation state by spring-back rate) maps onto the project manager's intuitive assessment of when a creative team has generated enough ideas and must transition from divergent exploration to convergent execution. Under-proofed projects (rushed to market) are dense and flavorless; over-proofed projects (development hell) collapse under their own gas.

name: Falconry Manning Theory of Organizational Onboarding
core_claim: Employee onboarding is structurally identical to falconry manning: the gradual process of habituating a wild raptor to the falconer's presence without breaking its hunting instinct maps precisely onto the challenge of socializing a new hire into organizational culture without extinguishing the independent judgment and outside perspective that made them valuable. Manning too aggressively (aggressive cultural indoctrination) produces a tame but useless bird; manning too slowly (sink-or-swim onboarding) produces a bird that bolts at the first opportunity. The optimal manning schedule, like the falconer's, is a carefully graduated exposure sequence calibrated to the individual temperament.

name: Intertidal Zonation Theory of Social Media Platform Ecosystems
core_claim: Social media platforms form an intertidal zonation gradient: platforms exposed to the full force of public attention (Twitter/X — the splash zone) host only extremophile content that can withstand constant battering; platforms in the mid-tidal zone (Instagram, TikTok) support the richest diversity of content types; platforms in the subtidal zone (Discord servers, private Substacks) host fragile, specialized communities that cannot survive exposure. Content and creators migrate between zones following the same competitive exclusion dynamics as intertidal organisms — a creator optimized for splash-zone survival (controversy, provocation) cannot compete in the subtidal zone where different selection pressures favor depth and specificity.

name: Sandcastle Wave Erosion Theory of Competitive Moat Degradation
core_claim: Competitive moats erode through sandcastle wave dynamics: each competitive wave (new entrant, technological disruption, regulatory change) removes a predictable quantity of sand (market share, pricing power) from the castle's seaward face, and the erosion rate accelerates as the castle shrinks because smaller structures have less mass to resist the wave's kinetic energy. The sandcastle insight is that the fatal erosion is not from large storm waves (disruptive competitors) but from the relentless minor waves (incremental commoditization) that arrive between storms. Companies that rebuild only after visible storm damage ignore the steady recession of their competitive shoreline between storms, and are surprised when a modest wave (minor competitor) breaches what they thought was a thick wall.

name: Kintsugi Gold Line Aesthetics Theory of Scar Tissue in Narrative
core_claim: The most resonant narratives are those that practice kintsugi gold-line aesthetics on their own plot fractures: rather than concealing narrative disruptions (time skips, perspective shifts, tonal breaks) with seamless transitions, great storytelling draws attention to the seams with golden joinery — making the points of fracture the most beautiful and meaningful elements of the work. The gold line (explicit meta-narrative acknowledgment of disruption) transforms structural weakness into aesthetic strength. This explains why fragmented narratives (Faulkner, Borges, Sebald) often achieve greater emotional resonance than linear ones: the gold-filled cracks between fragments are where the reader's own meaning-making floods in, producing a text that is stronger at its repaired joints than at its original surface.
source_a: Kintsugi Gold Line Aesthetics (Kintsugi / Ceramics Repair)

name: Vowel Chain Shift Theory of Market Price Cascades
core_claim: Asset price adjustments in interconnected markets follow vowel chain shift dynamics: when one asset class (vowel) moves in price space, it creates a vacuum or compression that forces adjacent asset classes to shift in sequence, producing a cascade of price realignments that preserves the relative 'phonological distance' between assets. Push chains (one asset inflating forces others upward) and drag chains (one asset deflating pulls others down) produce systematically different cascade patterns, and the entire system resists mergers — two asset classes cannot occupy the same price niche indefinitely.

name: Hardy-Cross Theory of Academic Department Resource Balancing
core_claim: University resource allocation across departments self-corrects through Hardy-Cross iterative balancing: each budget cycle, departments with excess pressure (overenrollment, grant overhead) shed flow to neighboring departments through shared resources (cross-listed courses, joint hires, shared facilities), while departments with deficit pressure (underenrollment) receive compensating flow. The system converges through successive corrections, but convergence requires accurate resistance coefficients (administrative cost models) — if these are miscalibrated, the iteration diverges and produces resource oscillations between over- and under-funded departments.

name: Zero-Knowledge Proof Theory of Terroir Verification
core_claim: Terroir claims in enology can be verified without revealing proprietary winemaking techniques through a protocol structurally identical to zero-knowledge proofs: the winemaker demonstrates that their wine possesses specific mineral isotope ratios, microbial signatures, and phenolic profiles consistent with the claimed origin, without disclosing the viticultural and cellar practices that produced them. The 'soundness' of the proof depends on the computational difficulty of forging these multi-dimensional chemical fingerprints from a different terroir.

name: Tephra Dispersal Theory of Particle Swarm Exploration
core_claim: Particle swarm optimization's exploration phase follows tephra dispersal sorting dynamics: the initial 'eruption' of particles into the search space disperses them with a size-dependent sorting where high-inertia particles (large tephra) land close to the eruption point and explore locally, while low-inertia particles (fine ash) travel farthest and explore globally. The optimal inertia weight schedule in PSO corresponds to the atmospheric conditions that produce the most informative tephra deposit — one where the full range of particle sizes is well-separated across the landscape, maximizing information about the search space topology.

name: Graph Coloring Theory of Interfaith Dialogue
core_claim: Productive interfaith dialogue requires a graph coloring solution: each theological tradition is a vertex, edges connect traditions with overlapping truth-claims, and the 'colors' are interpretive frameworks. Dialogue succeeds when adjacent traditions adopt different interpretive frameworks (comparative, experiential, historical, ethical) such that no two traditions sharing a contested claim use the same framework to discuss it. The chromatic number of the interfaith graph determines the minimum number of distinct interpretive frameworks needed, and the four-color theorem suggests that even the most densely interconnected theological landscape requires at most four distinct dialogical approaches.

name: Stratigraphic Superposition Theory of Memory Consolidation
core_claim: Long-term memory consolidation follows archaeological stratigraphy: newer memories deposit as upper layers that compress and distort older ones. Retrieval of deep memories requires 'excavation' through intervening strata, and the accuracy of recall degrades in proportion to the number and thickness of overlying deposits. Traumatic memories act as intrusive features that cut through normal stratification, remaining accessible regardless of depth but disrupting the chronological ordering of surrounding memories.

name: Rigid Origami Theory of Tectonic Plate Subduction
core_claim: Tectonic plate subduction follows rigid origami mechanics: plates are rigid panels connected by crease lines (fault zones) that fold along predetermined hinge patterns. The Miura-ori-like fold geometry of subduction zones explains why plates descend at characteristic angles rather than arbitrary ones — only certain fold angles satisfy the rigid-foldability constraints imposed by the spherical geometry of Earth's surface. Slab rollback is the kinematic consequence of a rigid fold pattern propagating through the plate, and the Wadati-Benioff zone traces the crease pattern of this planetary origami.

name: Waggle Dance Information Theory of Jury Deliberation
core_claim: Jury deliberation follows the information architecture of honeybee waggle dances: each juror who has identified a strong argument performs a 'deliberative waggle' — repeating and elaborating the argument with intensity proportional to their confidence — and the jury converges on a verdict when the aggregate dance-signal for one position crosses a quorum threshold. Hung juries occur when two competing waggle signals remain at parity, identical to the deadlock bees experience when two nest sites are equally attractive.

name: Kintsugi Fracture Path Theory of Organizational Restructuring
core_claim: Post-crisis organizational restructuring should follow kintsugi principles: rather than disguising or denying the fracture lines left by failure, organizations should make them visible and structurally load-bearing. The gold-filled cracks become the organization's institutional memory — visible, valued reminders of past failure that channel future stress along known, reinforced paths rather than allowing new, unpredictable fractures. Organizations that hide their fracture history (invisible repair) are more brittle than those that celebrate and reinforce it.

name: Tidal Pool Refuge Theory of Small Business Survival
core_claim: Small businesses survive economic cycles by occupying tidal pool refuges: when the tide goes out (recession), organisms stranded in pools (niche markets) survive if the pool retains sufficient volume (customer loyalty), oxygenation (cash flow), and temperature stability (price insensitivity). The key survival trait is not size but pool selection — businesses in deeper pools with better water circulation (diversified revenue within a niche) outlast larger organisms (big companies) stranded on exposed rock. Economic recovery (returning tide) reconnects pools to the open ocean, enabling migration and growth.

name: Kintsugi Structural Redistribution Theory of Post-Injury Athletic Biomechanics
core_claim: Athletes returning from injury undergo kintsugi structural redistribution: the repaired joint or tissue is not restored to its original state but becomes a structurally distinct element — stiffer, differently loaded, often stronger along the fracture line but weaker in adjacent areas. The gold-filled seam redistributes mechanical stress throughout the kinetic chain, forcing compensatory adaptations in connected joints. Successful rehabilitation programs should design for this redistribution rather than pursuing illusory 'full restoration,' just as the kintsugi artist designs the gold lines to create a new structural logic rather than disguising the break.

name: Washed-Rind Biofilm Theory of Open-Source Community Governance
core_claim: Open-source project governance develops through washed-rind biofilm dynamics: regular interventions by maintainers (washing with brine) cultivate a specific surface microbial community (contributor norms) that protects the interior (codebase) from contamination while producing distinctive flavor (project identity). The biofilm is not optional decoration — it is a living protective barrier that must be actively cultivated through consistent, repeated contact. Projects that stop 'washing' (active moderation, regular releases, maintainer engagement) develop wild mold (toxic community dynamics) rather than the intended Brevibacterium culture, producing cheese that smells wrong and drives away consumers.

name: Taxidermy Form Anatomy Theory of Historical Narrative Construction
core_claim: Historical narratives are taxidermy: the historian preserves the 'skin' of events (primary sources, dates, names) but must construct an internal 'form' (interpretive framework, causal narrative) that gives the skin shape and posture. The quality of history depends on the quality of the form's anatomy — a form built on accurate skeletal knowledge of the period produces a lifelike mount, while a form based on the historian's own era's assumptions produces something that looks like the past wearing a modern body. The taxidermist's discipline of studying actual anatomy before constructing the form is the methodological equivalent of archival immersion before narrative construction.

name: Horology Escapement Theory of Democratic Election Cycles
core_claim: Democratic election cycles function as escapement mechanisms: the escapement's job is to release energy (political power) in precisely metered increments, converting the mainspring's continuous force (popular sovereignty) into regulated discrete ticks (election cycles). Without the escapement, the mainspring would unwind explosively (direct democracy degenerating into mob rule) or not at all (power consolidation). The escapement's genius is that it simultaneously releases AND restrains — each tick both transfers power and immediately locks it again. Term limits, election schedules, and transition protocols are the pallets of the democratic escapement, and their precise geometry determines whether the clock keeps accurate time or runs fast (populist acceleration) or slow (institutional sclerosis).

name: Glass Annealing Stress Theory of Post-Conflict Reconciliation
core_claim: Post-conflict societies require annealing: like glass that is cooled too rapidly after forming (producing internal stresses that cause spontaneous shattering), societies that transition too quickly from conflict to 'normal' operations retain invisible internal stresses (unprocessed trauma, unresolved grievances, suppressed resentment) that produce delayed, catastrophic fracture. The annealing process — holding the glass at a carefully controlled intermediate temperature (transitional justice, truth commissions, controlled inter-group contact) for a sufficient duration, then cooling at a rate slow enough for internal stresses to relax — is the only process that produces durable peace. The annealing temperature must be high enough for molecular mobility (enough freedom for genuine dialogue) but low enough to prevent deformation (maintaining institutional structure during the process).

name: Kelp Forest Trophic Cascade Theory of Open Source Software Ecosystems
core_claim: Open source ecosystems follow kelp forest trophic cascade topology: a single keystone predator (major corporate sponsor — Google, Microsoft, Meta) maintains ecosystem health by suppressing urchin populations (rent-seeking middlemen, proprietary forks) that would otherwise overgraze the kelp (foundational open source libraries). When the keystone predator withdraws (corporate sponsor pivots away), urchin barrens (ecosystem collapse into fragmented, proprietary alternatives) follow rapidly. The trophic cascade model predicts that open source ecosystem health depends less on the number of contributors (kelp fronds) than on the presence and behavior of a few keystone predators, and that well-intentioned attempts to 'democratize' the ecosystem by removing keystone predators produce the opposite of the intended effect.
source_a: Kelp Forest Trophic Cascade Topology (Marine Biology)

name: Semaphore Alphabet Design Theory of API Interface Design
core_claim: API design should follow semaphore alphabet design principles: the semaphore system's alphabet was not arbitrary but optimized for maximum angular separation between letters (to minimize misreading at distance), minimum arm repositioning between common letter sequences (to maximize transmission speed), and a rest position that is unambiguous (clearly 'no signal' rather than mistaken for a letter). API endpoints similarly need maximum conceptual separation between operations (to prevent developer misuse), minimum cognitive repositioning between common call sequences (to enable fluent composition), and an unambiguous default/empty state (clear distinction between 'no data' and 'error'). The semaphore design principle that the most common letters should require the least arm movement translates to: the most common API operations should require the fewest parameters and the simplest request structure.

name: Consciousness Microstructure Theory
core_claim: Conscious experience has a microstructure analogous to financial market microstructure — the continuous feel of awareness emerges from discrete 'qualia transactions' with bid-ask spreads, order books, and latency, and phenomenal binding is a clearing mechanism that nets offsetting sensory signals.

name: Vowel Chain Shift Theory of Cryptocurrency Token Migration
core_claim: When one blockchain token shifts its functional niche (from currency to store-of-value), it displaces a neighboring token from that niche, which in turn displaces the next, producing a chain shift across the token ecosystem. The shift is driven by the same structural pressure as vowel chain shifts in phonology: tokens must maintain distinctiveness in the functional space, so one movement cascades through the entire system. The direction of the chain (push vs. pull) determines whether the ecosystem expands or contracts in functional diversity.

name: Paris Law Theory of Tannin Polymerization Fatigue
core_claim: Tannin polymerization in aging wine follows Paris Law fatigue crack growth: each 'cycle' of oxidation-reduction (driven by micro-oxygenation through barrel staves) advances tannin chain length by an increment proportional to the stress intensity range — the amplitude of the redox oscillation. Below a threshold oxidation intensity, no polymerization occurs; above a critical intensity, catastrophic over-polymerization produces excessive sediment. The optimal aging regime maintains the stress intensity in the Paris Law's stable-growth regime, where chain elongation is predictable and controllable.

name: SLAM Theory of Autobiographical Identity
core_claim: The construction of autobiographical identity operates as Simultaneous Localization and Mapping: the self must simultaneously determine where it is in life-space (localization) and construct a coherent narrative map of its past (mapping), using noisy sensory evidence from memory. Identity crises occur when accumulated loop-closure failures (contradictions between the believed map and new evidence) exceed the filter's capacity to maintain a consistent joint estimate.

name: Kawasaki-Justin Theory of Ecosystem Stability
core_claim: Stable ecosystems satisfy origami flat-foldability conditions at every trophic vertex: the alternating angles between species interactions (predation, competition, mutualism, parasitism) must sum to 180 degrees — a balance condition ensuring that energy and biomass flows can 'fold flat' without self-intersection (impossible biomass accounting). Ecosystems that violate Kawasaki's theorem at even one vertex accumulate structural stress that manifests as population oscillations, trophic cascades, or collapse. Keystone species occupy the vertices with the most crease lines (highest interaction degree), making them the points where flat-foldability is most easily violated by their removal.

name: Laban Effort-Shape Theory of Architectural Spatial Experience
core_claim: The experiential quality of architectural spaces can be analyzed through Laban's effort-shape framework: a cathedral's nave embodies sustained/direct/light/free effort qualities that produce awe, while a cramped corridor is sudden/indirect/strong/bound. Architects implicitly choreograph movement through buildings by sequencing effort-shape qualities along circulation paths. Successful spatial design creates an effort-shape 'phrase' with dynamic contrast; monotonous buildings fail because they sustain a single effort quality.

name: Brood Cycle Regulation Theory of Agile Sprint Planning
core_claim: Agile software development sprints should follow honeybee brood cycle regulation: the queen's egg-laying rate (product backlog input) must be calibrated to the colony's nursing capacity (developer bandwidth), and the 21-day brood cycle provides a natural sprint cadence. Overloading the brood nest (sprint overcommitment) produces neglected larvae (half-finished features) that consume resources without contributing to colony fitness. The colony's ability to thermoregulate the brood nest (maintain consistent development environment) is the critical constraint that most sprint planning ignores.

name: Harris Lines Theory of Economic Recession Detection
core_claim: Economic recessions leave Harris lines in demographic and institutional data: just as childhood growth arrest leaves visible lines in long-bone radiographs, economic stress arrest leaves detectable marks in cohort data — reduced educational attainment, compressed wage growth, delayed family formation. These lines are permanent records that allow retrospective dating and severity estimation of economic shocks, even when GDP data is unavailable or unreliable. Developing-economy recession history could be reconstructed from demographic Harris lines rather than national accounts.

name: Sandcastle Capillary Bridge Theory of Social Trust Networks
core_claim: Social trust operates through capillary bridge mechanics: trust between individuals (sand grains) is maintained by thin water films (shared experiences, reciprocal favors) that create capillary bridges generating cohesive force. Too little water (insufficient interaction) produces dry, non-cohesive sand; too much water (over-familiarity, obligation saturation) destroys the capillary bridges by flooding the contact points. The optimal moisture content for social cohesion — enough shared history to bind but not so much that boundaries dissolve — explains why both isolated individuals and enmeshed relationships produce structurally weak social formations.

name: Semaphore Angular Resolution Theory of Emotional Granularity
core_claim: Emotional intelligence depends on semaphore angular resolution: individuals with high emotional granularity can distinguish between closely spaced emotional signals (the 45-degree difference between frustration and disappointment), while those with low granularity see only the coarse categories (the 90-degree difference between 'good' and 'bad'). Therapeutic interventions that increase emotional vocabulary literally increase the angular resolution of the person's internal semaphore system, allowing them to send and receive more precise emotional signals. Alexithymia is a resolution problem — the semaphore has only two positions (arms up/arms down) rather than the full alphabet.

name: Taxidermy Skin Preservation Theory of Cultural Heritage Digitization
core_claim: Cultural heritage digitization is a taxidermic preservation challenge: the 'skin' (surface appearance — images, text, physical form) can be preserved with current technology, but the 'internal anatomy' (cultural context, embodied knowledge, community meaning) decays the moment the specimen is removed from its living environment. High-resolution scans of a ritual mask are equivalent to a well-tanned skin — they preserve surface detail indefinitely but contain none of the living organism's behavior. The field-prep insight from taxidermy applies: the most critical preservation work must happen at the moment of 'collection' (community engagement, oral history recording), not in the lab (digital archive).

name: Parkour Flow State Lock Theory of Surgical Performance
core_claim: Expert surgical performance depends on parkour flow-state lock: the surgeon must enter and maintain a state of continuous perceptual-motor integration where each completed action (suture, incision, dissection) automatically loads the motor program for the next. Interruptions (equipment failure, unexpected anatomy, team communication breakdowns) break the flow lock and force a costly restart from deliberate, conscious control — the parkour equivalent of stopping mid-run and having to re-evaluate the entire obstacle sequence from standing. Operating room design and team protocols should be evaluated primarily on their ability to maintain the surgeon's flow-state lock, not on their individual ergonomic properties.

name: Beekeeping Waggle Dance Theory of Academic Citation Networks
core_claim: Academic citations function as waggle dances: a paper's citation list is a choreographed communication that encodes the direction (intellectual lineage) and distance (methodological proximity) of valuable resource patches (prior work) for other foragers (researchers). The waggle dance's key feature — that it communicates relative position from the hive, not absolute coordinates — maps onto how citations orient readers relative to the citing paper's own position, not to any absolute intellectual map. Citation manipulation (self-citation rings, coercive citation) is the equivalent of a bee performing a fraudulent waggle dance to redirect foragers away from a competitor's patch, a behavior that degrades colony fitness.

name: Mycorrhizal Network Theory of Intergenerational Wealth Transfer
core_claim: Intergenerational wealth transfer operates as a common mycorrhizal network: established 'mother trees' (wealthy elder generation) channel resources (capital, social connections, institutional knowledge) through underground fungal networks (trusts, family offices, alumni networks) to seedlings (younger generation members) in their root zone. The network exhibits the same source-sink dynamics as forest mycorrhizae: carbon flows from photosynthetically productive adults to shaded juveniles, but also to stressed adults, creating a mutual insurance system. The critical insight is that severing the mycorrhizal network (inheritance tax, estate dissolution) doesn't just stop resource flow — it eliminates the information signaling that tells the network WHERE resources are most needed, producing a landscape of isolated individuals competing rather than a cooperative forest.

name: Coral Holobiont Feedback Theory of Team Performance
core_claim: High-performing teams are coral holobionts: the team's performance emerges not from individual competence (the coral animal alone) but from the symbiotic relationship between team members (coral polyps), their shared practices (zooxanthellae algae providing photosynthetic energy), and the accumulated institutional knowledge they build (calcium carbonate skeleton). Team 'bleaching' — the breakdown that occurs when shared practices are disrupted (reorganization, remote transition, leadership change) — expels the zooxanthellae and leaves the coral alive but starving. Recovery requires re-establishing symbiosis, not replacing individual polyps. The holobiont model predicts that teams transferred intact to new contexts will outperform teams assembled from individually superior members, because the symbiotic microbiome transfers with the colony.
source_b: Ashby's Law of Requisite Variety (Systems Theory)

name: Falconry Weight Management Theory of Portfolio Risk Calibration
core_claim: Portfolio risk management follows falconry weight management: a raptor kept too heavy (over-capitalized portfolio) is unmotivated to hunt (generates no alpha); a raptor kept too light (under-capitalized, over-leveraged) is weak and erratic, prone to crashing (margin calls, forced liquidation). The falconer's daily weigh-in and micro-adjustment of food rations maps onto the portfolio manager's daily risk assessment and position-size adjustment. The critical falconry insight is that optimal flying weight varies with weather conditions (market regime), the quarry pursued (asset class), and the individual bird's metabolism (fund structure) — there is no universal 'correct weight,' only a continuously recalibrated narrow band specific to the current hunt.

name: Lava Flow Channelization Theory of Information Cascade Routing
core_claim: Information cascades in social networks self-channelize like lava flows: initial flow on an undifferentiated slope (new information entering a network) rapidly self-organizes into channels as the edges of the flow cool and solidify (early-engaged users whose opinions are now fixed), creating levees that constrain subsequent flow into narrower, faster channels. The channelization process is irreversible — once the levees form, the information cascade is committed to a specific path through the network regardless of whether that path leads to a receptive or hostile audience. This explains why viral misinformation is so difficult to redirect once it has begun flowing: the rheological properties of social engagement mean that the channel levees (established narrative framing) harden faster than correction campaigns can erode them.

name: Dansgaard-Oeschger Oscillation Theory of Artistic Canon Formation
core_claim: The artistic canon oscillates between periods of inclusive expansion and exclusive contraction on a timescale governed by paleoclimatic-style bipolar seesaw dynamics: when one hemisphere of the art world (e.g., Western institutions) warms to diversity and expands the canon, it draws critical attention and resources away from the other hemisphere (non-Western traditions), which cools and contracts. The oscillation is quasi-periodic (~25-50 year cycles) and is driven by the 'freshwater forcing' of generational taste shifts that disrupt the established thermohaline circulation of prestige.

name: Stribeck Curve Theory of Workplace Conflict Lubrication
core_claim: Interpersonal conflict in organizations passes through Stribeck lubrication regimes. At low interaction velocity (rare contact), boundary friction dominates — every encounter generates maximal personal friction. At intermediate velocity, a mixed regime emerges where institutional protocols partially lubricate interactions. At high velocity (constant collaboration), full hydrodynamic lubrication develops — the thick film of shared context and routine eliminates personal friction entirely. The optimal operating point minimizes total organizational wear.

name: Plinian Column Collapse Theory of Oral Epic Fragmentation
core_claim: Long oral epic traditions collapse through a mechanism identical to Plinian volcanic column dynamics: a sustained vertical 'column' of performative energy — driven by audience engagement, performer skill, and cultural investment — maintains the epic's coherent structure against gravitational pull toward fragmentation. When the mass flux of new performers entering the tradition drops below a critical threshold, or when the cultural 'vent pressure' weakens, the column collapses, and the formerly unified epic breaks into pyroclastic flows of disconnected episodes, legends, and folk motifs that spread laterally across the cultural landscape.

name: Thermohaline Circulation Theory of Knowledge Flow in Organizations
core_claim: Knowledge circulates through large organizations via thermohaline-like overturning: 'hot' knowledge (novel insights, market intelligence) rises through informal networks to executive levels where it cools into codified strategy, then sinks as 'cold' directives through formal hierarchies back to operational levels. The circulation rate depends on knowledge density gradients (expertise concentration) and salinity analogs (organizational culture viscosity). Disruption of this circulation produces knowledge 'stagnation zones' equivalent to oceanic dead zones.

name: Fixative Evaporation Cascade Theory of Institutional Memory Loss
core_claim: Institutional memory evaporates in a three-stage cascade identical to perfume evaporation: top notes (recent procedural knowledge) vanish within months of employee turnover, heart notes (organizational culture, tacit norms) fade over 2-5 years, and base notes (founding values, origin myths) persist for decades but become unrecognizable without the heart notes to contextualize them. Organizations that invest only in documentation preserve top notes while neglecting the heart-note knowledge that gives documentation meaning.

name: Terroir Mineral Gradient Theory of Neighborhood Identity
core_claim: Neighborhood cultural identity is a terroir phenomenon: the unique combination of built environment (soil), economic conditions (climate), historical inhabitants (grape variety), and institutional frameworks (viticulture practice) produces a distinctive cultural 'minerality' that cannot be replicated by transplanting any single element. Gentrification is terroir destruction — replacing the native cultivar while preserving the marketing language of place, producing a wine that carries the appellation but lacks the mineral signature that made the original valuable.

name: Cave Speleogenesis Theory of Neural Pathway Formation
core_claim: Neural pathway formation follows speleogenetic principles: repeated cognitive activity (water flow) gradually dissolves resistance (limestone) along preferential routes, creating ever-larger channels (myelinated pathways) that capture increasingly distant tributaries (associated memories). The process exhibits the same positive feedback as cave formation — once a passage enlarges slightly, it captures more flow, which enlarges it further. Cognitive rigidity in aging mirrors cave maturity: the passages become so well-established that new water (novel information) cannot create alternative routes and must follow the existing cave system.

name: Taxidermy Form Anatomy Theory of Historical Biographical Writing
core_claim: Historical biography is a taxidermic art: the biographer preserves the subject's skin (documented facts, letters, accounts) and stretches it over an artificial form (narrative structure, psychological interpretation) that determines the final pose and expression. The quality of the biography depends on the anatomical accuracy of the internal form — a form built from the subject's actual skeletal structure (verified chronology, confirmed relationships) produces a lifelike mount, while a generic form (anachronistic psychological frameworks, presentist moral categories) produces an uncanny specimen that resembles the subject only superficially. The eyes (motivational attributions) are always artificial and always the most scrutinized element.

name: Splash Zone Extremophile Theory of Startup Founder Psychology
core_claim: Successful startup founders are splash-zone extremophiles: they thrive in the organizational equivalent of the supralittoral zone — above the high-tide line of institutional support, exposed to extreme desiccation (resource scarcity), UV radiation (public scrutiny), and thermal cycling (market volatility). Their adaptations (high risk tolerance, obsessive focus, narrative construction ability) are precisely the traits that would be maladaptive in the subtidal zone (large corporate environments) where stable conditions select for different organisms. The common failure of 'acqui-hired' founders is an ecological mismatch — transplanting a splash-zone extremophile into the subtidal kills it.

name: Morse Relay Amplification Theory of Pedagogical Scaffolding
core_claim: Effective teaching operates through morse relay amplification: just as telegraph relay stations re-amplified degrading electrical signals to send them further without distortion, each layer of pedagogical scaffolding should re-amplify the learner's fading understanding signal before it degrades below the noise floor. The key insight is that relays don't add new information — they faithfully reproduce the original signal at full strength. Similarly, the best scaffolding doesn't add new content at the point of confusion but re-presents the original concept with restored signal clarity, cleaning the noise (misconceptions) that accumulated during the learner's internal transmission.

name: Sewing Structure Tension Theory of Alliance Systems
core_claim: International alliance systems are bookbinding sewing structures: each treaty (sewing station) connects two signatures (nations) at a specific point, and the overall binding strength depends not on the strength of individual stitches (bilateral treaties) but on the sewing pattern — whether it is kettle-stitch (each signature linked to its neighbor, creating a chain), or long-stitch (each signature linked directly to the cover boards, creating a hub-and-spoke). NATO is a kettle-stitch binding (Article 5 links each member to each other); the US bilateral alliance system in Asia is a long-stitch binding (each ally connected to the US hub but not to each other). The sewing structure predicts failure modes: kettle-stitch fails by unraveling from one end (domino defection); long-stitch fails by the cover board detaching (US withdrawal).

name: Triboelectric Theory of Organizational Blame Transfer
core_claim: When organizational units come into repeated contact under pressure, blame accumulates on the unit with lower 'work-function' prestige, exactly as triboelectric charge separates at material interfaces. The resulting blame gradient drives defensive behaviors that further increase friction, until discharge events (firings, restructurings) reset the potential — only for the cycle to resume with the next contact pair.

name: Chiasmus Theory of Wound Healing Phase Reversal
core_claim: The four phases of wound healing (hemostasis, inflammation, proliferation, remodeling) follow a chiasmic rhetorical structure where the molecular signals of the second half mirror and invert those of the first: the pro-inflammatory cytokines of phase 2 are structurally answered by anti-inflammatory resolvins in phase 3, and the rapid clot formation of phase 1 is inverted by the slow matrix remodeling of phase 4. Failed healing occurs when this chiasmic symmetry breaks — when the rhetorical 'turn' from destruction to reconstruction is not properly executed.

name: Precipitation Hardening Theory of Mycorrhizal Network Resilience
core_claim: Mycorrhizal networks develop resilience through a process analogous to precipitation hardening in alloys: dissolved nutrients cycling through the hyphal matrix gradually precipitate as insoluble mineral-organic complexes at fungal cell junctions, creating microscopic 'hardening particles' that impede the propagation of pathogenic infections and physical disruptions through the network. Young networks (solution-treated) are ductile but vulnerable; aged networks (peak-hardened) resist damage but become brittle; over-aged networks lose both flexibility and resistance as the precipitates coarsen and lose coherency with the fungal matrix.

name: Felting Entanglement Theory of Social Trust
core_claim: Social trust in communities builds through a mechanism analogous to felting: random interpersonal encounters (fiber contacts) under social 'agitation' (shared crises, festivals, market interactions) form irreversible bonds when the interactions exceed a threshold of emotional intensity. Communities with insufficient agitation remain loose assemblages; those with excessive agitation compact into rigid, exclusionary structures. Trust density follows the same irreversible entanglement kinetics as wool fiber under heat and moisture.

name: Wavefront Resonance Theory of Religious Revivals
