core_claim: Religious revival movements propagate as spatial wavefronts analogous to epidemic wavefronts: they amplify when encountering susceptible population clusters (communities experiencing anomie, economic dislocation, or cultural upheaval) and attenuate when hitting 'immune barriers' (secularized or religiously saturated populations). The Great Awakenings in American history exhibit wavefront velocities and amplification patterns consistent with spatial SIR dynamics coupled to socioeconomic susceptibility gradients.

name: Escapement Regulation Theory of Attention
core_claim: Sustained attention operates like a mechanical escapement: the mind's 'mainspring' (arousal/motivation) provides continuous energy, but the attentional escapement converts this into discrete, regulated pulses of focus — each tick-tock cycle being a moment of engagement followed by a micro-disengagement that resets the mechanism. ADHD corresponds to a faulty escapement that either over-releases (hyperfocus) or under-catches (distractibility), rather than a deficit in the mainspring itself.

name: Maillard Reaction Theory of Creative Collaboration
core_claim: Creative breakthroughs in collaborative teams follow Maillard reaction kinetics: ideas from different disciplines are the amino acids and reducing sugars, and productive collaboration requires sufficient 'heat' (deadline pressure, creative tension) and controlled 'moisture' (communication bandwidth). Too much moisture (over-communication) prevents browning; too little heat (no pressure) leaves the ingredients unreacted. The hundreds of distinct flavor compounds produced by a single Maillard reaction explain why the outputs of true creative collaboration are irreducible to the sum of individual contributions.

name: Acid Paper Degradation Theory of Oral Tradition Decline
core_claim: Oral traditions degrade following the same autocatalytic kinetics as acid paper degradation: each retelling introduces hydrolytic 'acid' (reinterpretation, simplification) that weakens the narrative cellulose, and the degradation products themselves catalyze further degradation. The preservation half-life of an oral tradition depends on its initial 'alkaline reserve' — the redundancy of mnemonic devices, formulaic phrases, and ritual contexts that buffer against retelling-induced acidity. Traditions in societies with strong ritual scaffolding (high alkaline reserve) survive millennia; those in rapidly modernizing contexts (acid environments) crumble within generations.

name: Iceberg Calving Theory of Organizational Layoffs
core_claim: Corporate layoffs follow iceberg calving threshold dynamics: the 'ice shelf' of the workforce accumulates internal stress (misalignment between employee skills and market demands) until a calving threshold is reached, at which point large blocks of employees detach catastrophically. The calving rate accelerates non-linearly as the shelf thins (profit margins shrink), and each calving event further weakens the shelf edge, making subsequent layoffs more likely — the corporate analogue of how calving exposes new ice faces to warm water. Buyouts and hiring freezes are analogous to surface-melt strategies that reduce shelf mass gradually rather than through catastrophic calving.

name: Marionette String Coupling Theory of Central Bank Communication
core_claim: Central bank forward guidance operates through marionette string coupling: each policy instrument (interest rate, QE, yield curve control) is a control string attached to a specific joint of the economic marionette. The puppeteer's challenge is that strings are unidirectional (they pull but cannot push), creating asymmetric policy effectiveness — loosening is harder than tightening, just as making a marionette sit is harder than making it stand. Cross-talk between strings (policy spillovers) produces unintended limb movements that the audience (markets) interprets as intentional choreography.

name: Glass Color Redox Theory of Organizational Culture Change
core_claim: Organizational culture change operates through glass color redox chemistry: the same metallic ion (leadership initiative) produces entirely different colors (cultural outcomes) depending on the oxidation state of the furnace atmosphere (organizational environment). Copper produces turquoise in oxidizing conditions and deep red in reducing conditions — the same diversity initiative, for instance, produces genuine inclusion in a psychologically safe environment but performative compliance in a fear-based one. Culture consultants who prescribe specific 'colorants' (programs) without controlling the 'atmosphere' (environmental conditions) will get unpredictable and often unwanted colors.

name: Semaphore Night Adaptation Theory of Crisis Communication Modality Switching
core_claim: Crisis communication requires semaphore night-adaptation: when the visual channel (normal media, social platforms) is degraded by darkness (information fog, platform failures, censorship), operators must switch to alternative signaling modalities — torches, lanterns, signal fires — that trade bandwidth for reliability. Organizations that rely exclusively on high-bandwidth visual semaphore (social media, email blasts) in daylight conditions have no night-signaling capability when crisis hits. The historical semaphore night-adaptation protocols (pre-arranged torch signals, reduced-alphabet systems) provide a template for crisis communication planning: pre-establish low-bandwidth, high-reliability channels before the darkness falls.

name: Bonsai Ramification Theory of Knowledge Graph Construction
core_claim: Knowledge graph quality depends on ramification — the bonsai technique of encouraging fine branching through repeated tip-pruning. Each pruning cut (editorial decision to stop elaborating a concept) forces two or more subsidiary branches (sub-concepts) to develop, producing the dense, fine-textured canopy (rich interconnection) that distinguishes a mature bonsai from a juvenile tree (sparse ontology). Knowledge graphs that grow without ramification produce long, unbranched chains (deep taxonomies with no lateral connections); those pruned too aggressively produce stunted bushes (broad but shallow tag clouds). The bonsai master's patience — ramification takes decades — is the missing virtue in knowledge engineering.

name: Sandcastle Capillary Bridge Theory of Social Cohesion
core_claim: Social cohesion in communities operates through capillary bridges: just as sandcastle stability depends on thin water films between grains creating surface-tension bonds that vanish when water content is either too low (dry sand collapses) or too high (wet sand liquefies), community cohesion depends on an optimal density of weak interpersonal ties that provide just enough social 'moisture' to bind individuals without drowning individual identity. Gentrification and social atomization represent the dry and wet failure modes respectively — too few bridges between old and new residents, or too much institutional homogenization that liquefies distinct subcommunities.

name: Bookbinding Signature Gathering Theory of Scientific Consensus Formation
core_claim: Scientific consensus forms through signature gathering: individual research papers are 'leaves' that are folded into 'signatures' (research programs), which are then sewn together into a 'text block' (paradigm). The binding order matters — signatures sewn in the wrong sequence produce a book whose pages don't read coherently, just as research programs integrated in the wrong order produce paradigms with logical gaps. The key insight is that consensus is not about the quality of individual leaves (papers) but about the quality of the gathering (how research programs are organized into a coherent sequence) and the sewing (how they're linked by review, replication, and meta-analysis).

name: Volcanic Edifice Instability Theory of Institutional Collapse
core_claim: Large institutions collapse through volcanic edifice instability: the very growth that makes a volcano impressive (institutional accumulation of resources, reputation, complexity) simultaneously increases the gravitational stress on its flanks (organizational debt, legacy commitments, internal contradictions). Sector collapse — where an entire flank of the volcano slides away catastrophically — is not caused by the triggering earthquake (scandal, market shift) but by the pre-existing over-steepening that made the edifice gravitationally unstable. Post-collapse, the debris avalanche (departing talent, lost institutional knowledge) travels much further than expected because it liquefies upon failure, and the resulting amphitheater (organizational scar) permanently redirects future growth in unexpected directions.

name: Fixative Evaporation Cascade Theory of Cultural Memory Persistence
core_claim: Cultural memory persists through fixative evaporation cascades: like a perfume's fixative base notes (sandalwood, musk, amber) that remain on skin long after top and middle notes have evaporated, cultural events that encode themselves into the 'fixative layer' of a society (architecture, language, cuisine, ritual) persist for centuries after the 'top notes' (news coverage, social media discourse) and 'middle notes' (academic analysis, political debate) have dissipated. The fixative layer is slow to absorb (events must be sustained or intensely traumatic to reach it) but nearly permanent once set. Cultural amnesia occurs when the fixative layer itself is stripped — through colonization, forced assimilation, or physical destruction of the built environment that serves as the base note carrier.

name: Falconry Imprinting Theory of Brand Loyalty Formation
core_claim: Brand loyalty forms through falconry imprinting: raptors imprinted on humans during a critical developmental window (first 2 weeks post-hatch) form a permanent, species-level identification with their handler — they don't merely tolerate humans but genuinely categorize the handler as conspecific. Consumer brand relationships formed during critical developmental windows (first independent purchases in late adolescence, first-job brand encounters, first-child purchases) produce similarly deep identification that persists irrationally against superior alternatives. The falconry insight is that imprinting is irreversible and species-general rather than individual-specific: an imprinted hawk bonds to 'human' not 'this human.' Similarly, brand imprinting creates loyalty to a category-brand identity (Apple as 'my kind of company') rather than to specific products, explaining why brand loyalty survives individual product failures.

name: Triboelectric Charge Theory of Denominational Ladder Selection
core_claim: Denominational systems in coinage self-organize through triboelectric charge separation dynamics: frequent transactional 'contact' between denomination levels generates a charge differential — an information gradient about relative purchasing power — that separates denominations into distinct functional roles (small change vs. store of value vs. unit of account). Denominations that fail to accumulate sufficient charge separation from their neighbors are eliminated from circulation, predicting that stable monetary systems require minimum ratio gaps between adjacent denominations analogous to the triboelectric series spacing.

name: Selvage Edge Theory of National Borders
core_claim: National borders function as selvage edges in the geopolitical fabric: specialized binding structures (customs, border patrols, checkpoints) that prevent the unraveling of state sovereignty. Borders that lack adequate 'edge construction' (weak states, disputed territories) fray under tension, allowing cross-border flows of people, goods, and violence that progressively unweave the state's territorial integrity. The stability of a border depends not on its length but on the quality of its edge-locking mechanism.

name: Warp-Weft Tension Theory of Bilingual Syntax
core_claim: Bilingual speakers maintain two grammars in tension like warp and weft threads on a loom: the dominant language provides the structural warp (word order, morphological frame), while the secondary language contributes weft-like insertions (code-switches, loan translations) that interlock at grammatically licensed crossing points. Code-switching failures occur at points of incompatible tension — where the two grammars' 'thread angles' prevent clean interlocking — predicting exactly which syntactic boundaries permit switching and which do not.

name: Vine Training Architecture Theory of Neural Network Pruning
core_claim: Neural network pruning should follow viticulture vine training principles: just as grapevines are pruned to specific architectures (cordon, cane, goblet) that optimize the tradeoff between fruit quality and canopy size for a given terroir, neural networks should be pruned to architecture-specific patterns that match their deployment environment. Over-pruning (excessive spur pruning) reduces yield but increases per-output quality, directly paralleling the accuracy-efficiency tradeoff. The viticultural insight that different rootstock-scion combinations require different training systems predicts that optimal pruning patterns are not universal but architecture-dependent.

name: Glass Bubble Inflation Theory of Speculative Narrative Construction
core_claim: Speculative narratives (conspiracy theories, hype cycles, utopian visions) inflate like glassblower's bubbles: a small gather of molten factual material is inflated by rhetorical breath into a thin-walled vessel whose internal volume (narrative scope) vastly exceeds the mass of its material (factual basis). The bubble's shape is determined by gravity (reality constraints), centrifugal force (social momentum), and the tools applied to its surface (media framing). The glassblower's critical skill — knowing when the glass is too thin to sustain further inflation — has no equivalent in narrative construction, which is why speculative bubbles expand until they burst rather than being gracefully closed.

name: Falconry Molt Cycle Theory of Career Sabbatical Planning
core_claim: Career sabbaticals should follow the falconry molt cycle: during molt (sabbatical), the falcon is grounded and cannot hunt (produce), but the process replaces worn feathers (depleted skills, outdated knowledge) with fresh ones that restore full flight capability. Falconers who rush molt (abbreviated sabbaticals) produce birds with incomplete feather replacement — functional but with reduced performance that compounds over subsequent seasons. The molt must be supported with rich feeding (financial and intellectual nourishment during sabbatical) despite zero hunting output, and the bird that emerges is measurably superior to the pre-molt version but requires a re-manning period (re-onboarding) before returning to peak performance.

name: Lock Tumbler Shear Line Theory of Democratic Consensus
core_claim: Democratic consensus-building is a lock-picking problem: each pin tumbler represents a constituency whose 'key pin' (core interest) must be raised to exactly the shear line (the threshold of acceptability) for the lock (legislative process) to turn. A key that raises all pins to the shear line simultaneously is a successful coalition; a key that raises some too high and others too low produces binding (political deadlock). The lock-picker's technique of applying slight rotational tension while manipulating individual pins maps onto the legislative strategy of maintaining just enough procedural momentum (tension) while addressing individual constituency concerns (pin-by-pin setting). Security pins (ideological hardliners) create false sets that feel like progress but prevent the lock from fully opening.
source_a: Pin Tumbler Shear Line (Lock-Picking / Security Engineering)

name: Warp-Weft Tension Balancing Theory of Conversational Dynamics
core_claim: Conversation operates as a woven textile: one speaker provides the warp threads (stable, structural contributions — topic, frame, context) while the other provides the weft threads (dynamic, cross-cutting contributions — reactions, elaborations, challenges). Good conversation requires balanced tension: if the warp is too tight (one speaker controls topic rigidly), the weft cannot pass through and the fabric becomes a monologue; if the weft is too loose (responses are tangential, non-engaging), the fabric has no structural integrity. Conversational repair is re-tensioning — adjusting the balance when one dimension becomes slack or over-taut. Therapy, specifically, is the art of providing optimal warp tension so the client's weft can produce a coherent fabric.

name: Steel Tempering Martensite Theory of Habit Formation
core_claim: Habit formation is steel tempering: the initial behavior change (heating steel above the critical temperature) creates an unstable, highly stressed state (austenite — the 'white-knuckling' phase of behavior change). Rapid quenching (sudden, total commitment) produces martensite — extremely hard but brittle habits that shatter under unexpected stress. Slow cooling (gradual behavior change) produces pearlite — softer, more flexible habits that bend without breaking. The tempering process (reheating quenched steel to an intermediate temperature) corresponds to the critical maintenance phase where rigid new habits are deliberately softened and made more flexible through controlled 'reheating' — intentional exposure to the old behavioral environment in controlled doses, producing tempered martensite: hard enough to hold an edge but tough enough not to shatter.

name: Scent Molecule Competition Theory of Attention Economy Dynamics
core_claim: Digital attention markets follow scent molecule competition: different content types (fragrance molecules) compete for the same olfactory receptors (user attention slots), and the dynamics follow the same competitive binding kinetics. High-volatility content (viral posts — top notes) reaches receptors first but evaporates quickly; low-volatility content (long-form analysis — base notes) binds more slowly but occupies receptors for longer periods, blocking subsequent high-volatility content. Platform algorithms that optimize for initial engagement (top-note intensity) systematically undervalue the base-note content that produces lasting user satisfaction. The perfumer's insight applies: a fragrance composed entirely of top notes (a feed of only viral content) smells intense for seconds then disappears, while one with proper base-note foundation (substantive content) maintains presence for hours.

name: Sandcastle Height Scaling Theory of Organizational Hierarchy Limits
core_claim: Organizational hierarchy has sandcastle height scaling limits: a sandcastle's maximum height scales as the 2/3 power of its base width (because gravitational stress scales with height while structural strength scales with cross-section), and organizational hierarchies face an analogous scaling law — the maximum number of management layers scales sub-linearly with organizational base (headcount). Adding hierarchy levels above the scaling limit produces the organizational equivalent of sandcastle collapse: the weight of the management structure exceeds the load-bearing capacity of the operational base. The scaling exponent varies with 'sand quality' (employee skill and autonomy) and 'water content' (cultural cohesion): organizations with higher-quality sand and optimal moisture can build taller before collapse.

name: Shell Company Network Theory of Third-Body Wear Layers
core_claim: Third-body wear layers in tribological contacts form hierarchical structures topologically identical to shell company networks in financial fraud: primary wear particles (first-shell entities) aggregate into secondary structures (holding companies) that create a layered topology designed to obscure the direct contact between the two sliding surfaces (the ultimate beneficial owners). Detection of the true contact state requires the same graph-theoretic techniques used in forensic accounting — tracing connectivity through the obfuscating intermediate layer to reveal the load-bearing paths hidden within the debris.

name: Soil Aggregate Hierarchy Theory of Corporate Structure
core_claim: Corporate organizational structure follows soil aggregate hierarchy dynamics: individual employees form microaggregates (teams) bound by interpersonal chemistry (organic binding agents), which nest into macroaggregates (departments) stabilized by institutional policies (fungal hyphae). Organizational resilience depends on aggregate stability — companies with strong micro-level team bonds but flexible macro-level department boundaries outperform those with rigid hierarchies, just as soils with stable aggregates resist erosion while maintaining porosity for resource flow.

name: Intertidal Zonation Theory of Corporate Hierarchy Stress Distribution
core_claim: Corporate hierarchies exhibit intertidal zonation: employees at different organizational levels occupy distinct stress-exposure bands analogous to splash, high-tide, mid-tide, and low-tide zones. Middle managers inhabit the mid-intertidal — the zone of maximum biodiversity but also maximum environmental variability, exposed to both executive pressure (wave energy) and subordinate demands (substrate competition). The organisms that thrive in each zone are precisely those whose stress-tolerance mechanisms match the zone's exposure pattern, explaining why lateral transfers across organizational levels fail more often than vertical promotions.

name: Sake Parallel Fermentation Theory of Bilingual Cognition
core_claim: Bilingual language processing operates like sake's parallel fermentation: in sake brewing, saccharification (koji converting starch to sugar) and alcohol fermentation (yeast converting sugar to ethanol) occur simultaneously in the same vessel, each process's output feeding the other. Bilingual speakers similarly run parallel 'fermentations' — phonological decoding and semantic integration in both languages simultaneously — where each language's partial outputs feed into the other's processing pipeline. This explains both the bilingual advantage (parallel processing produces higher 'alcohol content' — cognitive flexibility) and the bilingual cost (the process requires more careful temperature management — executive control — to prevent one fermentation from overwhelming the other).

name: Wine Volatile Acidity Oscillation Theory of Public Discourse Quality
core_claim: Public discourse quality oscillates like volatile acidity in wine: a small amount of acetic acid (provocative, contrarian speech) adds complexity and liveliness to the wine (discourse), but above the perception threshold it becomes vinegar (toxic discourse). The oscillation occurs because the bacteria producing volatile acidity (provocateurs, contrarians) thrive in the same conditions that produce the best wine (open, oxygenated discourse environments). Attempting to eliminate volatile acidity entirely (aggressive content moderation) requires sulfiting the wine (suppressing free expression) to the point where fermentation itself is inhibited. The winemaker's art — maintaining volatile acidity just below the perception threshold — is the content moderator's impossible challenge, made harder by the fact that different tasters (audiences) have different perception thresholds.

name: Fretting Fatigue Theory of Chronic Relationship Deterioration
core_claim: Long-term relationship deterioration follows fretting fatigue mechanics: two surfaces (partners) under constant contact pressure (cohabitation, shared responsibilities) with small oscillatory relative motion (daily micro-disagreements, minor annoyances) develop surface fatigue cracks far more rapidly than either cyclic loading alone (big fights) or static contact alone (stable coexistence) would predict. The fretting mechanism explains why relationships fail from accumulated micro-damage rather than single catastrophic events: the oscillatory motion prevents the protective oxide layer (polite social veneer) from re-forming between cycles, exposing bare metal (raw emotional responses) to corrosive environments (stress, fatigue). Relationship maintenance is the engineering challenge of reducing fretting amplitude without reducing contact pressure — staying close while minimizing abrasive oscillation.

name: Fermentation Oxygen Phase Transition Theory of Organizational Innovation Modes
core_claim: Organizations oscillate between two fundamentally different innovation modes that mirror fermentation's oxygen phase transition: aerobic mode (open innovation, external partnerships, idea exchange with the environment — producing rapid growth but no distinctive 'alcohol') and anaerobic mode (closed, internally focused development that is slower but produces the distinctive products — proprietary technology, unique culture — that differentiate the organization). The phase transition between modes is sharp, not gradual: partially aerobic fermentation doesn't produce half the alcohol; it produces off-flavors (confused strategy, half-executed pivots). Organizations must commit fully to one mode at a time and manage deliberate transitions between them, not attempt to run both simultaneously.

name: Dialectical Trinity: Substance Through Sublation
core_claim: The three divine persons of Trinitarian theology can be structurally re-derived as a necessary dialectical unfolding where the Father posits the Son as antithesis, and the Spirit emerges as the synthesis that preserves both distinction and unity, such that Trinitarian coherence is the only stable resolution of a self-relating absolute.

mapped_components.unit: Divine person as a moment of absolute self-relation that demands its own negation to become determinate
mapped_components.variation: The Son arises as antithesis of the Father by the logical necessity that unbounded self-identity must externalize itself to know itself
mapped_components.selection: The Holy Spirit as Aufhebung (sublation) — cancels opposition while preserving distinctness; modalism and tritheism are 'selected out' as incomplete syntheses
mapped_components.fitness: Whether the triad achieves fully self-mediated unity — internally differentiated yet non-composite
mapped_components.boundary: Creedal formulations function as the logical space constraining which dialectical resolutions count as orthodox

falsifiable_predictions: Any historically proposed Trinitarian heresy should classify as either premature synthesis (collapsing distinction) or stalled dialectic (refusing sublation) — a survey of the first seven councils should yield 100% classification rate.

Independent threefold ultimate-reality structures (Trimurti, Neoplatonic triad, Trikaya) should exhibit the same thesis/antithesis/synthesis grammar when formally analyzed — finding one whose internal relations are purely parallel (no generative ordering) falsifies the prediction.

name: Complexity-Bounded Cryptographic Hardness
core_claim: RSA security is proportional to the Kolmogorov complexity of the private key: algorithmically random primes resist factoring because no program shorter than the primes themselves can derive them, while low-complexity primes admit shorter attack programs exploiting structural regularity.

mapped_components.unit: RSA key pair treated as a binary string whose algorithmic complexity determines strength
mapped_components.variation: Space of all programs that could output the private key given the public key
mapped_components.selection: Shortest such program defines the effective attack complexity
mapped_components.fitness: Incompressibility of prime factors — random primes force brute search as only viable strategy
mapped_components.boundary: Prime generation method and key length set the complexity floor

falsifiable_predictions: Primes with detectable algorithmic structure are factorable faster than equally-long random primes.
Compressibility pre-test on primes correlates with empirical factoring time.
A Kolmogorov complexity threshold exists below which structured factoring succeeds in polynomial time.

name: Entropic Uncertainty Budgeting
core_claim: Measurement uncertainty components can be optimally combined by treating each as a symbol in an information-theoretic alphabet, weighting by informational surprise — the resulting combined uncertainty minimizes redundancy and approaches a theoretical lower bound analogous to the Shannon entropy bound in source coding.

mapped_components.unit: Single uncertainty component as a symbol emitted from a source
mapped_components.variation: Probability distributions assigned to each component (normal, rectangular, triangular)
mapped_components.selection: Entropy-weighted combination assigning greater weight to high-entropy components
mapped_components.fitness: Combined uncertainty approaches entropy-derived lower bound
mapped_components.boundary: Independent components and linear measurement model

falsifiable_predictions: For systems with many high-entropy type B components, entropic budgeting produces 5-15% larger uncertainties matching inter-lab spreads.
Ranking components by Shannon entropy predicts which yield greatest reduction when improved, outperforming sensitivity coefficients.
Methods converge when one low-entropy component dominates, diverge when multiple high-entropy components compete.

name: Heroic Clonal Selection
core_claim: Immune clonal selection follows monomyth narrative logic: naive cell (ordinary world) → antigen encounter (call) → germinal center (ordeal) → affinity maturation (transformation) → memory cell (return with boon). Clones that skip stages produce lower-quality immunity.

mapped_components.unit: B-cell clone as narrative protagonist traversing a developmental arc
mapped_components.variation: V(D)J recombination as ordinary-world endowment determining which call the clone answers
mapped_components.selection: Antigen binding as call to adventure; T-cell help as threshold guardian
mapped_components.fitness: Somatic hypermutation as ordeal; affinity maturation as boon; memory cell as integrated return
mapped_components.boundary: Self-tolerance as refusal-of-return constraint — autoreactive mutants must be eliminated

falsifiable_predictions: Full germinal-center transit produces higher-affinity, longer-lived memory than extrafollicular shortcuts.
Interrupting GC at different stages produces distinct failure modes mapping to monomyth stages.
Autoimmune disease correlates with 'return boundary' failures — fully matured clones escaping tolerance.

name: Inhibitory Field Settlement Theory
core_claim: New settlements nucleate at points of lowest cumulative inhibition from existing centers, producing golden-angle spiral patterns rather than hexagonal grids — yielding more efficient coverage than Christaller's static tiling when growth is sequential.

mapped_components.unit: Settlement as leaf primordium generating an inhibitory field suppressing nearby formation
mapped_components.variation: Inhibitory fields of different radii scaled by settlement order
mapped_components.selection: New settlements at lowest cumulative inhibition — where unmet demand is greatest
mapped_components.fitness: Minimized travel distance + maximized non-overlapping coverage
mapped_components.boundary: Requires sequential founding, continuous demand surface; linear infrastructure distorts fields

falsifiable_predictions: On uniform terrain, consecutive settlement angular separations cluster near 137.5° ± 15°.
Linear infrastructure collapses spiral patterns into bilateral symmetry.
Nearest-to-second-nearest neighbor distance ratio approximates golden ratio (1.618).

name: Chromatophoric Brand Display Theory
core_claim: Corporate rebranding operates as a chromatophore system: rapid, neurally-centralized modulation of surface-level identity elements that simultaneously serves multiple contradictory functions through patterns the organism itself cannot perceive from the outside. Like cuttlefish displaying patterns invisible to their own eyes, companies project brand identities they cannot objectively evaluate, making rebranding an act of blind signaling whose success depends on audience neurology, not sender intention.

name: Sonata Form Theory of Wound Healing Phases
core_claim: Wound healing follows sonata form development: the exposition presents two contrasting 'themes' — pro-inflammatory destruction and pro-regenerative growth — in separate phases. The development section (proliferative phase) fragments, modulates, and recombines these themes through complex cytokine interactions. The recapitulation (remodeling phase) restates both themes in the 'home key' of tissue homeostasis. Chronic wounds are analogous to a sonata stuck in its development section, endlessly modulating without achieving recapitulation, while keloid scarring represents a recapitulation that overshoots into a coda of excessive tissue production.

name: Circadian Oscillator Theory of Financial Markets
core_claim: Market sentiment follows a transcription-translation feedback loop structurally identical to circadian clock genes: optimism (the 'protein product') accumulates during trading hours, eventually triggering its own negative regulator (risk aversion), which suppresses further optimism until it degrades overnight. The resulting ~24-hour sentiment oscillation explains persistent intraday patterns in volatility and volume better than rational expectations models.

name: Tannin Polymerization Theory of Social Trust
core_claim: Social trust in institutions polymerizes like tannins in aging wine: individual trust interactions are monomeric phenolics that, over time and under the right conditions (consistent institutional behavior = cellar temperature), cross-link into stable polymeric chains. Young institutions have harsh, astringent trust profiles; mature ones develop smooth, integrated trust that precipitates out extremists as sediment. Institutional scandals act as oxidative shocks that cleave polymer chains back to bitter monomers.

name: Isochronism Theory of Democratic Institutions
core_claim: Stable democracies achieve institutional isochronism: their electoral and legislative cycles maintain constant period regardless of the amplitude of political disruption, just as a pendulum's period is independent of swing amplitude. Authoritarian drift occurs when the political escapement loses isochronism — crisis amplitudes begin to distort cycle timing (emergency powers extend terms, elections are delayed), breaking the constant-period guarantee that allows citizens to plan and hold leaders accountable.

name: Phonological Loop Resonance Theory of Pricing Psychology
core_claim: Price memorability and perceived fairness are determined by phonological loop resonance: prices whose verbal representation fits neatly into the phonological loop's ~2-second rehearsal window are perceived as more trustworthy and are recalled more accurately. Prices that require longer verbal encoding ($19.97 vs. $20) exploit a resonance mismatch — the complex phonological representation prevents the price from settling into a stable loop orbit, causing the consumer to approximate downward. This explains why charm pricing works in English but has variable effectiveness across languages with different number-word lengths.

name: Exposure Gradient Morphology Theory of Language Contact Change
core_claim: Languages in contact zones develop morphological features that vary with exposure gradient, just as intertidal organisms develop different body forms based on wave exposure. Languages in the high-exposure zone (trade pidgins, border dialects) develop streamlined, simplified morphology — reduced inflection, simplified phonology, analytic structure — analogous to the flat, encrusting forms of wave-battered organisms. Languages in sheltered zones (isolated mountain communities, island dialects) preserve elaborate morphology (complex case systems, tone, polysynthesis) the way sheltered-coast organisms develop tall, branching forms. The gradient is continuous, not binary.

name: Ferrofluid Spike Dynamics Theory of Social Media Viral Content
core_claim: Viral social media content follows ferrofluid spike dynamics: content (ferrofluid surface) in a weak ambient field (normal algorithmic amplification) remains flat, but localized field intensification (trending topic, influencer amplification) causes individual spikes to erupt. Each spike's height is proportional to local field strength, and spikes interact — tall spikes (viral posts) suppress nearby spike formation by concentrating the field lines (attention), creating a winner-take-most dynamic within any trend. The spikes are inherently unstable; removing the field (algorithmic de-amplification) causes immediate collapse, and the fluid returns to flat equilibrium with no permanent deformation (content is forgotten instantly).

name: Glassblowing Gather Viscosity Theory of Negotiation Timing
core_claim: Negotiation outcomes are determined by viscosity management: like a glassblower who must work the gather at precisely the right viscosity — too hot and it drips uncontrollably (too much flexibility leads to concession cascades), too cool and it cracks (rigidity produces deadlock) — negotiators must manage the 'temperature' of the deal. The critical insight from glassblowing is that viscosity is not uniform: skilled blowers create temperature gradients within the same gather, keeping some areas workable while others set. Master negotiators similarly maintain different 'viscosity zones' across issues, keeping some fluid while allowing others to solidify strategically.

name: Tidal Pool Refuge Theory of Startup Ecosystem Survival
core_claim: Startup ecosystems function as tidal pools: small, isolated bodies of high-salinity water (concentrated capital and talent) left behind when the economic tide retreats (market downturns). Species (startups) that survive in tidal pools are extremophiles adapted to rapid environmental fluctuation — they tolerate both desiccation (funding droughts) and submersion (boom-time capital floods). The tidal pool's key ecological feature is that its isolation creates unique selection pressures that produce species found nowhere else, explaining why startup innovation clusters (Silicon Valley, Shenzhen, Bangalore) generate ideas that large-ocean (corporate) environments cannot. But tidal pools are also fragile: a single rogue wave (regulatory disruption) can flush the entire community.

name: Bonsai Root Pruning Theory of Database Index Optimization
core_claim: Database index optimization should follow bonsai root pruning principles: periodically cutting back the root system (index structure) forces the tree (query engine) to develop a dense, compact root ball (highly efficient index) rather than sprawling tap roots (over-specialized indices that serve single query patterns at the expense of overall performance). The bonsai master's counterintuitive insight — that restricting the root system strengthens the tree above ground — translates to the database principle that constraining index proliferation forces the query optimizer to develop more creative execution plans. Root-pruned databases, like root-pruned bonsai, are smaller but more resilient to transplantation (migration) and environmental change (schema evolution).

name: Entanglement Distribution Theory of Diplomatic Alliances
core_claim: International alliances function as entanglement distribution networks where trust (the diplomatic analog of quantum entanglement) degrades with geopolitical distance and is subject to a no-cloning constraint: a nation cannot simultaneously maintain equally strong bilateral trust with two mutually hostile powers. Alliance maintenance requires continuous 'entanglement distillation' through summits, joint exercises, and treaty renewals, and the effective alliance radius is bounded by the rate at which trust decoheres through competing interests.

name: Formulaic Speech Preservation Theory of Constitutional Interpretation
core_claim: Constitutional phrases survive centuries of interpretive drift because they function as oral-formulaic speech: 'due process,' 'equal protection,' and 'cruel and unusual' are formulaic epithets whose meaning is preserved not by semantic precision but by their rhythmic and syntactic position within the constitutional 'song.' Originalist and living-constitution debates are the legal equivalent of the Homeric Question — whether the formulae carry fixed meaning from their composition or accumulate new meaning with each performance (judicial application).

name: Ferrofluid Chain Formation Theory of Political Alliance Polarization
core_claim: Political polarization follows ferrofluid chain formation: individual political actors (magnetic nanoparticles) in a weak field (normal politics) exhibit Brownian randomness (independent positions), but as the external field strengthens (crisis, media polarization), particles align into chains along field lines. Once chains form, they self-reinforce — each particle's field adds to its neighbors', making the chain structure resistant to thermal disruption (moderate voices). The chains are one-dimensional and fragile perpendicular to the field axis, explaining why polarized systems are strong along the partisan dimension but brittle to orthogonal shocks.

name: Steganographic Cover Object Selection Theory of Resume Writing
core_claim: Effective resume writing is cover-object selection for steganographic embedding: the resume's explicit content (cover object) must be a natural, unsuspicious document that simultaneously carries a hidden payload (the candidate's unique value proposition) detectable only by the intended recipient (hiring manager with domain expertise). Resumes that make the payload too visible (aggressive self-promotion) fail the steganalytic test (recruiter pattern-matching rejects them as unnatural); resumes with too little payload (generic descriptions) pass as natural but carry no useful hidden signal. The optimal embedding rate depends on the cover object's statistical properties — highly standardized formats (accounting resumes) tolerate less embedding than variable formats (creative roles).

name: Mercerization Theory of Cultural Transformation
core_claim: Rapid cultural transformation follows mercerization dynamics: immersion in a strong ideological 'solution' (revolution, religious conversion, colonization) causes differential swelling and crystalline rearrangement of social structures. Communities that fully convert from their original 'cellulose I' cultural arrangement to 'cellulose II' gain new properties (cohesion, identity clarity, external signaling power) but lose their original texture irreversibly. Partially converted communities exhibit the weakest structural properties of either arrangement.

name: Magma Chamber Recharge Theory of Creative Burnout
core_claim: Creative burnout and recovery follow magma chamber recharge dynamics: creative output (eruption) depletes the chamber of differentiated ideas (evolved magma), and recovery requires slow recharge from deep mantle sources (subconscious processing, diverse experience intake). Premature creative demands on a partially recharged chamber produce weak, undifferentiated output — the creative equivalent of basaltic rather than rhyolitic eruption. The recharge rate is governed by the 'thermal flux' of new experiences and the 'crustal thickness' of institutional demands that impede deep-source access.

name: Alarm Fatigue Theory of Prophetic Desensitization
core_claim: Religious communities experience alarm fatigue toward prophetic warnings: when prophetic voices (alarm systems) generate too many urgent signals without corresponding crises (false alarms), the community habituates and begins ignoring all prophetic input. The alarm fatigue curve from ergonomics — where response probability drops exponentially with alarm frequency — maps precisely onto the historical observation that prophetic traditions lose efficacy in proportion to how frequently they predict imminent divine intervention. Effective prophetic traditions self-regulate alarm frequency to maintain response sensitivity.

name: Pipe Network Pressure Balancing Theory of Information Ecosystem Health
core_claim: Healthy information ecosystems maintain pressure balance across their distribution network, following the same Hardy Cross method used for pipe networks: each information channel (pipe) carries a flow of content, and the system reaches equilibrium when pressure (credibility) is balanced at every junction (platform, aggregator). Misinformation propagates when a channel develops a pressure imbalance — either excess pressure (viral amplification without verification) or vacuum (censorship creating information voids that suck in conspiracy content). Rebalancing requires adjusting flow resistance (moderation) at specific pipes, not at nodes.

name: Stalactite Growth Theory of Compound Interest Accumulation
core_claim: Compound interest accumulation follows stalactite growth dynamics: each supersaturated drip (interest payment) deposits a thin mineral layer (reinvested return) whose thickness depends on evaporation rate (spending rate), drip frequency (compounding period), and mineral concentration (interest rate). The resulting formation grows exponentially in mass but its shape is determined by local airflow (market conditions) — stalactites in still air (stable markets) form regular cones, while those in drafts (volatile markets) develop asymmetric, unpredictable forms. Cave formations that took millennia to build can be broken in seconds, mirroring the asymmetry between wealth accumulation and wealth destruction.

name: Sourdough pH Regulation Theory of Democratic Deliberation Quality
core_claim: Healthy democratic deliberation requires sourdough pH regulation: the acidity (rhetorical intensity) of public discourse must stay within a narrow band — too alkaline (overly civil, euphemistic) and pathogenic ideas (bad-faith arguments) proliferate unchecked, too acidic (hostile, combative) and the productive organisms (good-faith participants) die off. The lactic acid bacteria (institutional norms, moderation) regulate pH through their own metabolic output, creating a self-stabilizing system. The key insight is that some acidity is essential — completely pH-neutral discourse is as lifeless as sterilized flour paste.

name: Sandcastle Drip Technique Theory of Agile Software Development
core_claim: Agile development follows sandcastle drip technique: rather than building from pre-formed blocks (waterfall specifications), the developer drips small amounts of wet sand (minimal viable increments) that stack into organic, load-bearing structures whose final form emerges from the process rather than preceding it. Drip-built structures are remarkably strong relative to their material because each drip bonds chemically to the one below before the next arrives (continuous integration). The technique's limitation is that drip-built structures cannot produce flat surfaces or right angles (clean architectural boundaries) — they inherently produce organic, irregular forms that may not match the client's rectangular expectations.

name: Puppet Illusion Threshold Theory of AI Anthropomorphism
core_claim: AI anthropomorphism operates at a puppet illusion threshold: puppeteers know that audience belief in the puppet as a living entity requires crossing a perceptual threshold — below it, the puppet is obviously an object; above it, audiences genuinely forget the puppeteer exists. This threshold depends not on realism but on consistency of behavioral 'breath' — the micro-movements (idle animations, response timing, hesitation patterns) that signal life. AI systems cross the anthropomorphism threshold through the same mechanism: not photorealistic avatars or perfect grammar, but consistent micro-behavioral patterns that trigger the audience's life-detection circuitry. The puppet master's warning applies: once the threshold is crossed, the audience's emotional responses are as real as those directed at living beings, creating ethical obligations independent of the puppet's ontological status.

name: Innate Diffraction Grammar
core_claim: The universal structural patterns in human language arise from interference effects — phonological and syntactic primitives combine like wave fronts passing through a lattice, producing constructive interference at grammatical positions and destructive interference at ungrammatical ones, with Bragg-like selection rules determining which combinations amplify.

name: Cultural Plume Theory
core_claim: Deep structural conditions form fixed innovation plumes that persist in geographic locations. As the broader socioeconomic plate drifts over these plumes, each location experiences a temporary eruption of creative output, then goes dormant, leaving chronologically ordered chains of formerly innovative cities.

name: Morphogen Gradient Theory of Wealth Distribution
core_claim: Income distribution in market economies follows morphogen gradient dynamics: economic policy centers (central banks, fiscal authorities) act as morphogen sources, and the concentration of economic stimulus diffuses outward through institutional layers. Cells (firms and households) at different distances from the source interpret threshold concentrations as fate instructions, differentiating into distinct economic roles. Wealth inequality is thus a positional-information problem, not merely a market-competition outcome.

name: Fourier Decomposition Theory of Personality
core_claim: Personality is not a fixed set of trait dimensions but a superposition of behavioral harmonics: each situation activates a different frequency component of the person's behavioral repertoire, and the observed personality at any moment is the Fourier sum of all active components. The Big Five factors correspond to the lowest-frequency (most persistent) harmonics, while situational variability reflects higher-frequency components that average out over time. Personality disorders represent missing or overamplified harmonics.

name: Adhesive Wear Theory of Democratic Norm Erosion
core_claim: Democratic norms erode through Archard's adhesive wear law: the volume of institutional material removed is proportional to the sliding distance (duration of political contact), the normal load (intensity of partisan pressure), and inversely proportional to the hardness of the institution. Each norm violation transfers a small particle of institutional legitimacy from the public body to the violating actor. The wear coefficient K — determined by institutional design — predicts whether a democracy experiences mild polishing (healthy adversarialism) or severe galling (institutional seizure).

name: Canopy Microclimate Regulation Theory of Classroom Management
core_claim: Effective classroom management creates a canopy microclimate: the teacher's instructional 'canopy' (lesson structure, behavioral expectations) regulates the thermal environment (emotional temperature), light penetration (individual attention), and humidity (social interaction density) that students experience. Over-dense canopy (authoritarian control) produces etiolated learners who stretch toward any available light; over-sparse canopy (permissive environments) exposes students to scorching direct sun (peer pressure, distraction) without protective filtering. Optimal leaf area index for learning varies by developmental stage.

name: Semaphore Error Cascade Theory of Misinformation Propagation
core_claim: Misinformation propagates through semaphore error cascades: each relay station (social media reshare) introduces a small angular error in the signal, and these errors compound geometrically across the relay chain. By the fifth relay, the original semaphore position (factual claim) may have drifted into an entirely different letter (meaning). The error rate is highest for signals near the boundary between two valid positions (ambiguous claims), and fog (information overload) increases error at every station simultaneously. The historical solution — requiring acknowledgment signals before proceeding — maps onto fact-checking friction in content sharing.

name: Sandcastle Wave Erosion Theory of Brand Equity Degradation
core_claim: Brand equity degrades through sandcastle wave-erosion mechanics: each competitive wave (market disruption, PR crisis, competitor campaign) removes a layer of carefully constructed brand structure. The castle's survival depends not on absolute height (brand value) but on the angle of the seaward face (brand resilience architecture) and the cohesion of the building material (customer loyalty moisture content). Brands built above the high-tide line (premium positioning) experience less frequent erosion but face catastrophic loss when unusually large waves (industry disruption) reach them, while brands in the intertidal zone (mass market) experience constant erosion but develop adaptive morphologies.

name: Ferrofluid Labyrinthine Pattern Theory of Urban Street Network Evolution
core_claim: Organic urban street networks evolve through ferrofluid labyrinthine pattern formation: competing magnetic domains (property boundaries, land uses) produce a maze-like pattern whose geometry emerges from local energy minimization rather than global planning. The labyrinthine pattern's characteristic wavelength (typical block size) is set by the competition between surface tension (construction cost of street frontage) and magnetic energy (land value gradient). Planned grid cities suppress the labyrinthine instability through overwhelming external order, but wherever planning control weakens (informal settlements, historical cores), the labyrinthine pattern re-emerges with predictable wavelength.

name: Glacial Surge Oscillation Theory of Creative Productivity
core_claim: Creative productivity follows glacial surge oscillation: glaciers alternate between long quiescent phases (slow, steady movement with internal pressure building) and sudden surges (rapid advance where decades of accumulated basal water pressure releases in months). Creative workers similarly alternate between extended 'quiescent' periods (reading, note-taking, failed drafts — internal pressure accumulating) and surge periods (sudden prolific output where the accumulated material releases rapidly). The surge is not triggered by willpower but by reaching a critical basal water pressure threshold — a quantity of internalized material that lubricates the creative bed sufficiently for movement. Attempting to force a surge before the threshold is reached produces crevassing (burnout), not advance.

name: Combustion Propagation Front Theory of Revolutionary Movements
core_claim: Revolutionary movements spread as combustion propagation fronts: the revolution 'ignites' at a point where fuel (popular grievance), oxidizer (organizational infrastructure), and heat (triggering event) converge, then propagates outward as a self-sustaining combustion front. The front's velocity depends on the fuel-oxidizer ratio ahead of it (the balance of grievance and organizational capacity in not-yet-revolutionary regions), not on conditions behind it (post-revolutionary governance quality). This explains why revolutions spread rapidly through regions with similar fuel-oxidizer ratios regardless of the first revolution's success or failure — the front is self-sustaining once initiated. Counter-revolutionary strategy should focus on creating firebreaks (institutional reforms that reduce fuel density) rather than extinguishing the front directly (suppression), because suppression merely delays propagation while the fuel continues accumulating.

name: Zero-Knowledge Proof Theory of Therapeutic Disclosure
core_claim: Effective psychotherapy operates through a zero-knowledge proof protocol: the client must convince the therapist of their internal state (the 'witness') without revealing the traumatic content directly, using indirect challenges (metaphors, somatic responses, behavioral patterns) that allow the therapist to verify the emotional truth without possessing the specific memories. This explains why narrative exposure therapy sometimes re-traumatizes: it violates the zero-knowledge property by forcing full witness disclosure.

name: Rosensweig Instability Theory of Crowd Panic Formation
core_claim: Crowd panic events follow Rosensweig instability dynamics: a crowd under stress (ferrofluid in a magnetic field) remains flat and orderly until the applied stress (field strength) exceeds a critical threshold, at which point the surface spontaneously erupts into spike-like formations — surges, crushes, and stampede fronts. The wavelength of the instability (spacing between crush points) is predictable from crowd density (fluid depth) and stress intensity (field strength), offering a quantitative tool for venue safety design that goes beyond simple density thresholds.

name: Ferrofluid Viscosity Tuning Theory of Monetary Policy Transmission
core_claim: Central banks attempt monetary policy through ferrofluid viscosity tuning: by adjusting the external field strength (policy rate), they change the apparent viscosity (financial conditions) of the economic fluid. In low fields (accommodative policy), the fluid flows freely (easy credit); in high fields (restrictive policy), aligned particle chains increase viscosity (tighter lending standards). The non-Newtonian behavior of ferrofluids under shear (market stress) explains why monetary policy transmission breaks down during crises — under high shear rates, the chains fragment and the fluid's viscosity drops unpredictably, making precise control impossible precisely when it matters most.

name: Glass Cane Pull Theory of Mentorship Knowledge Transfer
core_claim: Mentorship knowledge transfer follows glass cane-pull dynamics: two glassblowers (mentor and mentee) simultaneously pull a molten gather (shared knowledge) in opposite directions, stretching it into an increasingly thin cane (refined, transmissible understanding) that preserves the cross-sectional pattern (core insights) at any scale. The pull must be symmetrical — if the mentor pulls faster than the mentee can keep up, the cane necks down and snaps (knowledge transfer breaks); if the mentee pulls without the mentor's counterpull, the gather simply moves laterally (the mentee absorbs the mentor's position but not their knowledge structure). The pattern visible in the finished cane is the proof that genuine bilateral transfer occurred.

name: Rennet Coagulation Theory of Market Bubble Solidification
core_claim: Market bubbles solidify through rennet coagulation dynamics: rennet (a catalytic narrative — 'this time is different') cleaves the protective casein micelle structure (rational price discovery) of the market 'milk,' causing dispersed protein (capital) to aggregate into a gel (bubble). The coagulation is initially invisible — the milk looks unchanged until a critical fraction of casein is cleaved, then suddenly solidifies. This explains the paradox of bubbles: the transition from liquid market to solid bubble is a phase transition with a sharp threshold, not a gradual process, and the catalyst (narrative) need only be present in trace amounts. The firmness of the resulting curd (bubble rigidity) depends on temperature (market enthusiasm) and rennet concentration (narrative virality).

name: Phyllotaxis Theory of Urban Zoning
core_claim: Optimal urban land-use zoning follows phyllotactic packing principles: each new development creates an inhibitory field (noise, traffic, shade) that repels similar developments, and the next project of the same type is placed at the point of least inhibition. When market forces are allowed to operate freely under basic constraints, the resulting spatial distribution of commercial, residential, and industrial zones converges on golden-angle spacing that maximizes resource access and minimizes inter-zone conflict.

name: Glacial Surge Theory of Technological Innovation
core_claim: Technological innovation surges follow glacial surge dynamics: knowledge accumulates slowly at the base (basic research = basal meltwater), institutional friction holds back deployment (ice-bed coupling), until lubrication pressure exceeds a threshold and the entire technology front surges forward in a rapid advance. Post-surge, the knowledge base is thinned and depleted, entering a quiescent phase of slow re-accumulation. The AI boom, dot-com bubble, and Industrial Revolution all exhibit surge-quiescence periodicity consistent with glacial oscillation models.

name: Ensemble Unison-Canon Theory of Legislative Voting
core_claim: Legislative voting patterns oscillate between unison and canon choreographic modes: party-line votes are unison (all dancers executing the same movement simultaneously), while bipartisan coalition-building follows canon structure (the same voting pattern initiated by a leader and replicated by followers with a time delay). The phase offset in canon voting — how long it takes for each legislator to adopt the leader's position — reveals the true influence network of the legislature more accurately than formal party membership.

name: Sake Parallel Fermentation Theory of Bilingual Language Acquisition
core_claim: Bilingual language acquisition operates through sake's parallel fermentation mechanism: unlike sequential fermentation (wine), where sugar conversion and alcohol production happen in series, sake brewing runs saccharification and fermentation simultaneously in the same vessel. Bilingual children similarly run two language systems in parallel — converting environmental input (rice starch) into linguistic structure (sugar) and communicative competence (alcohol) for both languages concurrently. This parallel process achieves higher 'alcohol content' (fluency) than sequential approaches because each system's metabolic byproducts feed the other.

name: Parkour Momentum Conservation Theory of Negotiation Dynamics
core_claim: Skilled negotiators conserve momentum like parkour practitioners: each concession or demand is a vault or jump that must convert the momentum of the previous move into the setup for the next. Stopping mid-run (breaking the negotiation rhythm) forces a costly restart from standing, while maintaining flow allows the negotiator to clear obstacles (objections) that would be impassable from a static position. The parkour insight that 'the landing IS the takeoff' maps onto the negotiation principle that how you frame a concession determines the energy available for your next demand.

name: Sake Yeast Strain Selection Theory of Startup Team Composition
core_claim: Startup success depends on yeast strain selection with sake-brewing precision: different yeast strains (team archetypes) produce different flavor profiles (company cultures) and alcohol tolerances (scaling capacities). Association No. 7 yeast (reliable generalists) produces consistent, marketable sake but never transcendent quality; No. 9 (creative risk-takers) produces aromatic excellence but is temperamental and fermentation-failure-prone. The brewer's deepest expertise is matching strain to rice and water — the founder's deepest expertise should be matching team archetype to market and technology. Using a ginjo yeast (artist team) for a table-sake product (commodity market) wastes its best qualities.

name: Puppet Counterweight Balance Theory of Legislative Bicameralism
core_claim: Bicameral legislatures operate through puppet counterweight balance: each chamber is a counterweight that stabilizes the other's movement, preventing the wild swings (radical legislation) that a single-string (unicameral) system produces. The upper chamber (Senate/Lords) provides gravitational drag on the lower chamber's quick, responsive movements, producing smoother, more deliberate legislative 'gestures.' The system fails when the counterweight becomes too heavy (obstructionist upper chamber) — the puppet cannot move at all — or too light (rubber-stamp chamber) — the puppet jerks uncontrollably. The puppeteer's art of adjusting counterweight mass maps onto constitutional reform of chamber powers.

name: Parkour Route Selection Theory of Surgical Decision-Making
core_claim: Surgical decision-making in complex cases follows parkour route selection: the traceur (surgeon) must read the environment (anatomy) in real-time and select a movement path (surgical approach) that balances efficiency (minimal tissue disruption), safety (avoiding critical structures), and flow (maintaining operative momentum). The parkour concept of 'reading' the environment — perceiving affordances that untrained eyes miss (a wall ledge becomes a launch point; a tissue plane becomes a dissection corridor) — is the perceptual skill that distinguishes expert from novice surgeons. Training should emphasize environmental reading and adaptive route-finding, not memorization of fixed technique sequences.

name: Moraine Formation Theory of Cultural Heritage Preservation
core_claim: Cultural heritage accumulates as moraine deposits: glacial advance (dominant cultural movements — empires, religions, ideologies) scrapes up and transports enormous quantities of debris (artifacts, practices, languages, beliefs) from the terrain it overrides, depositing this material as moraines (heritage sites, diaspora communities, syncretic traditions) when the glacier retreats. Terminal moraines (the heritage deposited at the glacier's maximum extent — colonial-era institutions in former colonies) mark the furthest reach of cultural influence and often become the most culturally productive zones precisely because they contain material from every terrain the glacier traversed. Lateral moraines (heritage at the glacier's edges — border cultures, frontier communities) are narrower but equally diagnostic of the glacier's path.

name: Contact Improvisation Weight-Sharing Theory of Democratic Deliberation
core_claim: Democratic deliberation works best when modeled on contact improvisation: two dancers (deliberating parties) share weight (argumentative burden) through a constantly negotiated point of contact, and the quality of the dance depends on each party's willingness to both give AND receive weight simultaneously. Pure weight-giving (one-sided argumentation) causes the receiving partner to collapse; pure weight-receiving (pure listening without counter-argument) provides no resistance for the giving partner to work against. The contact improvisation principle that the most creative movement emerges at the point of mutual weight-sharing — where neither party is fully supporting nor fully supported — maps onto the deliberative sweet spot where genuine perspective-shifting occurs. Debate formats that enforce alternating monologues (you speak, then I speak) miss this dynamic entirely.

name: Grain Boundary Strengthening Theory of Coalition Government Stability
core_claim: Coalition government stability follows the Hall-Petch relationship: just as smaller grain size in metals produces stronger material (because grain boundaries impede dislocation movement), coalition governments with more, smaller parties (finer political 'grain') can be more stable than two-party systems (large-grain) because the numerous inter-party boundaries (coalition agreements, cross-cutting alliances) impede the 'dislocations' (defections, policy reversals) that cause political failure. The Hall-Petch relationship predicts a square-root dependence: stability scales with the inverse square root of party size, not linearly. Below a minimum grain size (too many tiny parties), the inverse Hall-Petch effect takes over — the grain boundaries themselves become the dominant failure mechanism (coalition coordination costs exceed the stability benefits), predicting an optimal party count for maximum governmental stability.

name: Coriolis Effect Theory of Organizational Spin
core_claim: Large organizations deflect initiatives from their intended trajectories through a Coriolis-like effect: the 'rotation' of bureaucratic hierarchy means that a project launched straight toward a goal from any non-central position curves systematically — rightward in corporate cultures (toward revenue) and leftward in governmental ones (toward compliance). The magnitude of deflection increases with organizational latitude (distance from the power center) and initiative velocity (speed of implementation). Only projects launched from the organizational equator (C-suite) travel in straight lines.

name: Tonal Drift Theory of Monetary Policy Expectations
core_claim: Central bank communication shifts market expectations through tonal drift: just as tone systems in language drift gradually when speaker communities become isolated (each community's pitch targets shift without awareness), central bank forward guidance drifts in interpretation across different market participant communities. Bond traders, equity analysts, and retail investors each develop slightly different 'pitch targets' for the same policy language, and these drifts compound until a single Fed statement is effectively a different phonological system for each community, producing the divergent market reactions that puzzle monetary economists.

name: Bonsai Root Pruning Theory of Institutional Reform
core_claim: Effective institutional reform follows bonsai root-pruning logic: periodic, deliberate cutting of the root system (bureaucratic infrastructure) forces the institution to develop a denser network of fine feeder roots (responsive local processes) rather than relying on a few thick taproots (centralized systems). The critical constraint is that root pruning must be proportional to canopy reduction (mandate trimming) — pruning roots without reducing the canopy kills the tree, just as cutting bureaucracy without reducing institutional scope causes collapse. The bonsai master's seasonal timing maps onto electoral and budget cycles.

name: Affinage Rind Coevolution Theory of Student-Teacher Relationship Development
core_claim: The student-teacher relationship develops through affinage rind coevolution: the teacher's interventions (washing, turning, environmental control) and the student's autonomous development (internal biochemistry) co-produce a 'rind' (shared pedagogical culture) that is neither fully the teacher's creation nor the student's. Different affinage styles produce different rinds — the cave-aged natural rind of Montessori education differs fundamentally from the washed-rind of direct instruction, and each produces a distinct interior (learning outcome). The cheese cannot be separated from its rind; the student cannot be separated from the pedagogical relationship that shaped them.

name: Curd Moisture Partitioning Theory of Information Classification Security
core_claim: Information security classification follows curd moisture partitioning: the cheesemaker controls final texture (security outcome) primarily through how much whey (unclassified context) remains in the curd (classified document). High-moisture cheeses (documents with extensive unclassified context surrounding classified facts) spoil rapidly (leak easily through contextual inference), while low-moisture cheeses (tightly compartmented information) last longer but are harder to use (require extensive processing before consumption). The classification decision is fundamentally a moisture-partitioning decision: how much context to drain off, and at what stage of the process to cut the curd.

name: Cave Passage Morphology Theory of Organizational Communication Channels
core_claim: Organizational communication channels develop cave passage morphology: initial communication pathways form as narrow phreatic tubes (fully saturated, pressure-driven channels like formal email chains), but as organizational 'water table' drops (formality decreases), passages transition to vadose canyons (gravity-driven, open-channel flows like Slack conversations) that cut downward along the path of least resistance. Mature organizational communication systems exhibit the full spectrum of passage types, and the most critical pathways are often the 'fossil passages' — channels that no longer carry active flow but whose historical routing determined the geometry of all subsequent development. Understanding why communication follows certain paths requires speleological survey, not organizational chart analysis.

name: Speleogenesis Theory of Organizational Knowledge Formation
core_claim: Organizational knowledge forms through speleogenesis: the slow dissolution of rock (established assumptions) by slightly acidic water (novel information trickling through the organization) carves out cavern systems (knowledge structures) whose geometry is determined not by the water's intent but by the rock's pre-existing fractures and solubility. Knowledge management initiatives that try to 'drill' knowledge tunnels directly (top-down KM systems) produce unstable bores, while the natural speleogenetic process — slow, following existing weakness planes, creating self-supporting arched passages — produces knowledge structures that persist for millennia.

name: Bonsai Branch Wiring Theory of Neural Network Architecture Search
core_claim: Neural architecture search should follow bonsai branch wiring principles: the bonsai master wraps wire around branches to guide their growth direction over months, then removes the wire once the branch has 'set' in the desired position. Similarly, architectural constraints (skip connections, attention heads, layer widths) should be applied as temporary 'wire' during the early training phases to guide the network's structural development, then selectively removed to see which structural changes have become self-sustaining and which spring back. Branches that hold their shape after wire removal (architectural features that the network maintains without constraint) are genuine structural discoveries; those that spring back (features that disappear when unconstrained) were artifacts of the search process, not properties of the problem.

name: Aerial Shell Burst Timing Theory of Comedic Timing
core_claim: Comedic timing follows aerial shell burst timing mechanics: the shell (setup) is launched with a lift charge (premise establishment) and must detonate (punchline) at exactly the apogee of its trajectory — too early and the burst occurs too close to the audience (punchline arrives before the setup has fully expanded in the listener's mind), too late and the shell falls past apogee (the audience's attention begins declining before the payoff). Multi-break shells (jokes with multiple punchlines — callbacks, tags, toppers) require precisely sequenced time-delay fuses so each successive burst occurs at a higher altitude than the last, and the choreography of multiple shells in a show (a comedy set's joke sequence) must balance spectral variety (joke types), spatial distribution (topic range), and temporal rhythm (density and pacing).

name: Bragg Diffraction Theory of Electoral Periodicity
core_claim: Voter behavior in periodic elections exhibits constructive and destructive interference patterns analogous to Bragg diffraction. When the 'wavelength' of policy-cycle expectations aligns with the 'lattice spacing' of election intervals, voter mobilization constructively interferes to produce landslide outcomes. Misalignment between policy-expectation cycles and electoral timing produces diffuse, low-turnout elections where no clear mandate crystallizes.

name: Aerial Shell Burst Theory of Market Crashes
core_claim: Financial market crashes follow the physics of pyrotechnic aerial shells: the 'lift charge' of speculative leverage propels asset prices upward, while a timed 'burst charge' of margin calls and forced liquidation detonates at apogee, scattering 'stars' (selling pressure) in a radial pattern across correlated asset classes. The time-fuse delay between peak euphoria and crash onset is determined by the same pressure-containment dynamics that govern shell burst timing — the casing (market structure) must fail before energy release occurs.

name: Phoneme Neutralization Theory of Brand Differentiation Collapse
core_claim: Competing brands in mature markets undergo phoneme neutralization: distinctive brand identities (phonemic contrasts) merge into a single undifferentiated perception in the consumer's mind, just as phonemic contrasts neutralize in unstressed positions in language. Market saturation acts as the unstressed environment — when consumers pay less attention (reduced cognitive stress on the 'syllable'), brand distinctions that were once salient collapse into a single category. Brands must occupy 'stressed positions' (high-salience moments) to maintain differentiation.

name: Parkour Route Selection Theory of Judicial Precedent Navigation
core_claim: Judges navigate precedent landscapes using parkour route-selection logic: the optimal legal path between current case and desired outcome is not the straight-line doctrinal route but the one that chains together available 'surfaces' (prior rulings) based on their grip quality (binding authority), gap distance (factual similarity), and landing reliability (appellate survival rate). Landmark cases function as parkour 'vaults' — high-commitment moves that clear large doctrinal gaps but risk catastrophic failure if the landing surface is misjudged.

name: Taxidermy Habitat Diorama Theory of UX Contextual Design
core_claim: User experience design is taxidermic habitat diorama construction: the product (mounted specimen) must be placed within a convincing environmental context (diorama) that tells a story about the specimen's natural behavior (intended use case). A technically perfect mount in a poorly constructed diorama (beautiful interface in a confusing information architecture) fails to convince — users sense the artificiality even if they cannot articulate why. The diorama artist's skill in creating seamless transitions between foreground specimens and painted backgrounds maps onto the UX challenge of integrating interactive elements with static content so users cannot perceive the seam.

name: Bonsai Seasonal Timing Theory of Product Launch Windows
core_claim: Product launches must follow bonsai seasonal timing: each intervention (pruning = feature cuts, wiring = market positioning, repotting = platform migration, fertilizing = marketing spend) has a specific seasonal window when the tree can tolerate the stress. Spring (market expansion periods) is for repotting and heavy pruning; summer (growth phases) for wiring and pinching; fall (maturation) for refinement; winter (dormancy) for structural evaluation. Launching a major feature cut (heavy pruning) during the tree's active growth season (market boom) wastes the growth energy the tree had allocated to the removed branches, producing die-back (customer churn) rather than redirection.

name: Sake Water Mineral Profile Theory of Organizational Communication Tone
core_claim: Organizational communication derives its character from the 'water' (communication medium and norms) that constitutes 80% of every message, just as sake's character is determined primarily by water mineral profile rather than rice variety.
